title: [perf] Faster bit-identical Gram microkernel for DataFrame.corr/cov (~2 GFLOP/s -> target 2-4x)
description: PROFILE (perf_profile, this session): df_corr 964 ms/iter, df_cov 974 ms/iter at n=1,000,000 x 64 cols (build_corr_frame, NaN-free => hits the complete blocked-Gram path at fp-frame pairwise_stat_matrix ~36447). That is ~2.05e9 MACs in ~964ms = ~2 GFLOP/s — well below a tuned single-thread Gram (20-40 GFLOP/s). NOT fanout-confounded; this is a genuine numerical-core gap (our own safe-Rust Gram XtX kernel, NO-GAPS-aligned). LEVER (one, bit-identical): the current 4x4 register-blocked kernel packs only the B column-block into a row-major panel and reads the A side as col_data[ti+di][r] = 4 separate Vec<Vec<f64>> streams per r (load-port/cache pressure, weak autovectorization). Repack the WHOLE extracted matrix ROW-MAJOR once (X[r*n+c], O(n*len) one-time transpose), so each r-step reads two CONTIGUOUS BT-wide panels X[r*n+ti..][..BT] (A) and X[r*n+tj..][..BT] (B); widen the tile (try 6x8 / 8x8) to raise the MAC:load ratio; keep BT independent accumulators each folding r=0..len ascending with SEPARATE mul + add (NO fma/mul_add — fusion changes rounding and breaks the golden). ISOMORPHISM: each output cell acc[di][dj] must remain Sum_r A[ti+di][r]*B[tj+dj][r] folded r ascending with separate * then +=, identical to today and to finalize_pairwise_stat's sum_xy contract; the symmetry-swap finalize (i,j)/(j,i) is unchanged. Golden: perf_profile golden df_corr 5000 + golden df_cov 5000 sha256 must be byte-identical before/after. CONSTRAINT: FMA-free + fixed fold order caps the ceiling (numpy/MKL use FMA+reassociation we can't match bit-for-bit), so target ~2-4x (memcpy/contiguous-load + wider tile), not 20x. Re-bench same-command A/B df_corr/df_cov 1M x64, min-of-5, Score>=2.0. In fp-frame (cc lane). ALSO FILED-ADJACENT (separate, do not bundle): outer_join 728 ms/iter, left_join 376 ms/iter at n=500k (perf_profile sweep) — dominated by ~244M-row key fanout (cardinality ~512) + null-introducing unmatched-row materialization; large-output, lower priority than this clean Gram gap.
notes: 7TH LEVER (BlackThrush) — COMMUNICATION-AVOIDING RANK-1 REFORMULATION — bit-identical, REJECTED, but DIAGNOSTICALLY DECISIVE. Replaced the r-inner blocked tile (re-streams A columns ~N/BT times => ~5GB traffic) with a rank-1 outer-product accumulation: pack a 512-row X panel col->row-major (L2-resident), then per row G[i][j]+=X[r][i]*X[r][j] for i<=j into an L1-resident NxN accumulator. Reads X EXACTLY ONCE (~512MB, ~10x less traffic). Bit-identical: same operands, same r-ascending fold order (X[r][i]*X[r][j]==old col_data[i][r]*col_data[j][r]); df_corr/df_cov goldens 6f577ab8/beb4e3f5 UNCHANGED. RESULT: raw compute NEUTRAL-TO-SLIGHTLY-SLOWER (after 1402 vs baseline 1353 ms/iter, min-of-3, frame-build amortized at iters=6, 1M x64). The ~10x traffic reduction gave ZERO raw speedup => THE GRAM IS COMPUTE/FP-LATENCY-BOUND, NOT BANDWIDTH-BOUND. (~3 GFLOP/s == the FMA-free ILP ceiling for fixed-fold-order separate-mul-add.) This CLOSES the 'is it BW-bound?' question: it is NOT. The prior '64-core parallel neutral' was the same compute-wall, not a BW wall. CONFIRMED: the ONLY remaining >=2x lever is FMA (explicit mul_add built with RUSTFLAGS=-Ctarget-feature=+fma), which halves FP ops but shifts bits => golden regen + jawxr sign-off REQUIRED. SIDE NOTE: the rank-1 version is 2x faster + rock-stable UNDER FLEET LOAD (BW-contention-resistant: after sigma=0.06s vs baseline sigma=3s under concurrent builds) and uses 256KB scratch vs the old 32MB panel (128x less) — if the orchestrator ever wants robustness/memory over raw single-thread GFLOP/s it's a clean bit-identical swap, but it FAILS the raw Score>=2.0 bar. 7 levers exhausted; bh7iy stays blocked on jawxr FMA build-decision.
close_reason: Rejected tile-8 Gram lever: goldens df_corr/df_cov/df_dot/df_spearman cmp=0, but paired 1M hyperfine failed Score>=2.0 (df_corr 978.5->994.5 ms regression; df_cov 943.9->940.1 ms neutral). Source removed; route next to profile-backed large-output join fanout/materialization primitive.

pandas: df.cumsum(axis=1)/cummin/cummax on Timedelta64 cols → Timedelta64; cumprod raises (per Timedelta² rule).

Smallest surgical: in each of the four axis1 entry points, detect uniformly-Timedelta DataFrame and route to a dedicated typed-i64 pass that emits Scalar::Timedelta64 (NaT for cumprod). Mirrors br-gqrmf/v7spg/v36qy Series surgery.

title: [mock-code-finder] fp-frame SeriesGroupBy::sum/mean silently drop Timedelta64 via agg_numeric (returns NaN per group)
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:13015 — SeriesGroupBy::agg_numeric uses `v.to_f64().ok()` to collect group values into Vec<f64>. Timedelta64.to_f64() errors, so all Timedelta64 values are silently dropped. SeriesGroupBy::sum() and SeriesGroupBy::mean() both go through this path.

Result: df.groupby("g")["dur"].sum() on a Timedelta64 column returns Scalar::Null(NaN) per group instead of Timedelta64 sums.

Pandas: groupby.sum()/.mean() on Timedelta64 returns Timedelta64.

Surgical fix: at the top of agg_numeric, detect if the source column is uniformly-Timedelta64; if so, accumulate per-group in i128 ns and emit Scalar::Timedelta64. Mirrors the nansum/nanmean fix from br-620mj.

OR: add a separate Timedelta64 fast-path branch in SeriesGroupBy::sum and mean that bypasses agg_numeric entirely.

Tests: SeriesGroupBy::sum on Timedelta64 column returns Timedelta64 per group; mean returns Timedelta64 per group. Existing Float64/Int64 tests stay green.

Sibling: br-qrn2w in fp-groupby (raw groupby_sum function) — same root cause, different file.
close_reason: Added column_is_timedelta detector + agg_timedelta helper in SeriesGroupBy. sum/mean preserve Timedelta dtype per group. Commit fa376ad0. 1914 fp-frame tests pass.

title: [perf] fp-index Int64 any/all avoid IndexLabel materialization
description: Index::any and Index::all currently iterate IndexLabel values, forcing lazy Int64 indexes to materialize labels. Add typed Int64 fast paths that evaluate truthiness directly from raw i64 values (nonzero=true), preserving empty-index semantics and mixed-index fallback behavior. Validate typed and affine Int64 any/all keep source labels unmaterialized.
close_reason: Added typed Int64 Index::any/all fast paths that evaluate raw i64 truthiness without materializing labels; focused typed/affine regression tests passed.

title: [testing-fuzzing] No property/fuzz coverage for Series::str.find/rfind char-position invariant on random multi-byte UTF-8
description:
Skill: /testing-fuzzing.

br-frankenpandas-02ae2b fixed Series::str.find/rfind to return char positions
matching pandas. Existing coverage:
- 6 hand-coded regression tests in fp-frame (héllo, café_lo, héllé, etc.)
- 2 live-pandas-oracle tests in fp-conformance (live_oracle_series_str_find_*)

Both use small, fixed inputs. Random multi-byte UTF-8 inputs (combining marks,
4-byte emoji, mixed scripts) could expose corner cases not covered by the
hand-picked test set. The metamorphic invariant is universal:
  if find(sub, s) returns p (>= 0), then chars(s).skip(p).take(|sub|) == sub

Fix: add proptest that generates random multi-byte UTF-8 haystacks and char-aligned
substrings, asserts the char-position invariant for both find() and rfind().
Locks in pandas parity for any future refactor of these methods.

title: Add MultiIndex diff/round/astype reject-closed surfaces
description: Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): MultiIndex is missing diff/round/astype methods. Pandas 2.2.3 raises TypeError/NotImplementedError on each because hierarchical indices can't support these scalar/numeric semantics. Mirror the existing reject-closed pattern used by MultiIndex::shift, MultiIndex::r#str, MultiIndex::asof — add typed methods that return IndexError::InvalidArgument with pandas-style messages, plus regression tests asserting the exact message shape. Single PR.
close_reason: Shipped in 426fcfa: MultiIndex now exposes pandas-parity diff/round/astype methods. astype('object'/'O') clones, astype('category') rejects with NotImplementedError-style message, other dtypes reject with TypeError-style message. diff/round are reject-closed (tuple subtraction / ufunc rint not supported). 3 regression tests pass in fp-index (248 total).

title: Timedelta::parse rejects ISO-8601 durations (PT1H, P1DT2H3M4S) that pandas accepts
description: pd.Timedelta accepts ISO-8601 duration strings (PT1H, P1DT2H3M4S, P2W, PT0.5S, P1D) but FP's Timedelta::parse errored on anything starting with 'P' (parse_compound: 'P' is non-digit -> num_end==0 -> InvalidFormat). pandas' ISO grammar is quirky: uppercase P required, 'T' separator ignored, units W/D/H/M/S work in any position, M is always MINUTES (not months), Y/months + lowercase rejected, only seconds may be fractional (fractional H/D is a pandas BUG giving garbage). Implemented try_parse_iso8601_duration matching pandas for the well-defined cases; rejects fractional non-second components rather than reproducing the pandas bug. Verified vs pandas 2.2.3.
close_reason: Shipped in 9ef0d4d6: Timedelta::parse now accepts ISO-8601 durations.

title: pct_change_axis1 + groupby.pct_change lack the default forward-fill (same root as 5ioai)
description: Sister to 5ioai (now fixed for Series/DataFrame.pct_change). pandas default fill_method='pad' (groupby: 'ffill') also applies to: (1) DataFrame::pct_change_axis1 (fp-frame ~38200) — should ffill ACROSS columns per row (ffill_axis1 exists) before computing; currently propagates NaN. (2) SeriesGroupBy::pct_change (fp-frame ~17781) and DataFrameGroupBy::pct_change (~44060) — should forward-fill WITHIN each group before computing; currently propagate NaN. Both deprecated-but-active in pandas 2.2.3. Verified pandas groupby ffills per group. FIX: axis1 -> self.ffill_axis1(None)? then compute; groupby -> ffill the per-group vals slice at the start of the transform_groups closure before the pct_change loop. Update any codifying tests. Kept out of 5ioai to keep that commit scoped + because per-group/per-row-across-columns ffill is more involved.
close_reason: Fixed in 383c3581: pct_change(axis=1) now ffills across columns per row (via ffill_axis1) and groupby.pct_change (Series+DataFrame) ffills within each group (via new forward_fill_scalars helper) before computing — matching pandas 2.2.3 default fill_method. axis=1 [1,nan,3]->[NaN,0,2]; groupby [1,nan,4]->[NaN,0,3]. Existing no-NaN tests stay green; added 2 NaN regression tests. fp-frame 2795/0, clippy clean. Completes the pct_change ffill-default story (with 5ioai).

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index unit/resolution accessors
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: DatetimeIndex/TimedeltaIndex miss unit/resolution. FrankenPandas's storage is fixed-resolution nanoseconds so both return 'ns'. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 6331deb: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity unit/resolution constants (ns/nanosecond). 1 regression test passes; 309 fp-index tests total.

title: Series::td_binary_op loses index name (td_add/sub/mul/div family)
description: fp-frame/src/lib.rs:2958 Self::from_values builds an unnamed index. Pandas td binary ops preserve the shared axis name. Fix: build manually with rename_index using shared-name logic (self.index.name() == other.index.name() ? self : None).
close_reason: Computed shared_index_name and applied rename_index in td_binary_op at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:2958. Covers all td_* binary ops that go through this helper.

title: Add Series shift and diff method calls to eval/query parser
description: Parity gap: pandas eval/query accepts Series.shift() and Series.diff() with default periods plus positional or periods= integer arguments; fp-frame implements both but fp-expr lacks method-call parser/evaluator support.
close_reason: Implemented eval/query Series shift and diff method parsing

title: [test] fp-index Int64 set ops duplicate-order conformance

close_reason: Added deterministic Int64 set-operation conformance harness for duplicate-order semantics

title: [perf] Typed extraction + O(n) order-stats for DataFrame.describe (was Scalar+full sort, ~2.9x)
description: eydcr 5th-wave: describe 43ms (NOT helped by quantile fix — own code path). Per column it materialized col.values() Scalars then FULL-SORTED for min/max/25/50/75. FIX: typed f64+validity extraction (no materialization) + O(n) min/max folds + reuse quantile_select for the 3 percentiles (select_nth). -0.0 guard routes negative-zero columns to the exact sort path (matches min/max=sorted[0]/[n-1]). count/sum/mean/var folds unchanged + same order. 43->14.8ms=2.9x bit-identical (golden 1cf44437, all-valid+nullable). DONE.
close_reason: Shipped 3407c5de: typed describe — f64+validity extraction + O(n) min/max folds + quantile_select for 25/50/75 (replaces per-column full sort). -0.0 guard routes negative-zero cols to exact sort. 43->14.8ms=2.9x bit-identical (golden 1cf44437, all-valid+nullable). fp-frame 2830 + conformance 0 failures.

title: fp-columnar: add nan_to_num, rint, fix functions
description: Added nan_to_num (with configurable replacement values), rint (banker's rounding), and fix (alias for trunc) for numpy parity.

title: [perf] Typed Float64 fast path for Series/DataFrame.pct_change (Scalar-path was 38x slow)
description: Part of eydcr family. pct_change materialized Vec<Scalar> + per-element to_f64 + Column::new even for typed Float64. 107.5ms@200kx8. FIX: as_f64_slice fast path in pct_change_core ((data[i]-prev)/prev into typed buffer; NaN ratio -> cleared validity bit to match Column::from_values is_missing; +/-inf stays present) + all-valid short-circuit in pct_change_with_fill so the default pad-fill (no-op on all-valid) doesn't re-materialize Scalars. Bit-identical golden 1b3c9972. DONE.
close_reason: Shipped 47e045aa: typed Float64 fast path for pct_change (core as_f64_slice + all-valid ffill short-circuit). 107.5->2.82ms = 38.1x bit-identical (golden 1b3c9972, incl inf from data[0]=0). fp-frame 2830 + fp-conformance 2047 green. eydcr family: diff+pct_change DONE; combine_first 221/corrwith 115 next.

title: [test] differential: Series.where_cond_series (Series other)
description: No-mock differential for Series.where_cond_series (wi1ce used scalar other). Seeded LCG (no rand, no mocks): same-index self/cond(Bool)/other Int64 series; assert out[i]==self[i] if cond[i] else other[i]. Test-only; fp-frame.

title: [porting-to-rust] Series::factorize missing sort + use_na_sentinel options (pandas-2.2 surface)
description:
Skill: /porting-to-rust.

pandas:
  pd.Series([...]).factorize(sort=False, use_na_sentinel=True)

fp-frame Series::factorize() takes no parameters — exposes only the
sort=False/use_na_sentinel=True default. fp-columnar already implements
Column::factorize_with_options(sort, use_na_sentinel) — see br-9433f.
The fp-frame Series wrapper just needs a thin pass-through and Series
reconstruction.

Port:
  pub fn factorize_with_options(&self, sort: bool, use_na_sentinel: bool)
      -> Result<(Series, Series), FrameError>

Behavior:
  - sort=true: uniques are sorted; codes remapped to new positions.
  - use_na_sentinel=false: missing values become a regular bucket with
    their own code instead of -1.

title: to_numeric loses source index name
description: fp-frame/src/lib.rs:19870 Series::from_values discards source axis name on the same-shape numeric-coerce result. Pandas pd.to_numeric preserves source axis name. Fix: build Index with rename_index(series.index().name()).
close_reason: Built result Index with rename_index in to_numeric at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:19870.

title: fp-frame DataFrameGroupBy::cum{sum,prod,min,max} silently coerce Timedelta64 to NaN
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:38115+ DataFrameGroupBy::cumsum/cumprod/cummax/cummin route through transform_groups with f64 accumulators that fall into `Scalar::Null(NaN)` per position when value.to_f64() fails on Timedelta64.

pandas df.groupby(k).cumsum()/cummin()/cummax() returns Timedelta64 per group; cumprod raises (mirror as NaT, per fp-types nanprod br-szq6a).

Sister to SeriesGroupBy cumsum/cumprod/cummin/cummax surgery (br-v6j38). Each closure should detect Timedelta input via vals.iter().find non-missing and route to a typed i64-ns accumulator emitting Scalar::Timedelta64.
close_reason: done

title: perf: drop_duplicates 229x slower than pandas (O(n²) algorithm)

Current API coverage drift reports IndexVariants missing pandas to_frame/to_series while base Index and MultiIndex already expose bounded materialization helpers. Add typed DatetimeIndex, TimedeltaIndex, PeriodIndex, RangeIndex, and CategoricalIndex to_frame()/to_series() forwarders via their existing flat Index representation, preserving label order and name through the existing to_index/to_flat_index paths. Add focused fp-index tests for representative typed labels, then validate with RCH fmt/test/check/clippy plus UBS on the touched file.

Seeded LCG (no rand, no mocks): random i64 i and finite f64 f; assert semantic_cmp(Int64(i),Float64(f)) == (i as f64).partial_cmp(f), the Float64-vs-Int64 direction is its reverse (antisymmetry), and exact-equal int/float compare Equal. Guards the cross-numeric ordering joins/sorts rely on. Test-only; fp-types.

title: perf(columnar): dense direct-address Column::unique for bounded Int64

Bit-identical golden sha256 77b4ca0... (dense vs HashSet; wide-range/nullable/utf8 fallback). Same gate as isin. fp-columnar 373/0 conformance 419/0. Shipped c44ea890.

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] Live pandas oracle missing op_series_str_zfill — sign-handling parity not pinned
description:
Skill: /testing-conformance-harnesses.

fp-frame Series::str.zfill (lib.rs:13963) handles the leading +/- sign
specially when zero-padding (matches Python str.zfill semantics):
  pd.Series(["5","-5","+5"]).str.zfill(4) -> ["0005","-005","+005"]

The conformance harness already exposes FixtureOperation::SeriesStrZfill
(lib.rs:650, 15925) reading fixture.str_width, but pandas_oracle.py has
no op_series_str_zfill handler. dispatch() falls through to OracleError(
unsupported operation), so any live-oracle test on zfill would error.

Effect: sign-handling pandas-parity is verified only by hand-coded crate
tests; no live-oracle regression net for the (sign-prefix, multi-byte
UTF-8, non-numeric inputs) edge cases.

Fix:
1. Add op_series_str_zfill(pd, payload) to pandas_oracle.py
2. Wire into dispatch()
3. Add live_oracle_series_str_zfill_sign_handling test in fp-conformance
   covering signed numerics, multi-byte UTF-8 ("5é"), and ASCII-only
   inputs across width=0/3/8.

title: [perf] COW/Arc-shared Column backing — O(1) clone for zero-copy identity join lanes (string joins ~3-5x)
description: COW / Arc-shared column backing so Column::clone() is O(1) instead of a deep per-element Vec<Scalar> clone. MOTIVATION: string left/outer joins (str_left 39ms, str_outer 59ms @100k) are byte-materialization-bound — the kept (identity) Utf8 lanes deep-clone their Vec<Scalar::Utf8> (per-String malloc; profile: 24% ScalarValues::clone + 19% drop_in_place). Confirmed 2026-06-09: routing through lazy_contiguous_utf8 (one byte gather) is only 1.16x because the byte COPY remains and Scalar-backed (from_dict) source can't be shared zero-copy. LEVER: change ScalarValues::Scalar eager backing from Vec<Scalar> to Arc<[Scalar]> (clone = O(1) Arc bump; mutation via Arc::make_mut COW). Then identity_merge_column / any kept-lane clone is zero-copy, and the bench (reads index.len() only) never materializes -> string joins approach the i64/f64 lazy-lane ceiling (~3-5x). BROAD: touches every ScalarValues::Scalar construct/mutate site; needs Arc::make_mut at &mut Vec<Scalar> sites; gate on full fp-columnar+fp-frame+fp-conformance suites + golden (str_left 995b9ca3 / str_outer 72546ef6 unchanged). Alien primitive: persistent/structural-sharing data structures. Also helps any clone-heavy Column path (DataFrame.copy, slicing, etc).
close_reason: Shipped fc692c56: ScalarValues::Eager backing Vec<Scalar> -> Arc<[Scalar]>, O(1) Column::clone. str_left_join 24.4->7.6ms = 3.2x bit-identical (golden 995b9ca3/72546ef6/be7d1711 unchanged). str_outer neutral (null-gather bound, not clone bound). fp-columnar/frame/join/conformance all green.

title: [test] metamorphic: expanding sum/max/min == cumsum/cummax/cummin
description: No-mock cross-op consistency: Series.expanding(min_periods=1).sum()==cumsum(), .max()==cummax(), .min()==cummin() element-wise. Seeded LCG, Int64. Test-only; fp-frame.

close_reason: Switched to with_labels_and_values_preserving_name in Series::append at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:3128.

One lever: make RangeIndex::get_level_values and RangeIndex::droplevel handle flat level validation directly instead of materializing a flat Index just to forward, preserving level-0 output, invalid-level OutOfBounds, droplevel-only-level InvalidArgument text, name propagation, and typed Int64 output backing. Validation limited to CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.rch-targets/frankenpandas-cod-b cargo check -p fp-index; batch-test pending.

title: dt.round/floor/ceil string path is second-granular: sub-second freqs (ms/us/ns) + 's'-freq half-second ties are imprecise
close_reason: done

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Discovered while fixing dt.round banker's ties (751bb0a1). fp-frame Series.dt round/floor/ceil go through round_datetime + total_secs_approx, which parse only Y-M-D h:mi:sec (no fractional seconds) and round_datetime returns Null for freqs finer than 's'. Consequences vs live pandas 2.2.3: (1) round('s') on 00:00:01.5 should banker's to 00:00:02 but FP drops the .5 -> 00:00:01; (2) freq in ms/us/ns unsupported (Null). Proper fix: operate on the i64-ns Datetime64 representation (units since Unix epoch) instead of the string repr, then ns/freq_ns round_ties_even. Larger refactor of the dt string accessor path. Not blocking; integer-second freqs (min/h/D) are now correct.

title: [parity] cut_bins/qcut_at_quantiles lack labels= (custom bin labels)
description: Follow-up to cut_bins (tlwfo) and qcut_at_quantiles (hlhfo): pandas cut/qcut accept labels=['lo','hi',...] to replace interval-string labels with custom names. Added labels: Option<&[&str]> to both; Some replaces per-bin labels (validated count==n_bins, error on mismatch), None keeps interval strings. Bucket assignment unchanged. Verified vs pandas 2.2.3.
close_reason: Added labels: Option<&[&str]> to cut_bins + qcut_at_quantiles — Some replaces interval strings per bin (count must == n_bins, else error), None keeps intervals; bucket assignment unchanged. Verified vs pandas 2.2.3 (cut/qcut labeled + out-of-range NaN + count-mismatch error). 15 cut/qcut tests green, clippy clean.

title: Add CategoricalIndex searchsorted/intersection/union/difference
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: CategoricalIndex still misses searchsorted plus the four set-operations. Forward via to_index() to the underlying Index. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 51bd25e: CategoricalIndex now exposes pandas-parity searchsorted + 3 set ops (intersection/union/difference). 1 regression test passes; 313 fp-index tests total.

title: [perf] nullable-Utf8/Bool typed column backing — kill Scalar tax in string/bool join+reindex null-introducing gathers (~3x)
description:
GAP: Column::reindex_by_positions (used by ALL left/right/outer merges for non-i64-dense keys AND DataFrame/Series.reindex) has typed null-introducing fast paths for Int64 (from_i64_values_with_validity) and Float64 (from_f64_values_with_validity, 0.0-datum gap convention), but Utf8 and Bool fall to the Scalar path: per-row Scalar clone + Column::new revalidation. Same Scalar tax the asof RightGather had before f84143df (which got 3.8x for f64). 

ROOT CAUSE: no nullable-Utf8 or nullable-Bool ScalarValues backing exists (only LazyContiguousUtf8 all-valid + LazyAllValidBool). 

LEVER (different memory layout, NO-GAPS): add LazyNullableUtf8 { bytes: Arc<[u8]>, offsets: Arc<[usize]>, validity } (null slot = empty span + cleared bit; materializes Utf8(span) when valid, Null(NullKind::Null) when invalid = missing_for_dtype(Utf8)) + from_utf8_values_with_validity, and a LazyNullableBool mirror. Route reindex_by_positions Utf8/Bool arms through them (gather present spans into one buffer + bitset, no per-row String clone). 

ISOMORPHISM: valid slot = Utf8 of same span bytes; invalid = missing_for_dtype(dtype) — exactly the Scalar fallback. Target ~3x like asof f64. 

PROOF PLAN: add a str-payload left-join perf scenario (string value column, ~half rows unmatched) + golden sha256; bit-identical A/B. 

BLAST RADIUS: new ScalarValues variant touches ~20 exhaustive matches (materialize/len/clone/take_positions/as_*_slice/etc) — dedicated session. fp-columnar + fp-join.
close_reason: Landed bc6d6ecf main+master: LazyNullableUtf8 backing, 1.7x bit-identical (goldens 884a3449/995b9ca3/72546ef6 unchanged), 383+108+2830 tests green. Bool follow-up remains.

title: [perf] csv_read SIMD delimiter scanner for simple typed numeric fast path
description: Post 42bc0930 RCH reprofile ranks csv_read 100000x20 as the hottest unclaimed/disjoint scenario at 700.6 ms +/- 14.5, above join_1to1 656.2 ms and RubyGoose-owned series_add_align 460.2 ms. Previous retained GDB profile sampled try_read_csv_str_simple_typed_numeric byte iteration plus parse_f64 paths; fresh GDB/perf/samply sampling is blocked by yama/perf_event_paranoid, so this bead must begin from the retained CSV profile and revalidate with benchmark/golden before keeping any code. Alien primitive: branchless/SIMD byte framing via memchr::memchr3_iter, replacing scalar per-byte delimiter/quote scanning in the simple typed numeric CSV path. Scope fp-io/Cargo deps only; do not touch fp-frame/fp-columnar/RubyGoose surfaces. Proof obligations: quoted fields reject to fallback exactly, CRLF accepted, bare CR rejected, ragged rows rejected, default NA/bool/NaN fallback unchanged, row/column/index order and FP bits unchanged, golden csv_read sha256 unchanged. Keep only if RCH csv_read 100000x20 Score>=2.0; otherwise revert code and retain evidence only.
close_reason: Rejected and reverted: RCH hyperfine csv_read 100000x20 worsened from post-42bc0930 baseline 700.6 ms +/- 14.5 ms to 710.2 ms +/- 8.3 ms. Golden sha256 unchanged at 17f95925cec162c66933d5e935566b6f6fec5f8ef56872b98b00fb97ebf59fff, so parity held but Score < 2.0. Next action: leave this scalar-framing loop and attack a different profiler-backed primitive.

title: [test] differential: concat_series stacks values (ignore_index)
description: No-mock differential for concat_series_with_ignore_index. Seeded LCG (no rand, no mocks): concat 2-3 Int64 series with ignore_index=true; assert result values == concatenation of inputs in order and length == sum of lengths. Test-only; fp-frame.

title: [conformance] 25 packet fixtures expect Float64 dtype where current code returns Int64/Bool (cumsum/cumprod/cummin/cummax/describe/round)
description:
fp-conformance/src/tests/packet_filter_runs.rs has 25 failing tests as of commit 6080c79e (main). The mismatch class is "execution_critical" — value type mismatch:

> "value mismatch at idx=0: actual=Int64(1), expected=Float64(1.0)"

Affected packets (sample):
- FP-P2D-066 series_cumsum_int_strict — expects Float64, code returns Int64 (correct per pandas 2.2.3)
- FP-P2D-* series_cumprod, cummin, cummax, describe, round — same pattern

Root cause: the fixture JSONs under crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/ were generated before the dtype-preservation upgrades (fp-frame Series::cumsum at line 5797 has an all-Int64 fast path that returns Int64; the fixtures still expect the older Float64-coerced output).

Pandas reality: `pd.Series([1,2,3]).cumsum().dtype` is int64. Our code is correct; fixtures are stale.

Fix options:
(a) Regenerate fixtures against pandas 2.2.3 oracle (requires the oracle script + python env). Most authoritative but heavyweight.
(b) Update PacketParityReport / case-comparator to accept Int64 where Float64 expected when values are equal — risky, masks real bugs.
(c) Hand-edit the small number of affected fixtures.

Recommend (a) but blocked on live-oracle environment. Filing as P2 to track.

Full failure list available via `cargo test -p fp-conformance --tests 2>&1 | grep FAILED`.
close_reason: Regenerated stale numeric packet fixtures against pandas 2.2.3 oracle

title: [parity-bug] ewm var/std/sum/cov/corr likely adjust=False; ewm() lacks com/halflife/min_periods/adjust/ignore_na params
description: Follow-up to usdk2 (ewm.mean fixed to adjust=True). Likely-divergent: (1) ewm().sum()/var()/std()/cov()/corr() probably use the same adjust=False recursion and diverge from pandas default adjust=True (var/std also default bias=False). Verify each vs live pandas and fix with the old_wt weighting (cov/var need the sum-of-weights and sum-of-squared-weights bias correction). (2) FP ewm(span,alpha) lacks pandas params: com=, halflife=, min_periods=, adjust= (to allow adjust=False), ignore_na=. Add them (signature change rippling to DataFrame/groupby/online ewm). Oracle: pd.Series(...).ewm(...).<agg>().
close_reason: ewm cov()/corr() rewritten to pandas' exact ewmcov: cov=debiased ewmcov(x,y) (adjust=True/bias=False/ignore_na=False); corr=cov_xy/sqrt(cov_xx*cov_yy) with biased accumulators. Combined with var/std (5762498d) and mean (934019fc), all ewm reduction stats now match pandas default. ewm().sum() was already correct. Oracle-verified 6-case cov/corr battery + 4-case var battery + 7-case mean battery. 41 ewm tests green, clippy clean. Param-coverage (com/halflife/min_periods/adjust=/ignore_na=) remains a separate enhancement (filed note).

Golden-identical (391 tests + golden_dedup_i64).

title: astype(bool) of NaN yields null (FP NaN=missing model) instead of pandas/numpy True
close_reason: done

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Found shipping the astype(bool) nonzero fix. cast_scalar_owned returns early Scalar::missing_for_dtype(target) when value.is_missing(); Float64(NaN).is_missing()=true, so NaN->Bool yields Null not pandas/numpy True (bool(nan)=True). FP's NaN=missing model is deliberate; matching pandas here needs a Float64(NaN)->Bool special-case BEFORE the is_missing early-return. LOW priority (edge); note astype('boolean') nullable WOULD give NA, so only the numpy-bool path diverges.

title: Add Index variants view/transpose/ravel/nlevels/infer_objects
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): the typed Index variants still miss the 'identity-like' surface that pandas exposes on every Index. Mirrors the forwarder pattern from earlier beads.

Add five methods on each of DatetimeIndex / TimedeltaIndex / PeriodIndex / RangeIndex / CategoricalIndex:
- view() -> Self — clones self (pandas returns a view; FrankenPandas's owned-storage model materializes a fresh clone).
- transpose() -> Self — 1D transpose is identity, matching pandas.
- ravel() -> the variant's value type vector (Vec<Option<i64>> for Datetime/Timedelta, Vec<Period> for PeriodIndex, Vec<i64> for RangeIndex, Vec<String> for CategoricalIndex).
- nlevels() -> usize — always 1 for a flat Index variant (pandas returns 1).
- infer_objects() -> Self — no-op for typed indexes (pandas re-infers dtypes; typed indexes stay typed).

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: identity behavior of view/transpose/infer_objects, ravel returning the same values as values(), and nlevels==1.
close_reason: Shipped in ee49689: 5 Index variants now expose pandas-parity view/transpose/ravel/nlevels/infer_objects. 1 regression test exercises the matrix; 290 fp-index tests total.

title: [mock-code-finder] DatetimeAccessor silently returns NaN for non-Utf8 series instead of erroring (pandas raises AttributeError)

pandas:

  AttributeError: Can only use .dt accessor with datetimelike values

fp-frame DatetimeAccessor::extract_component (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14782) and
try_extract_component (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14799) have catch-all match arms
"_ => Scalar::Null(NullKind::NaN)" that silently swallow Float64/Int64/Bool/Timedelta64
series, returning all-NaN. This hides type errors from users.

Skill: /mock-code-finder — silent-stub catch-all in match.

Fix: validate dtype at start of helpers; allow only Utf8 (fp-frame stores datetime as ISO
string) and Null (all-missing). Return CompatibilityRejected for other dtypes.
Affects all .dt accessor methods: year, month, day, hour, minute, second,
quarter, weekday, day_of_year, etc.

Widened to (Option<i64>)x3 + python_slice_chars helper (CPython PySlice_AdjustIndices), step=0 rejected. Plumbed str_slice_step through fp-conformance + oracle. Verified vs live pandas 2.2.3. title: [perf] Fuse redundant NaN pre-scans in aligned_binary_f64_same_positions (series add vs pandas 8x gap)
description: VS-PANDAS GAP (BlackThrush 2026-06-12): identical-index Float64 Series add is the only elementwise gap found in a vs-pandas ratio sweep. FP series_add_same 0.768ms vs pandas Int64Index-aligned a+b 0.096ms = 8x SLOWER. perf record: 60% self in TWO input NaN pre-scans (!lsrc.any(is_nan) && !rsrc.any(is_nan)), 15.5% from_f64_values output scan, 11.5% the add. LEVER: for non-Pow ops drop the two input NaN pre-scans, go straight to from_f64_values(apply_f64_slices). ISOMORPHISM: Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Mod/FloorDiv propagate NaN; from_f64_values marks NaN-output missing exactly as general nan_aware_validity routes input-NaN to Scalar::Null(NaN). Pow EXCLUDED (NaN**0==1.0 stays valid; existing behavior is missing). Golden series_add_same 1000/100000 + NaN/inf unit test. Target ~0.31ms Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: no parity-safe variant reaches Score>=2.0 (true gap ~2.5x, not 8x; validity-scan is an inherent extra pass). Shipped parity-locking test + non-removable-scan comment. Real lever (deferred Float64 validity) filed as follow-up.

title: [test] differential: groupby cumcount within-group ordinal
description: No-mock differential for DataFrameGroupBy.cumcount. Seeded LCG (no rand, no mocks): keys (small domain); groupby(k).cumcount() yields 0-based within-group ordinal aligned to original row order, vs HashMap<key,counter> oracle. Test-only; fp-frame.

title: [mock-code-finder] DataFrame row-axis reductions (sum/mean/min/max etc.) silently drop Bool columns (pandas treats Bool as 0/1)
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:31188 — `reduce_rows` filters columns to only Int64/Float64 before row-wise aggregation:

```rust
.filter(|c| {
    let dt = self.columns[c.as_str()].dtype();
    dt == DType::Int64 || dt == DType::Float64
})
```

This excludes Bool columns. Pandas treats Bool as 0/1 numeric in row reductions:
> pd.DataFrame({'a': [True,False], 'b': [1,2]}).sum(axis=1) == [2, 2]
Our code excludes column 'a' and returns [1, 2].

Affects all row-axis reductions that go through reduce_rows: sum_axis1, mean_axis1, min_axis1, max_axis1, std_axis1, var_axis1, sem_axis1, skew_axis1, kurt_axis1, median_axis1.

Surgical fix: extend the dtype filter to include DType::Bool. Bool.to_f64() already returns Ok(0.0 / 1.0), so the per-element conversion at line 31206 already handles Bool values correctly once the filter lets the column through.

Tests: sum_axis1 on a frame with a Bool column counts True=1/False=0 contributions. Existing Int64+Float64 tests stay green.
close_reason: Extended reduce_rows dtype filter to include Bool. Bool.to_f64() already returns 0.0/1.0 so per-element conversion is correct. Commit d6946701. 1905 fp-frame tests pass.

title: [mock-code-finder] fuzz_rolling_window seed expects min_periods > window to succeed; conflicts with br-7c962 pandas-faithful validation
description:
fp-conformance/src/tests/fuzz_seed_fixtures.rs fuzz_rolling_window_bytes_accepts_min_periods_gt_window_seed fails with:
> "rolling sum unexpectedly failed: window=1 min_periods=Some(2) err=CompatibilityRejected(\"rolling: min_periods 2 must be <= window 1\")"

The seed expects rolling(window=1, min_periods=2).sum() to succeed and produce all NaN. But br-frankenpandas-7c962 introduced the pandas-faithful validation at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:9308 explicitly because pandas DOES raise on min_periods > window:
> Per br-frankenpandas-7c962: pandas validates min_periods <= window.

The seed predates br-7c962 and froze the older lenient behavior.

Fix: delete the obsolete seed file fuzz/corpus/fuzz_rolling_window/min_periods_gt_window.bin (or wherever) and rename/repurpose the test name. Match the pattern used in br-frankenpandas-je5o4 (just-shipped corpus cleanup).

Alternatively if the test was deliberately authored to assert the lenient behavior, update the test to assert that Err is returned instead.
close_reason: Added min_periods>window tolerated rejection arm to fuzz validator. Commit 23c30bad. All 4 min_periods tests pass.

Materializing a multiindex level to a column (to_csv/to_json of multiindex frames) made datetime levels object columns (pandas keeps datetime64), and serialized inconsistently (ISO string in to_json vs epoch-ms for single DatetimeIndex; bypassed to_csv column-uniform format).

title: [testing-fuzzing] No proptest for Series::quantile_with_interpolation cross-mode ordering invariant
description:
Skill: /testing-fuzzing.

br-frankenpandas-a56003 added Series::quantile_with_interpolation with 5
modes (linear/lower/higher/nearest/midpoint). 14 hand-coded tests cover
fixed inputs but no random property test asserts the cross-mode ordering
invariant.

Strong invariants on any non-empty numeric Series s and q in [0,1]:
  quantile(q, lower)   <= quantile(q, nearest) <= quantile(q, higher)
  quantile(q, lower)   <= quantile(q, linear)  <= quantile(q, higher)
  quantile(q, lower)   <= quantile(q, midpoint) <= quantile(q, higher)
  quantile(q, midpoint) == (quantile(q, lower) + quantile(q, higher)) / 2

Random Series and q surface boundary cases the hand-written tests miss
(small Series of length 2, q very close to 0 or 1, identical-value runs).

title: [perf][no-gaps] DataFrame.corr(spearman/kendall) pairwise matrix: exploit exact symmetry + spearman rank-precompute (bit-identical)
close_reason: Shipped 2-commit deliverable, both BIT-IDENTICAL (golden sha256 unchanged: spearman=36c26986.., kendall=0095a8b0..; 5 rank-corr unit tests pass). Commit 568c3757 (symmetry): spearman/kendall matrices are exactly symmetric (pearson_on_slices deviation-form cov is commutative per-term; kendall concordant/tied counts symmetric under x<->y) -> compute upper triangle + copy. Commit 4c5f825d (rank-precompute): fully-numeric columns have partner-independent ranks -> precompute once (O(N) not O(N^2) rankings). Bench (release-perf, hyperfine): spearman 4000x64: 13.254s -> 7.772s (symmetry) -> 0.192s (precompute) = 69x total; kendall 1500x32: 34.114s -> 17.793s = 1.92x (symmetry; precompute N/A). Harness: df_spearman/df_kendall scenarios added to perf_profile. Technique: [[fp-symmetry-parity-technique]].

title: [perf] Precompute Kendall no-tie sorted orders
description:
Profile-backed target: after Spearman pass, RCH-built release-perf reprofile shows df_kendall 512x5 around 100 ms, now the remaining non-owned frame-correlation hotspot while join/typed-columnar are owned by other agents. Current pairwise_rank_corr already reuses complete finite column values, but every Kendall pair still calls kendall_no_tie_fast_slices, re-sorting x and y and re-checking no-tie witnesses for the same columns across O(cols^2) pairs.

Alien primitive: reusable sorted-permutation semantic witness / inversion-count kernel. One lever only: for complete finite Kendall columns, precompute each column's sort order and no-tie witness once using the same comparator and EPSILON tie rule. Pair cells then reuse the left column's sorted order, gather the right values into y-order, and run the existing inversion counter. Fallback unchanged if either column is incomplete, non-finite, or tied.

Proof obligations: RCH-built before/after hyperfine for df_kendall, golden sha256 unchanged, row/column order unchanged, tie handling unchanged, count formula unchanged, no RNG/hash/FP association behavior. Validate focused fp-frame Kendall tests/check/clippy/fmt/UBS as feasible; retain only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Kept Kendall sorted-order witness reuse: RCH-built paired hyperfine df_kendall 512x5 improved 101.1 ms +/- 3.2 to 68.5 ms +/- 2.9 (1.48x faster). Golden output byte-identical; sha256 dcce8a4e1f13d887361650ec24fcfee13c6c4c3954936fd124a1feb53cf125ce. Validation: RCH cargo test -p fp-frame kendall --lib -- --nocapture, RCH cargo check -p fp-frame --all-targets, RCH cargo clippy -p fp-frame --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt -p fp-frame -- --check, git diff --check, golden sha256/cmp. UBS on crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs timed out after 90s in rust scan with no findings emitted and no lingering shadow processes. Reprofile after shows df_spearman ~50.6 ms, df_kendall ~64.3 ms, df_corr ~116 ms, df_cov ~119 ms.

Implement TimedeltaIndex::components() over existing Timedelta64 nanosecond labels using fp_types::Timedelta::components, preserving NaT as None. Add focused fp-index tests for positive, negative, zero, and NaT component rows, prove the drift scanner no longer lists components, and validate with RCH fp-index test/check/clippy/fmt, git diff --check, and UBS on touched Rust files.
close_reason: Added TimedeltaIndex::components with pandas-normalized negative component rows and NaT propagation; validated with RCH fp-index test/check/clippy/fmt, drift scanner, git diff --check, and UBS.

title: [profiling] DataFrame::normalize_column_order O(n²) dedup-keep-last in DataFrame constructor hot path
description:
Skill: /profiling-software-performance.

crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:18374 normalize_column_order is called from
DataFrame::new_with_column_order (line 18682) — i.e., every DataFrame
construction with explicit ordering. Three quadratic ops:
  1. normalized.iter().position(|entry| entry == &name)  -- O(n) per name
  2. normalized.remove(existing_idx)                      -- O(n) shift
  3. normalized.iter().any(|entry| entry == name)         -- O(n) per missing column

For column_order of size N with duplicates, total O(N²). Becomes a real
bottleneck in get_dummies / transpose / pivot / wide-DataFrame paths that
produce hundreds of columns.

Fix: pre-compute last_idx HashMap<name, usize> once, then iterate forward
emitting only at the last-occurrence index. Use HashSet<&str> for the
missing-column check. O(n+k) total where n = column_order.len(), k = columns.len().

title: [perf] Skip dense inner-join parallelism probe when all lanes are lazy
description: Profile-backed follow-up after br-frankenpandas-l4adm. The after profile tests/artifacts/perf/perf_report_dense_join_shared_plan_after_l4adm_inner_join.txt shows fp_join::build_dense_i64_inner_output_data still hot (52.48% children / 34.09% self) and std::thread::functions::available_parallelism at 4.57% children. Code currently computes available_parallelism before checking whether full_specs is empty. On high-fanout dense inner joins, repeat-run/repeated-slice lazy lanes remove all full specs, so the syscall/metadata probe is wasted. One lever: make the parallelism query conditional on full_specs being non-empty (or otherwise eliminate it on the all-lazy path) without changing row order, right-bucket tie order, output column order/names/dtypes, null/fallback behavior, FP bits, or RNG. Baseline current main via RCH release-perf perf_profile + golden SHA first; rebench inner_join/join_1to1 controls; retain only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: guarded available_parallelism probe preserved golden SHA but failed paired timing gate. inner_join 100000x50 regressed 116.8ms +/- 10.9 -> 302.9ms +/- 13.8; inner_join_read 1.105s +/- 0.016 -> 1.145s +/- 0.064; join_1to1 60.7ms +/- 5.2 -> 67.8ms +/- 4.9. Source edit reverted; evidence tests/artifacts/perf/parallelism_probe_ikphf_rejection_witness.md. Route next to deeper dense-output row-plan primitive.

title: [mock-code-finder] SeriesGroupBy::pct_change silently returns NaN for Timedelta64 input
description:
Skill: /mock-code-finder — sister to br-naeja.

SeriesGroupBy::pct_change (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:13594) uses the same to_f64-else-NaN catch-all pattern. Timedelta64 values silently produce NaN per element instead of computing the dimensionless f64 ratio.

Same fix as br-naeja: add a Timedelta64 fast path before the to_f64 conversion, with NaT propagation and zero-prev handling consistent with the existing pandas-style logic.

Tests:
- groupby(...).pct_change on Timedelta64 produces ratios per group.
- NaT propagation per group.

title: DataFrame.corr/cov matrix path one-pass cancellation (finalize_pairwise_stat)
description: Series.corr was fixed to two-pass mean-centered (71ccc531, br r3sfc follow-up). The DataFrame.corr()/cov() MATRIX path (fp-frame finalize_pairwise_stat ~41676) STILL uses the cancellation-prone one-pass form: cov_xy=(sum_xy - n*mx*my)/(n-1), var_x=(sum_x2 - n*mx^2)/(n-1). For large-magnitude near-equal columns var_x cancels to 0.0 -> NaN where pandas gives a finite value (same defect, verified: x~1e9/y~5e9 anti-correlated -> pandas -1.0, one-pass NaN). ENTANGLEMENT (why not a trivial fix like Series): the matrix path is a deliberately perf-optimized single-pass GRAM-MATRIX kernel (pairwise_stat_matrix ~41727, accumulates moments ONCE per unordered pair, halving O(N^2*M) work) with BIT-FOR-BIT golden pinning (the (i,j)/(j,i) ULP-symmetry comment). A naive two-pass per pair ~2x the O(M) work (undoes the optimization) AND changes goldens (ulp). OPTIONS: (a) two-pass per pair (correct, slower, regen goldens); (b) SHIFTED one-pass — accumulate Sigma(x-Kx)(y-Ky) with per-column shift Kx (e.g. first valid obs) keeping the single Gram pass but killing cancellation since (x-Kx) is small (changes goldens by ulp, preserves perf); (c) accept the edge-case gap. Recommend (b). Conformance df.corr fixtures (fp_p2d_146) use semantic_eq relative-tol so they tolerate the change; check local assert_text_golden corr/cov tests. cod owns the Gram perf optimization — coordinate.
close_reason: FIXED c26015c3. Exploited cov/corr shift-invariance: per-column pre-shift (first finite obs) at the top of pairwise_stat_matrix brings accumulators to range-scale, killing the one-pass cancellation, while the Gram kernel + finalize_pairwise_stat internals stay UNTOUCHED (both complete + per-pair NaN paths run unchanged on shifted data). Turned out BIT-TRANSPARENT for normal data — all corr/cov/corrwith text goldens + conformance corr fixtures pass unchanged, only the large-magnitude NaN->finite case changes. No golden regen, no kernel risk, ~O(n*m) overhead. Verified vs pandas 2.2.3 (df.corr -1.0 / df.cov -0.91666 for a~1e9/b~5e9). The recommended option (b) shifted-one-pass worked perfectly.

title: [modes-of-reasoning] prop_dataframe_abs_is_sign_flip_invariant fails on Int64 inputs due to mul_scalar dtype promotion
description:
Skill: /modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis.

Failing proptest: prop_dataframe_abs_is_sign_flip_invariant.
Minimal failing input: df = {a: [Int64(0)], b: [Int64(0)]}.

Property: df.abs().equals(df.mul_scalar(-1.0).abs()).

Root cause analysis:
1. df has Int64 columns (a=0, b=0).
2. baseline = df.abs() preserves Int64 dtype: {a: Int64(0), b: Int64(0)}.
3. flipped = df.mul_scalar(-1.0). apply_scalar_op (lib.rs:29283) ALWAYS
   emits Scalar::Float64 line 29299, so flipped has Float64 dtype.
4. flipped_abs = flipped.abs() = {a: Float64(0.0), b: Float64(0.0)}.
5. baseline.equals(flipped_abs) -> false (dtype mismatch — equals is
   dtype-strict, matching pandas .equals).

This is a test-design bug, not an fp-frame bug. pandas itself promotes
Int64 * float -> Float64 (>>> (pd.DataFrame({"a":[0]}) * -1.0).dtypes
== a:float64), and pandas .equals() is also dtype-strict.

Fix: replace baseline.equals(&flipped_abs) with the existing
approx_equal_dataframe helper, which converts both sides to f64 with
tolerance — dtype-agnostic. Same shape as 14 other proptests in the
file that use approx_equal_* helpers when arithmetic is involved.

Added typed inline-i64 right-position maps (left identity 0..n for align_left, union=left.clone(); inner emits left-order matches). inner 193->72ms (2.7x), left 181->74ms (2.4x). Golden-identical (391 tests + golden_align_inner_left_i64 inner+left across sorted/unsorted/disjoint/identical/subsets/neg/empty/extremes). Propagates to fp-frame binary ops + reindex.

title: fp-columnar: add flip alias for reverse
[mock-code-finder] Series::cumsum/cummin/cummax errors on Utf8+Null mix instead of skipna pass-through
description:
Skill: /mock-code-finder.

Series::cumsum (lib.rs:5288), cummin (lib.rs:5384), and cummax (lib.rs:5446)
each have a Utf8 specialization gated by:
  values.iter().all(|v| matches!(v, Scalar::Utf8(_)))

Any Null in the series breaks the homogeneity check, so cumsum/cummin/cummax
on a Utf8 series with missing values falls through to the float64 path,
which then errors at val.to_f64() because Scalar::Utf8("a") cannot parse
as f64.

Pandas (skipna=True default) preserves Null positions and updates the
accumulator only on non-missing values:
  >>> pd.Series(["a", None, "b"]).cummax()
  0      a
  1    NaN
  2      b

Fix: loosen the gate to accept Utf8 OR missing, with at least one Utf8.
In the loop, branch on missing (push NaN, skip acc update) vs Utf8
(update acc, push the running result).

Affects:
- Series::cumsum (lib.rs:5288)
- Series::cummin (lib.rs:5384)
- Series::cummax (lib.rs:5446)

title: [mock-code-finder] fp-columnar Column::diff silently returns NaN for Timedelta64 and forces Float64 output dtype
description:
fp-columnar/src/lib.rs:3327-3354 — Column::diff uses `match (cur.to_f64(), prev.to_f64())` with catch-all `_ => NaN` arm, and hardcodes `DType::Float64` for output. Timedelta64.to_f64() returns NonNumericValue, so diff on a Timedelta64 column silently produces all-NaN Float64 output instead of Timedelta64 differences.

Pandas: diff on a Timedelta64 series returns Timedelta64 differences (preserves dtype). NaT propagates.

Fix: detect when input dtype is Timedelta64; for Timedelta64 columns, output Scalar::Timedelta64(cur_ns - prev_ns) with NaT propagation and emit a Timedelta64 dtype column. For other types, keep existing Float64 path. Mirrors the fp-frame Series::diff fix from br-q82t4.

Tests: diff on Timedelta64 Column returns Timedelta64 differences (NaT at boundary positions). NaT propagation. Existing Int64/Float64 tests remain green.
close_reason: Implemented Timedelta64 fast path in Column::diff with dtype-preservation. Commit 360f2ad1. Tests: 2 new + 16 existing diff tests, 296 fp-columnar tests pass.

title: fp-frame Series::var_ddof + std_ddof don't preserve Timedelta64

Surgical: copy the Timedelta64 branches added to var() (br-yy0ks) and std() (br-j0ilf), using ddof instead of hardcoded 1 in the divisor.

title: fix: DataFrame.value_counts tie-break first-occurrence vs pandas key-asc; MASKED by FP-adapted oracle (2nd instance)
close_reason: done

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fixed in 11422ecf. 2nd oracle-masking instance (after rsplit_get 77179735). DataFrame::value_counts used stable count-desc sort -> ties kept FIRST-OCCURRENCE; pandas breaks count ties by composite key ASCENDING (numeric-aware): [(b,2),(a,1),(c,3)] all count2 -> a/b/c; [(10),(10),(2),(2),(5)] -> (2),(10),(5). MASKED: oracle took pandas correct output and re-sorted to FP first-occurrence (items.sort(-count, first_occ)). Fix: sort count-desc then composite_key_cmp asc; oracle keeps pandas native order; regenerated basic_strict+tie_order_strict fixtures (signed_zero/with_nulls had distinct counts, unchanged). Verified vs live pandas 2.2.3. fp-frame value_counts 30/0. Found via the audit grep of pandas_oracle.py for 'matches FP'/'FP'/'Rust' comments.

Add arms delegating to fp_types::nanskew/nankurt/nansem (same kernels as agg). Additive. Code-first; test added. fp-frame.

Added typed all-Int64 path: as_i64_slice raw &[i64] + inline-key FxHashMap<i64,&Scalar> (skip materialization + SipHash), PLUS typed-output sub-path (full-coverage int64->int64 emits Vec<i64>+from_i64_values directly, no Scalar boxing / from_values rescan). 42.2->10.6ms = 4.0x (interleaved A/B), now 1.06x FASTER than pandas. Same for map_with_default (Int64 default => always typed). Golden-identical (33 map tests + golden_series_map_i64: full/partial/mixed-value-types/dup-keys/neg/empty/extremes).

title: [perf] series_add_align semantic witness fingerprint reuse primitive
description:
Profile-backed follow-up after rejected 8zgjh and m7b5d position-map levers. Retained pass-27 binary plus fresh m7b5d GDB sampling show series_add_align remains hot and repeatedly samples record_alignment_semantic_witness -> semantic_index_identity -> semantic_integer_index_labels_fingerprint -> SHA256/update_i64_decimal, alongside align_union_merge_sorted. Position-map range and streamed-merge attempts preserved golden SHA but regressed, so do NOT repeat them.

Target a deeper structural primitive: reuse/carry semantic output index identity or fingerprint for sorted unique Series alignment so the hardened EvidenceLedger witness does not re-hash the newly materialized output index every iteration. Candidate families: cached output fingerprint on alignment plan, incremental merge-time SemanticFingerprintBuilder, or an fp-index/fp-runtime witness identity primitive with exact byte-for-byte fingerprint equivalence. Must preserve ledger contents exactly; no skipping witnesses.

Required loop: baseline/golden with RCH-built retained binary, profile/sampling must confirm witness/fingerprint remains top-5, implement exactly one lever only if Score>=2.0, prove ordering/tie-breaking/null-NaN/floating-point/RNG and semantic witness bytes unchanged with golden sha256 and focused witness tests, rebench via RCH-built candidate, retain only if paired A/B wins with Score>=2.0, then reprofile.
close_reason: Completed: cached sorted-unique alignment witness fingerprints; paired A/B series_add_align 100000 100 improved 772.3 ms to 471.5 ms (1.64x) with unchanged golden sha256 0d328c9236e35ac3f85c109248a2177080d25ac7693f01873a4dc0b97d219ca2

title: [perf] drop_duplicates exact-bits FxHashSet uniqueness (drop sort) 7.6x
description: fp-bench drop_duplicates 100k float64 = 3.64ms vs pandas 1.89ms (1.93x). drop_duplicates' all-valid-unique Float64 subset shortcut (subset_has_all_valid_unique_column) did a SipHash std::HashSet exact pre-check (n inserts) THEN an O(n log n) comparison sort_by(total_cmp) + fuzzy float_semantic_eq adjacency walk. The fuzzy walk is redundant for the final output: duplicated() buckets by EXACT to_bits, so fuzzy-near pairs are always kept; an exact-unique column yields all-rows-kept via both the shortcut clone AND the fallback's no-dup-marked clone. Lever: replace the whole thing with one exact-bits FxHashSet pass (no sort). Bit-identical output (dd_golden digest 6811f9fc38d507c5 over all-unique/exact-dup/fuzzy-near/+-0 cases). Labels: fp-frame perf no-gaps.
close_reason: KEPT 7.6x, beats pandas 4x. drop_duplicates all-valid-unique Float64 subset shortcut: replaced SipHash std::HashSet exact pre-check + O(n log n) sort_by(total_cmp) + fuzzy adjacency walk with ONE exact-bits FxHashSet pass (no sort). The fuzzy walk was redundant: duplicated() dedups by exact to_bits, so exact-unique => all rows kept by both shortcut clone AND fallback clone; fuzzy-near only changes which path yields the identical frame. dd_golden digest 6811f9fc38d507c5 unchanged over all-unique/exact-dup/fuzzy-near/+-0; 18 drop_duplicates + 11 duplicated + 61 unique tests pass; fmt+clippy clean. fp-bench drop_duplicates 100k 10-col 3.64->0.48ms=7.6x (pandas 1.89ms). Proof: tests/artifacts/perf/proof_edi9i_drop_duplicates_exact_fxhashset.md

title: Enforce asupersync recovery deadlines before retry attempts

Fix by fail-closing expired recovery plans before each attempt, preserving deadline_unix_ms=0 as no-deadline compatibility, and add fp-runtime tests proving expired plans perform zero transport attempts while active/no-deadline plans keep existing behavior.
close_reason: Enforced RecoveryPlan.deadline_unix_ms in recover_once before every attempt while preserving 0 as no-deadline; added feature-on fp-runtime tests for expired zero-attempt plans and future-deadline retry budgets.

Gates: golden dump of keys()/groups()/transform output before==after sha256, fp-frame+fp-groupby suites, groupby conformance proptests, alternating A/B + hyperfine, Score>=2.0. NOTE fp-frame/fp-columnar UNRESERVED (cod panes idle); check br ready for collisions first.

title: [parity] resample missing B (business-day), 2W (multi-week anchored), and empty-bin filling
description: Follow-up after 2sxy7 added resample('W'). Still missing vs pandas: (1) B (business-day) resample — rejected; needs business-day bin labels AND empty-bin filling (pandas fills 0.0/NaN for dataless business days). Fallback for duplicates, unsorted keys, nulls, non-Int64, multi-key, sort/indicator/validate, and all other semantics.

Retain only Score>=2.0.

After RCH release-perf fast mean/p50/p95/p99 1.541/0.601/3.730/4.135 ms vs forced generic 8.003/6.512/11.611/12.981 ms; checksum unchanged 7499393340.000. title: [parity] Series.nlargest/nsmallest missing keep= param (first/last/all)
description: FP Series::nlargest(n)/nsmallest(n) have NO keep parameter; pandas supports keep='first'(default)/'last'/'all'. first=value-order, ties earliest pos; last=ties latest pos; all=include ALL ties at the n-th boundary value (result may exceed n). Verified vs live pandas 2.2.3. Add nlargest_keep/nsmallest_keep additively (bare fns = keep=first, untouched fast path). fp-frame Series.
close_reason: Already implemented. Series::nlargest_keep(n, keep:&str)/nsmallest_keep (lib.rs ~11848/11855 via nlargest_smallest_impl) + DataFrame::nlargest_keep/nsmallest_keep(n,col,keep) (~42570/42647) all support first/last/all with correct semantics (last=ties latest pos via b.0.cmp(a.0); all=retain values not-past the n-th threshold via semantic_cmp, may exceed n). Bare nlargest(n)/nsmallest(n) are the keep=first convenience wrappers. Misframed as missing.

title: [perf][bench] fp has higher fixed per-op overhead than pandas at small N — bench at 1M, most 100k 'gaps' are phantom
description: PROFILE-BACKED (2026-06-15 vs_pandas sweep, float64, release-perf). The default bench size 100k produces PHANTOM perf gaps that are fixed-per-operation overhead (allocation, thread-pool spawn, Arc/index setup, Column::from_values), NOT algorithmic. They INVERT at 1M (fp wins). Evidence (ratio@100k -> ratio@1M, <1 = fp faster): value_counts 2.01x -> 0.59x; reindex 1.36x -> 0.20x; rolling_std 1.38x -> 0.56x; ewm_mean 1.37x -> 0.67x; groupby_mean 0.94x -> 0.68x. At 1M fp beats pandas on EVERYTHING except groupby_agg_multi (1.36x, see fused-agg bead). cumsum 0.05x (20x), drop_duplicates 0.14x, reindex 0.20x, sort_multi 0.21x. IMPLICATIONS: (1) the swarm has been chasing 100k phantom gaps (this agent nearly shipped a value_counts radix rewrite for a gap that doesn't exist at scale; the iloc 12x 'gap' f597a3c6 was also a 100k+methodology artifact). (2) Make the harness default sizes include 1M (benches/vs_pandas_harness.py --sizes default, BENCH_MATRIX_SPEC), or at minimum report BOTH and weight by 1M for the perf-frontier decision. (3) Separately, reducing fp's fixed per-op overhead (lazy thread-pool, skip Column::from_values re-scan on already-typed output, arena small-result alloc) is a real BROAD small-N lever worth its own profile — but it is NOT an algorithmic gap and small per-op wins should not be filed as vs-pandas 'gaps' off 100k numbers.
close_reason: Completed: vs-pandas harness default sizes now include 1M (10k,100k,1M), usage text corrected to --sizes, and join matrix rows for inner/left/outer now include 1M. Proof artifacts lavender_fa3um_*; py_compile PASS; help/default proof SHA recorded; Score 4.00.

title: [perf] Cache-blocked read-once GEMM for df.corr()/df.cov() to clear 2x (memory-bandwidth-bound after parallelization)
description: After 77157629 parallelized the corr/cov Gram (~1.48x, bit-identical, goldens 6f577ab8/beb4e3f5), df_corr/df_cov are MEMORY-BANDWIDTH-bound: output-partitioned parallel tiles re-stream column data from DRAM. FMA disproven (jawxr), packed-transpose regressed (extra traffic). Next lever for >=2x: a cache-blocked GEMM that streams X through L2/L3 ONCE per output block (row-chunk the r-loop with a persistent per-cell accumulator carried in ascending r order so the fold stays bit-identical), reducing DRAM re-reads. Constraint: per-cell fold must stay r-ascending (bit-identical to goldens). Target: 2-3x corr/cov on wide frames. Baseline: df_corr/df_cov 154ms->104ms parallel at n=200000x64.
notes: FOLLOW-UP (BlackThrush): band-local row-major TRANSPOSE lever tested on top of 5e4970aa — bit-identical (goldens d7d4c835/f3766dad UNCHANGED) but NEUTRAL (~87ms vs ~85ms). The strided col_data[c][r] access is NOT the bottleneck; transpose overhead cancels any locality gain. CONCLUSION: corr/cov Gram is at ~23 GFLOP/s / ~85ms (1.8x over the 154ms original), the practical ceiling for both bit-identical AND reassociated row-partitioned approaches on this box. Rejected levers (all): FMA (latency-bound), packed-tile (transpose traffic), MR4xNR8 widen (register/neutral), band-transpose (neutral). To clear a hard 2.0x+ would need a FUNDAMENTALLY different numerical formulation (Strassen-class recursion, mixed-precision accumulation, or a randomized/sketched covariance estimator) — NOT another tiling of the same O(n*m^2) Gram loop. Filing as the next-swing candidate; current 1.8x communication-avoiding GEMM shipped and stable.
close_reason: Kept borrowed Float64 corr/cov input columns: pairwise_numeric_column_values now returns Cow<[f64]> and borrows contiguous Float64 buffers instead of cloning them, while coerced/null-capable paths stay owned. Golden SHA unchanged vs current origin/main 1046942f: df_corr 5000 d7d4c83538939ca2d83ecc13d0f0376d90cd4f9176eaa9009a126e058231cb07 and df_cov 5000 f3766dadd4b2d36dda67aa9690a0fc3a17708fa8001fcee0c9ccb1895a452e89 with cmp identical. Same-host vmi1227854 A/B against 1046942f: df_corr 200000x3 381.4ms +/-40.9 -> 267.6ms +/-16.9 (1.43x +/-0.18); df_cov 415.5ms +/-34.6 -> 297.3ms +/-44.0 (1.40x +/-0.24). Gates: RCH cargo check -p fp-frame --all-targets pass; cargo fmt --check -p fp-frame pass; focused corr/cov tests pass. Clippy blocker tracked in br-frankenpandas-scowx; UBS broad inventory tracked in br-frankenpandas-yavyk; workspace fmt drift remains unrelated outside this pass.

title: [profiling] Series::between dispatches inclusive string match per element instead of once per call
description:
Skill: /profiling-software-performance.

fp-frame Series::between (lib.rs:5593) validates inclusive string at the
top, then re-matches the same string for every value in the closure body:
  match inclusive {
      "both" => ...,
      "neither" => ...,
      "left" => ...,
      "right" => ...,
      _ => unreachable!(),
  }

For a Series of N elements, this runs N str-equality dispatches when the
mode is fixed for the entire call. Each is short but constant work
multiplied across the loop adds up on filtering hot paths.

Fix: define a small enum InclusiveMode { Both, Neither, Left, Right };
resolve  to a mode once before the loop; match the enum
(jump-table) inside the closure. Same micro-opt shape as br-78d0c
(Vec::iter().position to HashMap) but here it is dispatch hoisting.

title: [perf] fp-frame::DataFrame::duplicated:28020 — per-row key alloc + slice hashmap; drop_duplicates 21.74ms vs pandas 5.66ms (3.8x)
description:
MEASUREMENT BY RubyGoose profiling pass 2026-06-01. See tests/artifacts/perf/HOTSPOT_TABLE.md.

SCENARIO: DataFrame::drop_duplicates(None, First, false), 100k rows x 2 cols (Int64 key 100 groups, unique Float64 value -> all rows kept).

BASELINE (100k, 20 hyperfine runs, release-perf + frame pointers):
- FP drop_duplicates: 21.74 ms/iter (sigma 0.94); alloc-heavy (User 599ms / Sys 270ms)
- pandas 2.2.3: p50 5.66ms, p95 7.68ms => 3.8x slower.

NOTE: the 2026-05-25 PROFILING_REPORT.md '229x / >7000ms timeout CRITICAL' is STALE — already fixed; scaling 10k->100k is ~linear (2.3->21.7ms). Do not chase the old number.

PROFILER EVIDENCE (samply, symbolicated; samply_drop_duplicates.json):
- ~10% self  fp_frame::DataFrame::take_rows_by_positions_unchecked (materialization; see br-frankenpandas-2a6ln)
- ~10% self  hashbrown HashMap<&[u64],usize>::insert/get
- ~8% self   Vec<fp_index::IndexLabel>::clone
- 5.1% self  libc memcpy (0x12ac5b)
- 2.7% self  fp_frame::DataFrame::duplicated

ROOT CAUSE: duplicated() builds a Vec<u64> hash key PER ROW (n heap allocations), keys a HashMap<&[u64],usize> (slice hashing/probing), and looks up each column via BTreeMap<String,Column> INSIDE the row x col loop. Sys 270ms = alloc churn confirms.

HYPOTHESIZED FIX (H4): (1) hoist column references out of the per-row loop (resolve &Column once); (2) replace per-row Vec<u64> + slice-keyed map with a single packed key (e.g. one u64/u128 via FxHash over the row's cells) into a HashMap<u64, ...> with explicit equality fallback, or a flat row-key arena; (3) reuse the materialization fast-path from br-frankenpandas-2a6ln for the final take. Keep semantic null/NaN equality intact (regression tests exist).

DO NOT optimize yet without re-baselining against HOTSPOT_TABLE.md.
close_reason: Completed: replaced duplicated row Vec key materialization with digest buckets. rch hyperfine drop_duplicates 100000x40: 682.84 ms mean before to 602.92 ms after (11.70% faster); golden sha256 unchanged 64d27a6b11527d27b9c08530971eab1a3b758531a5bf4dd94f6968082d1d0292.

title: [correctness?] Does DataFrame.corr/cov diverge from numpy/pandas at large n (naive vs pairwise summation)?
description: Surfaced while optimizing the corr/cov Gram (bh7iy). FP computes sum_xy/sum_x2 etc. with a naive left-to-right f64 fold (separate mul+add, fixed order). numpy/pandas use PAIRWISE (blocked) summation + FMA for reductions, which has different (smaller) rounding error. For large n (e.g. 1e6) the naive fold's accumulated error can exceed the conformance tolerance vs live pandas, i.e. FP's corr/cov may be LESS accurate / divergent from pandas — a possible parity bug hiding behind a tolerance gate or behind goldens that codified FP's own naive result. CHECK (differential vs live pandas 2.2.3, /dp/frankenpandas/legacy_pandas_code/pandas): build a 1e6-row x several-col frame with mixed magnitudes (incl. near-cancellation), compare FP df.corr()/df.cov() against pandas cell-by-cell; report max abs/rel error and whether it exceeds the conformance tolerance. Also confirm whether FP's existing corr/cov goldens were generated from FP itself (codifying naive) or from pandas. WHY IT MATTERS TWICE: (1) if FP diverges beyond tolerance it is a CORRECTNESS fix (switch the reduction to pairwise/compensated, matching numpy); (2) that same change REMOVES the bit-exact-naive-fold constraint that currently caps the Gram kernel at ~2 GFLOP/s (bh7iy), unlocking an FMA + cache-blocked Gram at ~5-10x. So a correctness alignment to numpy-pairwise is the key that also unblocks the perf. Decide pairwise-vs-naive parity contract here, then bh7iy becomes implementable.
close_reason: RESOLVED, no bug. Differential FP DataFrame.corr() vs live pandas 2.2.3 on identical splitmix input, n=1,000,000 x 8 cols: max_abs_diff 1.10e-16, max_rel_diff 1.29e-11 — FP matches pandas to ~machine-eps, FAR inside the 1e-10 conformance tolerance. So (1) NO correctness divergence — FP's naive left-to-right fold is as accurate as pandas here; (2) parity is tolerance-based and FP is already ~1e-16 off pandas (not bit-exact), so an FMA/reassociated Gram is PERMITTED parity-wise. Tooling committed: crates/fp-frame/examples/corr_parity_dump.rs + tests/artifacts/perf/corr_parity_check.py (reusable FP-vs-pandas numerical differential). Perf implication recorded on bh7iy.

title: Add DatetimeIndex month_name/day_name/normalize/is_normalized accessors
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): DatetimeIndex still missing locale-stable string accessors and the normalize/is_normalized pair. Mirrors the day-of-X (br-k860x) and boundary (br-qy7yd) patterns.

Add four pandas-named methods on DatetimeIndex:
- month_name() -> Vec<Option<String>> — full English month name (January..December). Pandas defaults to None locale (English).
- day_name() -> Vec<Option<String>> — full English weekday name (Monday..Sunday).
- normalize() -> Self — return a new DatetimeIndex with each timestamp truncated to midnight UTC (subsecond + time-of-day zeroed).
- is_normalized -> bool — true when every label is at 00:00:00.000000000 (or NAT).

Single PR. Add regression tests covering January/December name formatting, Monday/Sunday name formatting, normalize zeroing time-of-day while preserving the date and NAT, and is_normalized returning false for non-midnight, true for midnight, and true for empty/all-NAT.
close_reason: Shipped in cb1b2b3: DatetimeIndex now exposes pandas-parity month_name/day_name/normalize/is_normalized. 3 regression tests pass; 264 fp-index tests total.

title: [perf] Column-parallel frame-vs-frame arithmetic (binary_df_op)
description: gap_hunt add_df/sub_df/mul_df/div_df ~8ms each: binary_df_op same-index fast path loops column_order SERIALLY (typed f64/i64 + Scalar arms per column). Routed through par_map_columns (+Sync bound; ops |a,b| a+b etc are non-capturing => Sync). Covers add/sub/mul/div/floordiv/mod. Bit-identical (identical per-col arms, reassembled in column_order). add_df 5.6x, sub 5.5x, mul 5.7x, div 6.1x, i64.add_df 5.5x. golden 79c69469 unchanged. 19 unit + conformance green. NOTE: only the same-index FAST path parallelized; the rarer aligned (differing-index) path stays serial.
notes: DONE (9ecd939d): binary_df_op same-index fast path -> par_map_columns (+Sync). add/sub/mul/div/floordiv/mod. add_df 5.6x/sub 5.5x/mul 5.7x/div 6.1x/i64.add_df 5.5x at 200k x8. bit-identical golden 79c69469. 19 unit + conformance green. Aligned (differing-index) path still serial (rare).
close_reason: done

title: fp-frame Series::*_skipna(false) returns NaN instead of NaT for Timedelta64

title: [perf] parallel per-column build in merge_asof output (build_asof_output)
description: build_asof_output (fp-join, behind merge_asof/merge_asof_with_options + grouped) builds every output column serially: left columns via col.clone(), right columns via a per-row right_col.values()[j].clone() Scalar gather + Column::new revalidation. Output is left.len() rows x all columns; for wide frames the per-column materialization dominates. Columns are independent => parallelize across a std::thread::scope pool (proven pattern: inner-merge j3jnd, CSV merge e595e700), one column per task, disjoint slots, insert in order. Bit-identical by construction (identical per-column output, concurrent build; right NaN/null semantics unchanged — a typed reindex_by_positions is NOT safe here: Int64 would flip Null(NaN)->Null(Null) and matched genuine-NaN Float64 would flip to Null under the LazyNullable convention). Gate output_len>=1<<18 + ncols>1 + threads>1. Golden sha256 on a wide-Float64 asof join; Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Shipped fd835cbe: parallel per-column build in build_asof_output (merge_asof). Bit-identical golden (wide-f64 asof, before==after a88823e3). n=1M 1+8 Float64 cols: 200.8->79.0 ms/iter = 2.54x. 102 fp-join tests green.

title: Add Period/Range Index min/max + Range Index sum/mean closed-form reductions
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): PeriodIndex/RangeIndex still miss the basic ordering / arithmetic reductions.

Add four methods:
- PeriodIndex.min() -> Result<Option<Period>, IndexError> — minimum period by ordinal; rejects on mixed-frequency input.
- PeriodIndex.max() -> Result<Option<Period>, IndexError> — maximum.
- RangeIndex.min() / max() -> Option<i64> — closed form on (start, stop, step, len). Empty returns None. Negative step flips the end choice.
- RangeIndex.sum() / mean() -> closed-form via arithmetic-progression formula n * (first + last) / 2; mean returns Option<f64>.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: ascending Period min/max, mixed-freq rejection, RangeIndex with positive step / negative step / empty, and arithmetic-progression sum/mean for known cases (e.g. 1..=10 step 1 sum=55, mean=5.5).
close_reason: Shipped in 68c47b4: PeriodIndex now exposes pandas-parity min/max with mixed-freq rejection; RangeIndex now exposes pandas-parity min/max/sum/mean via O(1) closed-form arithmetic. 2 regression tests pass; 295 fp-index tests total.

Parent umbrella: frankenpandas-nt65g.

Replace with O(n) global radix-argsort + CSR scatter per-column kernel; parallelize independent value columns. Target >=2x, bit-identical.

Removed per-group O(num_groups*range) memset + range sweep.

title: [perf] Typed composite-key digest + equality for DataFrame.duplicated/drop_duplicates (Scalar-path ~92/55ms)
description: eydcr family, BIGGEST remaining (drop_duplicates 92ms, duplicated 55ms @200kx8 all-Float64). Hot: rows_equal_on_subset (13%, semantic_eq over materialized Scalars), row_digest closure (col.values()[row] Scalar per col per row), Column::new, and subset_has_all_valid_unique_column SORTS each f64 col (7%). LEVER: pre-extract per-column typed accessors (as_f64_slice_with_validity / as_i64_slice / as_bool) once; build row_digest + rows_equal from typed buffers (digest hash value need NOT match old mix_digest — output is determined by rows_equal verification, so any consistent typed hash works); also skip/cheapen subset_has_all_valid_unique_column. RISK: replicate Scalar::semantic_eq exactly for Float64 (NaN==NaN true for dedup, -0.0) + missing equality + all 3 keep modes. Bigger/riskier than the elementwise levers — dedicated effort. Target ~5-10x both.
close_reason: Shipped 88f00402: typed dedup — (1) duplicated typed digest+rows_equal from (f64,validity) slices [present=valid&&!NaN, identical mix_digest bytes so collision behavior matches, fuzzy float_semantic_eq]; (2) subset_has_all_valid_unique_column typed exact-dup pre-check (skip O(n log n) sort). drop_duplicates 92->29.8ms = 3.08x, duplicated 55->27.7ms ~2.0x, bit-identical (golden 50235fec). fp-frame 2830 + fp-conformance 2047 green. eydcr: 8 ops DONE; only fillna 34ms left (trivial).

title: [perf] dense all-matched Int64 outer fused output builder
description: Profile-backed continuation after br-frankenpandas-uza04.33. Fresh perf stack tests/artifacts/perf/perf_report_default_outer_after_uza04_33.txt on default outer join-bench shows residual in take_positions_typed (~29% children) and Column drop/free pressure (~24%) after many-to-many validation routing. Active perf beads are claimed outside fp-join; this claims a disjoint fp-join lever. One deeper safe-Rust lever only: for single-key all-valid Int64 outer joins where every emitted dense key bucket has both left and right rows, fuse dense row-map generation with typed output column construction, avoiding Option position vectors and repeated take_positions_typed gathers. Preserve sorted dense key order, duplicate tie-breaking (left bucket insertion order then right bucket insertion order), same-name key coalescing from left key values, no unmatched fallback changes (return None unless all nonempty buckets are matched), dtype/FP bits/RNG unchanged. Baseline/golden via RCH before edit; paired RCH rebench; crate-scoped fp-join tests/check/clippy/fmt/UBS; keep only Score>=2.0 and reprofile.
close_reason: Completed: fused dense all-matched Int64 outer output builder. Profile target after uza04.33: default outer take_positions_typed/drop residual. Baseline 797.6 ms +/-17.6; paired 796.0 -> 742.3 ms; confirmation 783.8 -> 665.2 ms; reprofile default outer 686.1 ms. Golden sha256 unchanged aef86306d079c31d85e74569a6be40c0f79e85d752ad16b3857feafc9ed3c952; byte-identical before/after. Isomorphism: dense key order, duplicate ties, left shared-key source, fallback, FP/RNG preserved. Score 2.55.
