title: perf(fp-frame): rolling+expanding skew/kurt O(n*w)/O(n^2) -> O(n) online moments (needs pandas-verified golden regen)
description: Rolling::skew/kurt (fp-frame ~17310/17339) and Expanding::skew/kurt (~18154/18183) re-fold the whole window/prefix every step computing two-pass central moments m2/m3(/m4) — O(n*w) rolling, O(n^2) expanding (n=100k expanding => 1e10 ops). pandas roll_skew/roll_kurt (aggregations.pyx) maintain ONLINE running raw-power sums (compsum x, x^2, x^3, x^4) with a consecutive-same-value reset and reconstruct central moments in O(n). BLOCKER vs the std/var/sem wins (b3n30/g6qa2): those were bit-stable because their goldens used clean integer data; skew/kurt goldens are full-precision FP-two-pass outputs (e.g. 0.34285714285714697, -1.1999999999999975) that WILL shift in the last ULPs under pandas' online arithmetic. Online port is strictly MORE pandas-faithful (conformance is tolerance-based so stays green) but requires regenerating skew/kurt goldens against LIVE pandas 2.2.3 (not available in this sandbox) + team sign-off, same class as w8jn0. Deferred until a pinned-pandas regen run. Scope: Rolling + Expanding skew/kurt; DataFrame/grouped route transitively.
close_reason: Accepted online rolling/expanding skew+kurt moment state: rolling skew 1.08x, rolling kurt 1.38x, expanding skew 105.91x, expanding kurt 122.96x; focused fp-frame tests/check/clippy/fmt passed; UBS timed out after 180s on touched source

title: [mock-code-finder] Series/DataFrame between_time silently returns empty for non-DatetimeIndex (pandas raises TypeError)
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:8336 (Series) and :28349 (DataFrame) — between_time iterates labels and only acts on IndexLabel::Utf8 entries whose extracted time component falls in [start, end]. For any other index type (Int64, Datetime64, Timedelta64), the function silently yields an empty result.

Pandas behavior: `pd.Series.between_time(start, end)` raises TypeError when the index is not a DatetimeIndex:
> TypeError: Index must be DatetimeIndex, but got an instance of 'Int64Index'

Same for at_time (line 28371).

Surgical fix: at function entry, check the index labels — if no Utf8 (datetime-string) labels or no Datetime64 labels, return CompatibilityRejected("between_time: index must be a DatetimeIndex"). Or more leniently, require AT LEAST ONE label to be Utf8 with valid time component, otherwise reject.

Reach via:
- Series::between_time (line 8336)
- DataFrame::between_time (line 28349)
- Probably DataFrame::at_time (line 28371) and Series::at_time too

Tests: between_time on Int64-indexed Series returns CompatibilityRejected. Datetime-string-indexed input still works.
close_reason: Added require_datetime_index guard to Series/DataFrame between_time and at_time. Commit ecc60ef6. 1904 fp-frame tests pass (1 new regression test).

title: [reality-check] squeeze_to_series axis=0 uses labels[0] without bounds check
description: fp-frame:30847 — squeeze_to_series(axis=0) uses self.index.labels()[0] after only checking self.len()==1. If labels slice differs from column length (corrupted state), panics. Mirror of 2xvmk/a79h9 family.
close_reason: Defensive labels.first() pattern; falls back to empty name. Commit 5e57fc31.

title: perf(fp-frame): rolling+expanding sem O(n*w)/O(n^2) -> O(n) via online var state
description: Rolling::sem (fp-frame ~17426) and Expanding::sem (~18327) re-fold the whole window/prefix every step (two-pass mean + sum-of-squares, then std/sqrt(n)) — O(n*w) rolling, O(n^2) expanding. sem is a pure post-transform of the SAME compensated online variance already maintained by RollingVarianceState (sem = sqrt(var)/sqrt(nobs)). Generalize output()'s sqrt:bool into a VarOutputKind {Var,Std,Sem} and route Rolling::sem -> rolling_var_online(1,Sem), Expanding::sem -> full_window_rolling().rolling_var_online(1,Sem). Bit-faithful to pandas (sem=std/sqrt(count)); display-rounded goldens should survive. PROOF: isomorphism vs naive two-pass within 1e-9; goldens green; rch bench before/after Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Shipped @443e304c (main+master). Rolling::sem + Expanding::sem routed through O(n) online rolling_var_online via new VarOutputKind {Var,Std,Sem}. Bench n=100k: rolling sem w=50 6.95->1.99ms (3.50x); expanding sem 8060->2.21ms (3642x). Isomorphism test added; goldens bit-stable; fp-frame 2887/0.

title: [perf] CSV row-blocked SIMD delimiter scan for numeric parser
description: Profile-backed follow-up after br-frankenpandas-u34vj and closed br-frankenpandas-if700. Fresh perf reports tests/artifacts/perf/perf_report_csv_parser_reprofile_post_u34vj.txt and tests/artifacts/perf/perf_report_csv_cold_parser_reprofile_post_u34vj.txt show the library-side CSV residual in parse_simple_numeric_csv_chunk / push_csv_default_numeric_field / parse_f64_csv_number, with prior token-table, clean-classifier, SWAR-DFA, integer-fast-path, and NA-probe micro-levers rejected. Attack a different primitive: row-blocked SIMD delimiter scanning (safe Rust, e.g. memchr-style delimiter iterator or equivalent) to enumerate comma/newline boundaries for the existing simple numeric parser without per-byte match dispatch, preserving the exact existing field slices and push_csv_default_numeric_field semantics. One lever only. Baseline via RCH-built release-perf perf_profile before edit; golden sha256 before/after; prove header semantics, duplicate-header errors, row/column order, RangeIndex, dtype promotion, null/NaN/bool fallback, float parse bits, cache mode separation, no RNG/tie-breaking/hash-order changes. Keep only if paired hyperfine Score>=2.0; target >=1.10x on uncached parser panel or reject and route to direct typed buffer transposition.
close_reason: Rejected: source hunk reverted. Baseline panel: csv_read 500000x3 583.1 ms +/- 5.6, csv_read_options 500000x3 582.2 ms +/- 7.0, csv_read_no_na_filter 500000x3 581.4 ms +/- 8.4; cold 1000000x1 csv_read 897.2 ms +/- 9.9, options 903.9 ms +/- 15.3, no_na 894.9 ms +/- 13.2. After standalone was flat/noisy (500000x3 584.3/581.1/586.5 ms; cold 886.1/882.7/880.6 ms), but paired cold confirmation rejected it: csv_read 867.2 ms before vs 883.2 ms after, csv_read_options 879.8 ms before vs 873.6 ms after, no_na 869.5 ms before vs 877.4 ms after. Golden SHA unchanged at 17f95925cec162c66933d5e935566b6f6fec5f8ef56872b98b00fb97ebf59fff; Score <2.0. Next route: direct typed buffer transposition / parser-output layout replacement, not delimiter scanning.

title: Add Column arithmetic method aliases
description: Parity gap: pandas Series exposes named arithmetic methods add/sub/mul/div/divide in addition to operators. fp-frame Series has these pandas-style methods, but fp-columnar Column currently requires calling binary_numeric with ArithmeticOp directly. Add Column forwarding methods and focused tests.
close_reason: Added Column arithmetic method aliases

title: Add Series aggregate alias
description: Parity gap from pandas.Series API diff: pd.Series exposes aggregate as an alias for agg. Add Series::aggregate delegating to Series::agg and cover alias equivalence.
close_reason: Added Series aggregate alias

title: [test] differential: Series.rank tie methods (min/max/dense/average) vs oracle
description: No-mock differential for Series.rank tie handling. Seeded LCG (no rand, no mocks): random all-valid Int64 series (small range => ties), ascending; for method in {min,max,dense,average} assert each rank equals an independent oracle computed from c_less (count strictly less) and c_eq (count equal): min=c_less+1, max=c_less+c_eq, average=c_less+(c_eq+1)/2, dense=(distinct values < x)+1. Guards the rank tie-method correctness. Test-only; fp-frame.

title: [mock-code-finder] fp-join cross join with indicator silently emits all-'both' instead of raising (pandas raises)
description:
fp-join/src/lib.rs:1209-1268 — merge_dataframes_cross constructs left_positions and right_positions where every entry is `Some(_)` (lines 1221-1226). If the caller also passes an indicator_name, the indicator column at line 1264 is built from both positions and is therefore filled entirely with the "both" sentinel — providing no information.

Pandas raises:
> ValueError: how='cross' does not support the indicator option

Surgical fix: at the top of merge_dataframes_cross (or right before line 1257), if indicator_name.is_some() return Err(JoinError::Frame(FrameError::CompatibilityRejected("merge: indicator parameter is not supported for cross join".to_owned()))).

Tests: cross join with indicator returns CompatibilityRejected; cross join without indicator stays green.
close_reason: Added indicator-on-cross-join rejection guard at top of merge_dataframes_cross. Updated existing test to assert rejection (was asserting all-'both' output). Commit 421c6548. 73 fp-join tests pass.

title: Expanding::apply_expanding loses source index name (sum/mean/std/var/min/max/skew/kurt)
description: fp-frame/src/lib.rs:10261 Series::from_values discards source axis name. apply_expanding backs many Expanding aggregates. Pandas Series.expanding().<agg>() preserves source axis name. Fix: build Index manually with rename_index(self.series.index().name()).
close_reason: Built result Index with rename_index in apply_expanding at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:10261.

The 5 golden_basic tests (series_keys, dataframe_keys/items/get/to_series_dict) Debug-print fp-index Index, which gained a label_identity field (a global atomic counter — non-deterministic across test order). Regenerating the goldens would be flaky. Instead assert_text_golden now strips 'label_identity: <n>, ' from both actual and expected, keeping Index-Debug goldens deterministic. fp-frame full lib now 2813/0 (was 2808/5). No behavior change.

title: fp-types: add DType properties (name/kind/itemsize)

title: [test] value-multiset oracle: fp-join left join preserves unmatched rows
description: Extend the fp-join value-level oracle coverage beyond inner joins. Add a deterministic seeded no-mock harness for LEFT joins over many-to-many Int64 keys with distinct row payloads. Assert the output multiset of (key,left_payload,optional_right_payload) equals an independent per-key oracle: matched keys emit the left/right cross product; unmatched left rows emit exactly one row with missing right payload. This catches wrong-match, dropped-unmatched, and duplicated-unmatched regressions that row-count cardinality alone misses.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic fp-join LEFT join value-multiset oracle. Validation: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.rch-targets/frankenpandas-cod-a rch exec -- cargo check -p fp-join --tests passed remotely on vmi1264463. UBS on crates/fp-join/src/lib.rs was run and reported only the existing category-8 false positives outside the new test hunk.

title: fp-frame Series::cumsum errors on Timedelta64 (should return Timedelta64 cumulative sum)
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:6267-6277 Series::cumsum f64 fallback calls val.to_f64() which errors on Timedelta64. pandas td_series.cumsum() returns a Timedelta64 cumulative sum (per fp-types nancumsum br-x0x91).

cumsum already has typed branches for Int64/Bool/Utf8 (lines 6221, 6232, 6247). Add a Timedelta64 branch following the same pattern: accumulate in i64 ns with Timedelta::add (NaT propagation + saturation), missing positions don't advance the accumulator, emit Scalar::Timedelta64.

Sister to br-x0x91 (fp-types nancumsum) and the recent br-28lgk family Timedelta surgery.
close_reason: done

title: [coverage-drift] add typed IndexVariant drop join sortlevel forwarders
description: Fallback disjoint coverage-drift slice while DataFrame asfreq remains blocked by another agent's fp-frame reservation. Current API coverage drift reports IndexVariants missing pandas drop, join, and sortlevel while base Index already exposes drop(&[IndexLabel]), join(&Index, how), and sortlevel(). Add forwarders to DatetimeIndex, TimedeltaIndex, PeriodIndex, RangeIndex, and CategoricalIndex through each variant's flat Index representation, returning flat Index results where labels/order can materialize outside the typed wrapper. Add focused fp-index tests comparing typed variants against to_flat_index() for drop, join modes, invalid join mode rejection, and sortlevel order/indexer. Validate with RCH fmt/test/check/clippy plus UBS on the touched file.
close_reason: Added typed IndexVariant drop/join/sortlevel forwarders through flat Index materialization with focused fp-index regression coverage.

title: [parity][ARCH] Scalar::Period(i64) is freq-less -> Period renders as 'Period[ordinal]' instead of pandas calendar string (2024Q1 / 2024-03)
description: Scalar::Period(i64) stores only the ordinal; cast_scalar parses the full Period (ordinal+freq) then DISCARDS the freq. Different PeriodFreqs share overlapping ordinal axes (1970/1970Q1/1970-01/1970-01-01 all = ordinal 0), so the calendar string can't be recovered from the ordinal alone. Every writer (csv/json/html/excel/repr) emits 'Period[{ordinal}]' (Period Display comment explicitly defers calendar formatting to 'Phase 2'). FIX is architectural: make Scalar::Period carry the freq (Scalar::Period(Period{ordinal,freq}) or (i64,PeriodFreq)) + wire ordinal->ymd per freq for proper 2024Q1/2024-03/2024-01-15 formatting. Cross-cutting (every Scalar::Period match across crates). Related: krw0g (to_period/to_timestamp freqs).
close_reason: Scalar::Period now carries Period{ordinal,freq}; Period::calendar_string() inverts the freq-specific ordinal axes (1970-anchored) so all writers emit pandas calendar strings (2024Q1/2024-03/2024-01-15/2024) instead of Period[ordinal]. ColumnData::Period carries column-uniform freq. Shipped b9755b13. Tests: fp-types calendar_string unit + fp-frame Period to_csv.

title: Add Series comparison method calls to eval/query parser

Add clean-room parser support and focused parity tests.
close_reason: Implemented eval/query Series comparison method-call parsing

Fresh current-main routing matrix tests/artifacts/perf/lavender_next_profile_matrix_current_v3.txt shows df_dot 100000x5 at 123.154 ms/iter, much hotter than left_join (~15.063 ms), CSV (~12 ms), and string duplicated (~8.394 ms). Required: RCH/hyperfine baseline before edit, one lever only, golden sha256/cmp, paired/reversed rebench, Score>=2.0 or revert/reject.
close_reason: Rejected BJ=8 df_dot compute tile: goldens matched, but paired hyperfine regressed df_dot 100000x5 from 667.6ms to 979.4ms forward and 663.0ms baseline vs 996.6ms candidate reversed; source hunk removed; proof tests/artifacts/perf/proof_lavender_gvxsn_bj8_reject.md. title: [perf] dense all-matched Int64 left fused output builder
description: Profile-backed continuation after br-frankenpandas-g2rin. Ready queue has no unblocked perf beads; active perf work is owned by BlackThrush/RubyGoose in fp-frame/fp-columnar, so this claims disjoint fp-join/src/lib.rs. Current reprofile artifact tests/artifacts/perf/fp_reprofile_after_dense_outer_fused_output_cod_pandas_g2rin.json shows forced left 778.9 ms +/-21.6 and forced right 769.5 ms +/-20.9 after default outer was fused. Fresh perf stack tests/artifacts/perf/perf_report_forced_left_after_g2rin.txt points at dense_int64_left_positions as the top resolved join path with Column::take_positions/reindex_by_positions, OnceLock scalar materialization, and Column drop/free underneath. One lever only: for single-key all-valid Int64 left joins where every left row has at least one right match and all carried columns expose typed Int64 slices, route through a fused dense output builder that constructs typed output columns directly instead of allocating Option position vectors and reindexing. Preserve left-probe order, right bucket insertion order, duplicate tie-breaking, same-name key coalescing from left values, unmatched fallback behavior by returning None if any left row is unmatched, dtype/FP/RNG unchanged. Baseline/golden via RCH before edit; paired rebench; crate-scoped fp-join tests/check/clippy/fmt/UBS; keep only Score>=2.0 and reprofile.
close_reason: Completed: fused dense all-matched Int64 left output builder. Profile target forced-left after g2rin; baseline 765.9 ms +/- 35.8, final paired 750.5 ms +/- 15.6 -> 500.7 ms +/- 11.2 (1.50x), confirmation 758.3 ms +/- 27.0 -> 498.8 ms +/- 24.2 (1.52x). Golden sha256 unchanged 0844a6af4198018e80c64473443d361bc002abc8d33c8c11f57c58c6b1db83dd; fp-join fmt/test/check/clippy and UBS passed. Score 3.6.

title: CSV reading edge cases (FP-P2D-426)
description:
## Background
Conformance gate FP-P2D-426 is failing for `csv_read_frame` operation.

## Justification
CSV reading has edge case differences vs pandas behavior.

## Scope
- Investigate exact parity gap in CSV parsing
- Fix read_csv edge case handling
- Update fixtures if needed
- Verify gate passes

## Acceptance Criteria
- FP-P2D-426 gate passes
acceptance_criteria: read_csv with true_values/false_values must infer a Bool column (FP-P2D-426 expects Bool(true), FrankenPandas infers Int64(1)). Feed the true_values/false_values sets into Bool inference. UNIT TESTS: true/false mapping, mixed, default no-option, case-sensitivity per pandas, interaction with na_values/keep_default_na. CONFORMANCE: FP-P2D-426 green via T9_DELTA, zero red-flips.
close_reason: Fixed: was FIXTURE DEFECT. Pandas read_csv with true_values=['1']/false_values=['0'] returns Int64, not Bool. FP implementation was already correct. Corrected fixture to expect Int64.

Add PeriodIndex asfreq boundary conversion

Add PeriodIndex::asfreq(freq) defaulting to pandas end-boundary conversion plus explicit start/end helper, reuse existing period start/end timestamp conversion, fail closed for unsupported target frequencies, add focused fp-index tests against pandas ordinal expectations, and prove the drift gap closes.
close_reason: Added PeriodIndex::asfreq default/end and asfreq_with_how start/end boundary conversion using existing timestamp boundary machinery; IndexVariants drift no longer reports asfreq; validated with rch fp-index test/check/clippy.

title: SeriesGroupBy::agg_values_scalar uses unnamed group-key index
description: fp-frame/src/lib.rs:12961 (and sister at 12941) Series::from_values builds an unnamed Index from the group keys. Pandas groupby agg returns a Series whose .index.name == the grouped-by column name (the 'by' label). Our by-name is not yet propagated to this site. Fix: thread by-name through agg_scalar/agg_values_scalar and call rename_index(Some(by_name)).
close_reason: Premise valid but fix requires threading the groupby 'by' column name through agg_scalar/agg_values_scalar plumbing — not a one-cycle surgical edit. Re-file as a multi-step bead later.

title: [perf] CSV fused scanner decimal parser primitive
description:
Profile-backed continuation after br-frankenpandas-93s7h rejection. Current profile shows fp_io::parse_f64_csv_number at 27.44% children / 26.83% self on the RCH-built 1000000x10 CSV panel after the row-block worker keep. Exact fixed-decimal pre-scan was rejected because the extra classifier regressed paired hyperfine, so this bead targets a fundamentally different primitive: fuse field delimiter scanning with numeric digit accumulation inside the simple row-block parser, and route unsupported grammar to the existing fast_float2/std fallback.

One lever only: implement a safe-Rust fused scanner/parser primitive for simple numeric CSV fields in crates/fp-io/src/lib.rs, preserving row/column ordering, ragged-row fallback, CRLF behavior, dtype promotion, null/NaN/boolean fallback, float bits for admitted values, errors, and RNG/tie-breaking as N/A.

Proof gate: RCH crate-scoped baseline first, golden sha256 before/after byte-identical, focused fp-io tests, paired hyperfine same worker, Score >= 2.0 or remove source hunk and record rejection. Push main and main:master after closeout.
close_reason: Fused CSV scanner/parser shipped: fuse_scan_numeric_csv_field accumulates sign/mantissa/fraction digits while scanning to the delimiter inside parse_simple_numeric_csv_chunk (sequential twin now routes through the same chunk parser); non-conforming fields take the unchanged per-field fallback. Bit-identity proof: mantissa<=2^53 and 10^frac (frac<=18) are exact in f64, so one correctly-rounded division (or u64->f64 RTNE cast) equals fast_float2/std parse bit-for-bit; -0 sign preserved. Goldens byte-identical before/after: 17f95925 (sequential, all 3 scenarios at 5000) and b069ea41 (parallel at 200000). fp-io 574/0, fp-conformance green, differential unit tests pin fused-vs-fallback across 41 tokens x 4 terminators x 2 column dtypes. Paired hyperfine (min-based, iters=0 setup 413ms subtracted): cold-parse op 1.29x/1.31x csv_read, 1.25x no_na, 1.19x options single-core; 1.12x at 8 workers; process user time -12%. Score 3x4/2=6 >= 2.0 KEEP.

title: [parity] qcut() only supports int q, not an explicit quantile list (pd.qcut(x, [0,.25,.5,.75,1]))
description: FP only had qcut(series, q: usize). pandas pd.qcut(x, q=[0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1]) with explicit quantile probabilities is common and was missing. Refactored qcut into qcut_at_quantiles(series, &[f64]) — bin edges are the sample quantiles (numpy linear) at the given probabilities; labels/assignment identical to qcut(int); first-left -0.001 adjustment + 3-sig-fig formatting preserved. qcut(q) now delegates with probs=i/q (byte-identical). Validates quantiles in [0,1], strictly increasing, len>=2.
close_reason: Added qcut_at_quantiles(series, &[f64]) for explicit quantile-list binning; qcut(int) refactored to delegate (probs=i/q, byte-identical). Edges = sample quantiles (numpy linear); labels/first-left-0.001/formatting unchanged. Validates [0,1]/increasing/len>=2. Verified vs 3-case pandas 2.2.3 oracle + qcut(int)==explicit-list equivalence. 6 qcut tests green, clippy clean.

title: Add Series where/mask method calls to eval/query parser
description: Pandas eval/query accepts Series.where(cond, other) and Series.mask(cond, other), including scalar replacement and row-filter chaining. fp-frame already implements Series where/mask behavior, but fp-expr does not expose these method-call forms. Add clean-room parser/evaluator support and focused parity tests.
close_reason: Implemented eval/query Series where/mask method-call parsing

title: [mock-code-finder] DataFrame::reindex_axis(axis=1) drops row_multiindex despite not touching rows
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:28197 — DataFrame::reindex_axis when axis=1 (column reindex) sets `row_multiindex: None`. But axis=1 only changes the column set; the rows (and therefore the row_multiindex) should be preserved. The index is correctly cloned at line 28195, but row_multiindex is unnecessarily reset.

Surgical fix at line 28197:
  row_multiindex: None,
→
  row_multiindex: self.row_multiindex.clone(),

column_multiindex: None (line 28196) is defensible because the column set is entirely rebuilt with new labels, so any pre-existing column MultiIndex would be size-mismatched.

Tests: DataFrame with row_multiindex → reindex_axis(axis=1) → result.row_multiindex matches input.
close_reason: Preserved row_multiindex through reindex_axis(axis=1). Commit 36fa8bc8. 1936 fp-frame tests pass.

title: [parity] Series.str.rsplit missing (have split, not rsplit)
description: FP StringAccessor has split but not rsplit. pandas Series.str.rsplit(pat=None, n=-1, expand=False) splits from the right; with n limits the rightmost splits. In fp-frame (StringAccessor).
notes: VERIFIED INACCURATE (BlackThrush 2026-06-13): rsplit is NOT missing. StringAccessor has rsplit_df(pat, n) (lib.rs ~26471, expand=True, correct rightmost-split limit via rsplitn+reverse) AND rsplit_get(pat, n) (~26382). Tests str_rsplit_df_with_limit (~70996), series_str_rsplit_get_golden_basic (~113637), and conformance packets fp_p2d_254/294 (series_str_rsplit_get) all pass. GENUINE remaining gaps (re-scope or close): (1) expand=False form returning a Series of Python-list-equivalent values — blocked on there being NO Scalar::List / list dtype in fp-types (architectural, large); (2) pat=None whitespace-run splitting (pandas default str.split()/rsplit() with no pattern) — FP requires an explicit pat: &str, and no conformance fixture exercises pat=None. Neither is the framed 'rsplit missing'. Recommend closing as already-implemented or re-filing the List-dtype work as its own architectural bead.
close_reason: Already implemented / misframed. StringAccessor HAS rsplit_df(pat,n) (~26471, expand=True, correct rightmost limit) + rsplit_get(pat,n) (~26669); tests str_rsplit_df_with_limit + series_str_rsplit_get_golden_basic + conformance fp_p2d_254/294 all pass. Genuine remaining gaps are (1) expand=False list-of-values Series (blocked: no Scalar::List in fp-types, architectural) and (2) pat=None whitespace-run split — neither is 'rsplit missing'. Fresh perf reports show the largest measured hotspot in perf_profile CSV panels is build_csv_string float Display formatting (core::num::imp::flt2dec::strategy::grisu::format_shortest_opt / float_to_decimal_common_shortest), while parser delimiter-scan variants were rejected. Baseline via RCH-built perf_profile before edit; golden sha256 before/after; prove generated CSV byte-isomorphism for the benchmark formula, parsed output equality, row/column order, dtype/null/NaN semantics, and no RNG/tie-breaking/hash-order changes. title: [parity] set_index on Float64 / Bool columns (IndexLabel lacks Float64/Bool variants)
description: scalar_to_index_label (fp-frame ~455) rejects Float64/Bool columns: 'set_index currently supports Int64/Utf8/Datetime64/Timedelta64'. pandas allows Float64Index and bool index. NEEDS new IndexLabel variants (fp-index enum ~99): Float64 requires a total-order/Hash wrapper (f64 isn't Ord/Hash — use the existing f64_radix_key/FloatBits bit pattern, normalize -0.0); Bool is trivially Ord/Eq/Hash. Cross-cutting: touches all IndexLabel match arms (Display, dtype, scalar<->label, sort/hash). Multi-step. Float64 is the higher-value/common case.
notes:
SCOUTED + DE-RISKED (BlackThrush). Validated design, ready to execute (~60 mechanical sites, blast radius far smaller than the raw 824 grep):

1) fp-index: add `pub struct OrderedF64(pub f64)` with MANUAL PartialEq/Eq/Hash (via canonical_bits: -0.0->+0.0, all-NaN->one bucket) and Ord (NaN sorts LAST, -0.0==+0.0, else partial_cmp) — MUST be mutually consistent (a==b => cmp==Equal). #[derive(Debug,Clone,Copy,Serialize,Deserialize)] + #[serde(transparent)]. Then add variants to IndexLabel (keep `#[derive(...Ord,Hash,Serialize...)]`): `Float64(OrderedF64)` and `Bool(bool)` placed BEFORE Null (so Null stays last in derived cross-variant Ord). This part is the careful bit; once it compiles, the rest is mechanical.

2) fp-index has exactly 17 exhaustive-match sites needing arms (verified by cargo check): is_missing (Float64=>v.is_nan(), Bool=>false), index_label_is_truthy, Display (Float64: repr-style with .0 for whole, NaN->"NaN"; Bool->"True"/"False"), detect_sort_order len<=1 match (Float64/Bool=>Unsorted), astype_int (Float64=>Int64(v as i64), Bool=>0/1), astype_str (Python str: "1.0"/"nan"/"True"/"False"), memory_usage (Float64=>8, Bool=>1), infer_dtype (Float64=>"floating", Bool=>"boolean") + its same_kind matches! closure, map_utf8(=>None), + ~9 datetime/timedelta/range/multiindex label maps that all ROUTE Float64/Bool to the existing default (None/i64::MIN/NAT/8), asof_comparison_type_name (Float64=>"float", Bool=>"bool").

3) fp-frame: exactly 43 exhaustive-match sites (lines captured 2026 session): 400,569,1366,1381,1484,1495,1526,1580,1596,2512,2615,2624,3923,3934,13155,13285,13400,18796,18837,18869,19246,32356,32380,32599,33725,37292,38288,38559,39261,39750,42573,42667,42935,43072,43162,43303,43658,43802,43865,44388,44440,54848,54953. MOSTLY route-to-default. The FEATURE-CRITICAL ones (need real handling, NOT default): scalar_to_index_label@452 (Scalar::Float64=>IndexLabel::Float64(OrderedF64(v)) [normalize -0.0], Scalar::Bool=>IndexLabel::Bool; this currently REJECTS float/bool — that rejection IS the bug), index_label_to_scalar@1365/33724 (Float64=>Scalar::Float64, Bool=>Scalar::Bool), index_label_to_utf8_scalar@1385 (str forms), and the IndexLabel match in to_period/reset_index/CSV repr (32356/32380 etc -> Scalar::Float64/Bool).

4) VERIFY: set_index on a Float64 column round-trips (set_index then reset_index gives back the float column); index repr/astype(str) matches pandas Float64Index ("1.0","2.5"); Float64 index sort numeric + NaN-last; -0.0/+0.0 dedup. Oracle: pd.Series(...).set_index(pd.Index([1.5,2.0])).
Then quantile([list]) (needs Float64 index) becomes unblockable too.
EFFORT: ~2-3h to land+verify with quality; NOT a one-hour job (reverted a scout that proved fp-index compiles clean with this design).
close_reason: LANDED: added fp_index::OrderedF64 (consistent Eq/Hash via canonical bits -0.0->+0.0 & NaN-collapse; Ord NaN-last) + IndexLabel::Float64(OrderedF64)/Bool(bool) before Null. set_index on Float64/Bool columns now produces Float64Index/boolean index (was rejected); reset_index round-trips; labels render like pandas (1.5/2.0/3.25, True/False). ~60 match sites fixed across fp-index(17)/fp-frame(45 incl scalar_to_index_label feature)/fp-io(5)/fp-join(JoinKeyComponent Ord)/fp-expr/fp-conformance. fp-index 388 + fp-frame 2870 tests green, clippy clean. (4 pre-existing conformance-drift fixtures fail on datetime-typing/ewm-adjust, unrelated, tracked in ie5q1.) Unblocks quantile([list]).

title: Add CategoricalIndex unique/duplicated/drop_duplicates/value_counts/factorize
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): CategoricalIndex still misses the dedup / value-count / factorize surface. Forward through the existing to_index() bridge that materializes labels as a Utf8 Index, then rebuild as CategoricalIndex via with_categories() so the categorical metadata is preserved when needed.

Add five methods on CategoricalIndex:
- unique() -> Self — first-seen unique labels using HashSet, preserves the categories list and ordered flag.
- duplicated(keep) -> Vec<bool> — forwarded to Index::duplicated through to_index().
- drop_duplicates() -> Self — first-seen filter; categories preserved.
- value_counts() -> Vec<(String, usize)> — pandas drops NaN by default; CategoricalIndex labels are non-null, so total == len.
- factorize() -> (Vec<isize>, Self) — standard first-seen factorization; uniques is a CategoricalIndex.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: a 6-label index with duplicates, dedup ordering preservation, factorize codes round-trip, and value_counts total.
close_reason: Shipped in e2ccdfb: CategoricalIndex now exposes pandas-parity unique/duplicated/drop_duplicates/value_counts/factorize. 2 regression tests pass; 275 fp-index tests total.

Bit-identical (each column's distinct count independent, reassembled in column_order). 10.3 -> 2.65ms ~3.9x. golden 77e61b71 unchanged (f/fnull/fi nunique + dropna=false already covered). 22 unit + 13 conformance green.

title: [perf] contiguous-Utf8 byte-span build+probe for single string-key inner join (skip JoinKeyComponent String clones)
description: Single string-key inner join (merge on names/ids/categories) falls through every Int64 fast path to collect_single_join_keys -> Vec<JoinKeyComponent>, which materializes the key column to Vec<Scalar> AND clones every String into JoinKeyComponent::Present(IndexLabel::Utf8(v.clone())) on BOTH sides, then hashes the wrappers via FxHashMap<&JoinKeyComponent>. Lever: when both key columns are all-valid contiguous-Utf8 (Column::as_utf8_contiguous), build FxHashMap<&[u8],SmallVec<positions>> over the right's raw byte spans and probe with the left's spans -> (left_positions,right_positions), then reuse the shared build_single_key_inner_merge_output. Same proven primitive as groupby s7b7q (byte-span FxHash). Skips all per-row String clones + Scalar materialization. Bit-identical: byte spans are the UTF-8 of the strings; right inserted ascending per bucket, left probed in order = the exact JoinKeyComponent pairing; null-bearing keys keep the generic path (as_utf8_contiguous gates all-valid, and FP matches null-to-null only there). Golden sha256 on a string-key inner join; Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Shipped 9cb0fa23: contiguous_utf8_inner_positions — byte-span FxHash build+probe for all-valid string-key inner joins, skipping ~4M String clones + Scalar materialization. Bit-identical (golden str_inner_join 55882afc before==after; 102 fp-join tests green). n=1M ~1:1 10%-overlap (build-dominated): 242->186 ms = 1.30x, AND eliminates the String-alloc latency variance (before swung 298-518ms full-overlap, after rock-stable). BELOW 2.0: the clone elimination saves ~56ms but the FxHashMap build+probe over n keys is the real wall, unchanged. The >=2x lever (byte-radix sort-merge or parallel hash-partition, building on these byte-span keys) filed as br-frankenpandas-53lat.

title: [parity] read_csv skipinitialspace= unsupported (strip leading spaces after delimiter)
description: pandas read_csv(skipinitialspace=True) strips leading SPACES (0x20 only, NOT tabs) from each field BEFORE quote detection, so '   "q,q"' -> 'q,q' (space skipped, then quote honored); trailing spaces preserved. CsvReadOptions has no such field. FP's main parse uses the external csv crate (.quote()), whose Trim::Fields trims BOTH sides + tabs (wrong semantics) and happens AFTER quote parsing, so it can't reproduce the space-before-quote case. IMPL: add skipinitialspace:bool (default false => bit-identical), and a quote-aware byte pre-processor over raw input that, in unquoted context, skips 0x20 after each delimiter (and line start? verify vs oracle); gate to default quoting (quotechar='"', doublequote=true, escapechar=None) to avoid replicating escape semantics, fall back otherwise. Self-contained in fp-io (non-contended). Oracle-verified semantics in bead notes.
close_reason: IMPLEMENTED. Added CsvReadOptions.skipinitialspace (default false). read_csv_with_options pre-processes the raw input (quote/escape/doublequote/terminator-aware byte pass, apply_skipinitialspace) to strip ASCII 0x20 at each field start BEFORE quote handling, then re-enters with the flag cleared so existing fast paths apply to the stripped text. Bit-exact vs pandas 2.2.3: leading spaces at first-field/header/after-delimiter stripped; tabs kept; trailing kept; spaces inside quotes preserved; '   "x,y"' parsed as the quoted value; all-space field -> empty. 2 new tests + 18 read_csv tests pass, clippy clean. Default false => no behavior change to existing reads (no fixture impact).

title: dt.total_seconds() conflates datetime & timedelta inputs; drops days; returns Int64; Timedelta::parse lacks 'N days HH:MM:SS' (gauntlet CONF-RC4a)
description: DatetimeAccessor::total_seconds (fp-frame) parses values as DATETIME and sums only h*3600+mi*60+sec, dropping the days component (so '1 days' -> 0) and returning Int64 not Float64. FP-P2D-333 (timedelta input) expects Float64(86400.0); FP returns Int64(0). A naive fix to Timedelta::parse(s) fails because Timedelta::parse (fp-types:1041) does NOT handle the combined 'N days HH:MM:SS' format (parse_compound chokes on the ':' after the days unit) -> returns Null, regressing FP-P2D-245. Also FP-P2D-245 is itself a FIXTURE DEFECT: it feeds datetimes and expects Int64 seconds-since-midnight, but pandas datetimes have no total_seconds(). Proper fix: (1) extend Timedelta::parse to accept 'N days HH:MM:SS'; (2) route timedelta-typed series total_seconds to Timedelta::total_seconds->Float64; (3) regenerate/retire FP-P2D-245. Gauntlet attempted+reverted the partial fix to avoid a gate regression.
acceptance_criteria: Two sub-fixes: (1) extend fp-types Timedelta::parse to accept combined 'N days HH:MM:SS' (parse_compound currently chokes on ':' after the days unit); (2) route timedelta-typed Series total_seconds to Timedelta::total_seconds -> Float64 (NOT datetime hour/min/sec extraction). UNIT TESTS: Timedelta::parse('1 days 00:00:00')==86400e9 ns, negative, fractional sec, NaT, '0 days 01:30:00'; total_seconds dtype==Float64 incl days. CONFORMANCE: FP-P2D-333 green; NO regression to FP-P2D-022/093 (working series_timedelta_total_seconds path). Coordinate FP-P2D-245 retirement (rg8ys.3.2). Verified via T9_DELTA, zero red-flips.
notes: RESOLVED + GATE-VERIFIED 2026-05-26. Two parts: (1) the TIMEDELTA total_seconds (FP-P2D-333) was fixed earlier (green); (2) the DATETIME case (FP-P2D-245) now passes — DatetimeAccessor::total_seconds (fp-frame:22568) detects YYYY-MM-DD datetime strings and returns Err('total_seconds is only valid for timedelta, not datetime'), matching the regenerated fixture's expected_error_contains='total_seconds' (pandas datetimes have no .total_seconds()). Verified green this session after a fresh rebuild (the rewrite had landed after a prior gate build). gate red 4->3.
close_reason: Complex dt accessor issue involving Timedelta parsing. Filed for systematic accessor audit.

title: [deadlock-finder] remove leftover piped stdin from GroupBy.apply oracle
description: Deadlock-finder follow-up: br-frankenpandas-50b6b0 described conformance_groupby_apply.rs as part of the subprocess pipe-deadlock sweep, but pandas_groupby_apply_shapes_or_skip still spawns the Python oracle with stdin(Stdio::piped()), synchronously writes an empty payload, drops stdin, then calls wait_with_output. The current empty payload cannot fill the pipe, but the leftover shape is the same pre-wait write pattern and will regress if the oracle starts accepting JSON input. Since the embedded Python script does not read stdin, replace the piped stdin with Stdio::null() and remove the write path entirely. Add/retain focused conformance_groupby_apply validation via rch.

title: [test-harness] 5 conformance fixtures drift under pandas 2.2.3 (legacy-pinned oracle); refresh on pinned-pandas upgrade
notes: PRECISE DIAGNOSIS 2026-06-14 (BlackThrush): the 'drift under pandas 2.2.3' framing is WRONG for 4 of the 5. Actual causes are TWO distinct things, NOT pinned-pandas drift: (A) ewm_mean_span_strict = genuine adjust-default change: fixture expects 1.5 (adjust=False, pre-rewrite) but fp-frame=pandas-2.2.3=1.6667 (adjust=True default, usdk2 rewrite). True stale fixture; fp-frame correct. (B) series_to_datetime (3 cases) + csv parse_dates (2 cases) = the 0ezw7 Datetime64-BACKING migration: fp-frame now returns typed Datetime64(ns) but fixtures expect legacy Utf8 str. fp-frame ns values are pandas-EXACT. series_to_datetime FIXED 2026-06-14 (43ec43c8) — its oracle serializer datetime_scalar_to_json is op-LOCAL so bounded. csv parse_dates is ENTANGLED: it serializes via the GLOBAL scalar_to_json which deliberately keeps datetime->utf8 (comment ~215) because to_timestamp/dt.floor still produce Utf8 in fp-frame (and pass). fp-frame's migration is PARTIAL/INCONSISTENT (csv parse_dates -> Datetime64, to_timestamp -> Utf8), so no single oracle rule satisfies both. RESOLUTION = complete the 0ezw7 migration: make ALL datetime-producing ops (to_timestamp, dt.floor/ceil/round/normalize, csv parse_dates) return typed Datetime64 consistently, then make scalar_to_json datetime64-aware, then regen all affected fixtures. Multi-session. Re-label: this bead is NOT 'refresh on pinned-pandas upgrade' — it's (A) one stale ewm golden + (B) the 0ezw7 migration tail.
close_reason: Closed stale drift bead: EWM fixture already regenerated to pandas 2.2.3 values (a9a13690), series_to_datetime fixtures already typed Datetime64 (43ec43c8), combined parse_dates fixture typed Datetime64 (1164bc42), and mixed naive/tz-aware parse_dates now normalizes per value with fp-io coverage; updated DISC-012 ledger.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Ran the FULL fp-conformance suite vs system pandas 2.2.3 (FP_ALLOW_SYSTEM_PANDAS_FALLBACK=1 cargo test -p fp-conformance): 1581 passed, 5 failed. All 5 are pandas-VERSION drift (legacy-pinned fixture/oracle vs system 2.2.3) or intentional DISC-011 — NOT FrankenPandas bugs. FP is parity-correct against its target. The 5: (1) conformance_io_read_csv_parse_dates_mixed_inferred_format_stays_object — datetime string repr drift: 2.2.3 gives '2024-01-15 10:30:00+00:00', legacy fixture '2024-01-15T10:30:00Z'; (2) conformance_datetime_utc_mixed_naive_offset_coercion — UTC offset coercion (pandas 2.x date-parsing overhaul); (3) conformance_io_read_csv_missing_heavy_numeric_column — int+NA -> Int64+null (deliberate DISC-011 nullable-Int64 migration, e7379f3a) vs pandas default float64; (4) conformance_concat_axis1_outer_int_columns_promote_to_float — same DISC-011 int+null, documented known issue; (5) conformance_reshape_pivot_table_missing_keys_dropna_default. ACTION: when the project upgrades its pinned legacy_pandas oracle to 2.2.3+, refresh these fixtures (esp. datetime string formats). No fix needed now; these tests skip/use legacy by default and pass against the pinned oracle. See memory live-oracle-gauntlet.

title: perf(types): O(n) select_nth for nanmedian/nanquantile
description: nanmedian/nanquantile full-sorted O(n log n) where only middle order-stats needed. select_nth_unstable_by O(n): median=mid-th + max-of-left; quantile=lo-th + min-of-right. ~5.54x (n=2M median+q0.9 x5, 864ms->156ms). Bit-identical golden sha256 f46ed3e... (order stats value-only). Helps Column/Series/DataFrame median+quantile, groupby median, describe. Timedelta paths left sorted (niche). fp-types 234/0 fp-columnar 373/0 fp-frame 2818/0 conformance 419/0. Shipped fcfafaec.
close_reason: Shipped fcfafaec: O(n) select_nth median/quantile, 5.54x.

title: [perf] SWAR byte-classified direct-fill CSV numeric parser
description:
Profile-backed target: residual default `csv_read 100000 20` remains the hottest non-columnar target after rejected parser admission/token-table trials; latest committed rejection evidence shows current baseline around 509-514 ms and token-table/clean-parser trials regressed.

Attack: replace parser-admission micro-levers with an algorithmically different primitive: a safe-Rust SWAR/byte-classified single-pass DFA for quote-free numeric CSV rows that classifies delimiter/newline/sign/digit/dot/exponent bytes in word-sized chunks where possible, fills per-column typed buffers directly, and falls back on quotes, CR complexity, ragged rows, default-NA/object/bool/NaN/inf/whitespace tokens, or unsupported shapes. No token-table allocation and no per-field default option checks on the admitted clean numeric path.

Proof obligations: rch release-perf baseline and hyperfine before edit; one lever only; golden-output sha256 before/after; preserve header semantics, duplicate-header errors, row/column order, RangeIndex, dtype promotion, null/NaN/bool fallback, floating-point parse bits, no RNG/tie-breaking/hash ordering; re-benchmark via rch and retain only Score>=2.0. Target ratio: >=1.20 on `csv_read 100000 20`.
close_reason: Rejected: RCH Criterion baseline io/csv_read/rows/100000 mean 16.164 ms; SWAR byte-state DFA candidate mean 22.375 ms, below Score>=2.0 keep gate. Source removed; next attack is row-blocked SIMD delimiter bitsets plus direct columnar numeric buffers.

title: [perf] Typed Float64 fast path for Series/DataFrame.diff (Scalar-path was 30x slow)
description: gap_hunt probe (NEW, typed columns n=200k x8): Series::diff materialized Vec<Scalar> + per-element to_f64 + Column::new revalidation even for typed Float64 columns, ignoring the typed backing. 53.9ms for 8x200k = 30x too slow vs pandas. FIX: as_f64_slice fast path computes data[i]-data[j] into a typed f64 buffer + validity, from_f64_values_with_validity. Bit-identical (golden 1b3c9972). DONE this session.
close_reason: Shipped aab15311: typed Float64 fast path for Series::diff (as_f64_slice -> f64 pass + from_f64_values_with_validity). DataFrame.diff 53.9->1.77ms = 30.4x bit-identical (golden 1b3c9972). fp-frame 2830 + fp-conformance 2047 green. Found via NEW gap_hunt probe; umbrella eydcr tracks the rest of the family.

title: Add to_csv index_label string option parity
description: Parity gap from pandas 2.2 Series.to_csv/DataFrame.to_csv: index_label='name' overrides the leading index header cell when index=True and header=True. FrankenPandas fp-frame currently derives that cell only from the index name/default. Add explicit Rust APIs for string index_label override while preserving existing defaults, with focused Series/DataFrame tests.
close_reason: Added to_csv index_label string option parity

title: [mock-code-finder] fp-frame coerce_scalar to Utf8 uses Display (yields 'NaN') instead of cast_scalar (pandas 'nan')
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:1586 — coerce_scalar for DType::Utf8 calls `format!("{val}")`, which goes through Scalar's Display impl. The Display impl emits "NaN" (uppercase) for Null(NaN), "NaT" for NaT, and Float64 NaN as "NaN".

fp-types::cast_scalar_owned at line 437 routes through `scalar_to_string_for_astype` which emits pandas-faithful "nan" (lowercase) for both Null(NaN) and Float64(NaN). This is locked in by br-x1k8c astype Utf8 parity work.

The two paths diverge: df.astype(Utf8) → cast_scalar (correct), but df.astype_safe coerce mode → coerce_scalar (wrong "NaN").

Surgical fix: replace line 1586 with `fp_types::cast_scalar_owned(val.clone(), DType::Utf8).unwrap_or(Scalar::Utf8(String::new()))`. Same for Categorical at line 1587.

Tests: astype_safe on Float64 NaN to Utf8 → Utf8("nan") in coerce mode; matches pandas. Existing astype Utf8 tests stay green.
close_reason: Not a bug — coerce_scalar early-returns missing values unchanged via is_missing() guard at line 1571, so the Display path for NaN/NaT is never reached during astype_safe. The Display 'NaN' spelling is internal to debug printing; user-facing astype goes through fp_types::cast_scalar which already produces pandas-faithful 'nan'/'NaT'/'None'. Reverted speculative change.

title: Add DType::Datetime64 variant for datetime column storage
description: Timestamp struct exists but DType enum has no Datetime64 variant. This prevents proper datetime column storage and dtype reporting. Need to add DType::Datetime64 and wire it through ColumnData, cast_scalar, common_dtype, and infer_dtype.
close_reason: done

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index intersection/union/difference/symmetric_difference
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): DatetimeIndex/TimedeltaIndex still miss the four set-operation forwarders. Mirrors the typed forwarder pattern from br-bbgg3 / br-834v9.

Add four methods on each of DatetimeIndex / TimedeltaIndex:
- intersection(other) -> Self — labels in both, first-seen order.
- union(other) -> Self — labels from self followed by labels from other not already present.
- difference(other) -> Self — labels in self not in other.
- symmetric_difference(other) -> Self — labels in either but not both, preserving self-then-other order.

Each preserves the index name when both operands share it (otherwise dropped, matching pandas).

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: typical 3-element vs 3-element overlap, empty operand passthrough, NAT membership semantics (NAT == NAT), and name reconciliation.
close_reason: Shipped in 0fe2252: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity intersection/union/difference/symmetric_difference with first-seen ordering, deduplication, and name reconciliation. 2 regression tests pass; 289 fp-index tests total.

title: [perf] Index::argmin/argmax materialize Int64 labels (affine = O(1))
description: Index::argmin/argmax (fp-index/src/lib.rs ~2498/2510) call self.labels.iter().min_by/max_by, materializing Vec<IndexLabel> via as_slice. Same lazy-Int64 materialization vein as gzi1i/k9tlb/etc. FIX: (1) affine Int64 (int64_affine_range): extreme is at a known end — argmin = step>=0?0:len-1, argmax = step>=0?len-1:0 (len==1 => 0 both; len==0 => None). O(1). (2) lazy typed/strided Int64 (guard has_lazy_int64_backing() so already-materialized indexes keep the label iter and avoid an extra i64 alloc, matching the any()/all() pattern): scan int64_view() &[i64] with the SAME min_by/max_by. Bit-identical: IndexLabel::Int64 Ord == i64 Ord, identical last-min/last-max tie-break, identical order. Add test asserting correctness + materialized.get().is_none().
close_reason: Added lazy-Int64 fast paths to Index::argmin/argmax (avoids Vec<IndexLabel> materialization via iter().min_by/max_by). Affine (RangeIndex/unit/affine): O(1) end index (argmin=step>=0?0:len-1, argmax=step>=0?len-1:0). Lazy typed/strided Int64 (guarded has_lazy_int64_backing like any()/all()): scan int64_view() &[i64] with identical min_by/max_by. Bit-identical: Rust min_by=first-min, max_by=last-max, IndexLabel::Int64 Ord==i64 Ord, same order. Test int64_argminmax_avoid_label_materialization_ikbh9 covers affine asc/desc, typed ties (first-min/last-max), object-path equivalence, single/empty, + materialized.get().is_none(). Verified: targeted test pass, full fp-index 410 passed/0 failed, ubs 0 critical. Artifact: tests/artifacts/perf/ikbh9_index_argminmax_lazy_int64.txt

title: [parity][feature] wide_to_long missing (the only absent reshaping fn)
description: pd.wide_to_long(df, stubnames, i, j, sep, suffix) was the only missing reshaping fn (melt/pivot/stack/unstack/crosstab/explode all present). Implemented as a free fn: per-stub anchored regex '^{stub}{sep}(suffix)$', distinct suffixes in FIRST-APPEARANCE order (pandas does NOT sort them), numeric suffix -> Int64 j else Utf8, builds a flat [i..,j,stubs] frame then set_index_multi to the (i..,j) row MultiIndex. Missing stub/suffix combos -> NaN.
close_reason: Implemented pub fn wide_to_long(df, stubnames, i, j, sep, suffix): per-stub anchored regex, first-appearance suffix order (not sorted), numeric->Int64 j else Utf8, flat [i..,j,stubs] frame -> set_index_multi (i..,j) row MultiIndex, missing combos -> NaN. Verified vs pandas 2.2.3 (numeric+sorted, sep+first-appearance-order). 1 test green, clippy clean. Float i column still blocked by i10en (IndexLabel no Float64); regex suffix + multi-i supported.

description: Ready queue is empty while DataFrame API coverage drift still reports eval/query/join/merge missing even though fp-expr exposes them through DataFrameExprExt for DataFrame and fp-join exposes merge/join through DataFrameMergeExt for DataFrame. Extend scripts/validate_api_coverage_drift.py so the DataFrame surface counts pandas-named extension trait methods from fp-expr and fp-join, preserving the existing IOWrappers report and keeping truly absent DataFrame methods such as asfreq/first/last/to_timestamp/to_xarray visible.

title: [perf] precompute complete-column corr/cov moments
description: Profile-backed target after br-frankenpandas-dhaaf: RCH reprofile shows df_corr 5000x64x10 at 115.3 ms mean and df_cov at 109.7 ms mean, with gdb landing in DataFrame::pairwise_stat_matrix via corr() (tests/artifacts/perf/gdb_before_df_corr_moments_cod_pandas.txt). Active perf ownership covers fp-columnar (RubyGoose) and fp-join (BlackThrush), so this targets fp-frame only. Alien primitive: safe-Rust BLAS-like Gram/moment matrix. One lever: for complete no-NaN numeric columns, precompute each column's sum and sumsq once and reuse those moments for every corr/cov pair while still accumulating sum_xy per pair in row order; fallback to the existing pairwise_stat_matrix path when any column has NaN/missing/unsupported values. Proof obligations: RCH hyperfine baseline/after for df_corr 5000 10 and df_cov 5000 10, golden sha256 unchanged for df_corr/df_cov, preserve column order, FP bit behavior by only admitting complete no-NaN columns and summing each precomputed moment in the same row order as the old pair loop, no RNG/tie/hash ordering, RCH fp-frame focused tests/check/clippy/fmt, UBS.
close_reason: Rejected: complete-column corr/cov moment precompute failed score gate; df_corr regressed 109.0 ms -> 123.1 ms and df_cov was flat/slower 111.6 ms -> 113.3 ms, so no source retained

title: [parity] groupby first/last must skip nulls (pandas skipna default)
description: REAL parity bug: pandas groupby.first()/last() return the first/last NON-NULL value per group (skipna=True default), but fp returns the literal group_vals[0]/[last] (which may be NaN/null). Sites in crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs: aggregate_named_func generic (~55795 DataFrameGroupBy.first/last), apply_agg_func (~57567 agg-dict), SeriesGroupBy.transform (~25351). Dense int64 path (~55584) is all-valid so unaffected. Likely golden-safe (existing first/last goldens use all-valid data where literal==non-null; only null-containing groups change). Fix: first = group_vals.iter().find(!is_missing); last = .rev().find(!is_missing), else missing. ALSO REVIEW: SeriesGroupBy.first/last bare methods, resample first/last, and the fp-groupby crate groupby_first/last free fns. Code-first; DataFrameGroupBy paths fixed + test here, remaining sites tracked. fp-frame.

Proof obligations: header order/names/duplicate errors preserved for admitted simple headers; quoted headers, CRLF/header edge cases, fallback object/raw text, row/column order, dtype promotion, FP bits, null/NaN/bool semantics, and RNG/tie-breaking unchanged. Baseline RCH hyperfine + golden sha256 first; keep only Score>=2.0.

Next attack: deeper CSV framing/token-table parser primitive, not another header-admission tweak.

title: [perf] blocked Gram for complete Spearman rank correlation
description: Profile-backed post-8qjiu target. Clean pushed-tip reprofile artifact tests/artifacts/perf/fp_reprofile_after_8qjiu_cod_pandas.json measured df_spearman 5000x64 iters=5 at 134.7 ms +/- 4.2 (26.9 ms/iter), the top unowned non-CSV hotspot; CSV is hotter but the fp-io surface has peer dirt/auajw artifacts and is not claimed here. perf record artifact tests/artifacts/perf/perf_df_spearman_after_8qjiu_cod_pandas.data and report tests/artifacts/perf/perf_report_df_spearman_after_8qjiu_cod_pandas.txt sample pairwise_rank_corr at 30.99% and rank sorting at 16.05%; gdb attach artifact tests/artifacts/perf/gdb_df_spearman_after_8qjiu_cod_pandas.txt records ptrace_scope failure. Alien primitive: safe-Rust blocked upper-triangle Gram over complete centered rank vectors, replacing scalar per-pair dot loops inside DataFrame::pairwise_rank_corr for the all-complete Spearman path only. One lever only. Proof obligations: RCH-built hyperfine baseline before edit, golden df_spearman sha256 unchanged, preserve column/row order, symmetric copy, rank tie handling, NaN/incomplete fallback to existing per-pair corr_spearman, FP dot accumulation order per pair, RNG/hash/tie behavior N/A; RCH rebench; keep only Score>=2.0 or remove source.
close_reason: Rejected: safe-Rust 4x4 blocked upper-triangle Gram over complete Spearman centered ranks preserved behavior but missed Score>=2.0. Baseline RCH-built df_spearman 5000x5: 141.3 ms +/- 5.9. Paired A/B: 181.9 ms +/- 8.4 -> 170.0 ms +/- 8.9, only 1.07x +/- 0.07. Golden sha256 unchanged: dc37f75e1ee2e23e28ed89e0f178a0268cb65cc420bc82f97cb16d219eb7dd1e. Isomorphism held: complete-rank path folded each pair in row order and preserved column/row order, symmetric copy, rank tie handling, NaN/incomplete fallback, FP/RNG/hash/tie behavior. Source change removed; fp-frame diff is clean, cargo fmt -p fp-frame -- --check and git diff --check pass. Pivot trigger: blocked rank-dot is not enough after current pairwise_rank_corr profile; next primitive is zero-Scalar typed Float64 rank extraction/materialization avoidance for Spearman ranks, unless the hotter CSV numeric parser surface is released by the peer working auajw/fp-io.

All chainable, HTML-only, compose with existing highlight/gradient/format/caption; no-directive to_html still byte-identical to DataFrame::to_html. Test dataframe_ium6s_style_set_properties_bar_hide_index; fp-frame --lib style tests 6/6 green, clippy clean.

title: [perf] parallel per-column gather in position-based inner merge output (f64-column joins)
description: build_single_key_inner_merge_output (fp-join, the position-based fallback used when the fused dense-i64 builder bails — e.g. i64 keys + Float64 value columns, the common real-world join shape) gathers every output column serially via take_positions_typed over the (up to 19.5M-row) position vectors. Columns are independent, so this is embarrassingly parallel (proven pattern: CSV merge e595e700, dense-i64 fused fill 6bsw3). Lever: collect output specs serially (name/suffix resolution is cheap), build the per-column gathers across a std::thread::scope worker pool (one column per task), then insert in spec order. Bit-identical by construction (same per-column output, concurrent build). Gate on output_len + ncols>1 + thread_count>1. Golden sha256 on a wide f64-column inner join; Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Shipped e49c4471: parallel per-column gather in build_single_key_inner_merge_output (position-based inner join, used for i64-key + Float64-value joins). Bit-identical golden (13-col f64 inner join, before==after 52737f78). n=100k card 512 (19.5M rows): 1766->553 ms/iter = 3.19x. 102 fp-join tests green.

title: [perf] to_datetime ISO parse 13-16x slower than pandas (Scalar round-trip + multi-format chrono)
description: PROFILE-BACKED (1M, release-perf, new datetime bench 591c828f): to_datetime over ISO YYYY-MM-DD strings is 15.85x@1M / 15.59x@100k slower than pd.to_datetime (fp=902ms vs pd=57ms; pandas cache=False ~68ms so the unique-dedup cache is only ~1.25x — the parser/path is ~13x). Root cause: (1) series.values() materializes a heap Scalar::Utf8 PER ROW (1M String allocs); (2) parse_datetime_string tries 2-3 chrono parse_from_str formats per string (most fail) then RENDERS a Utf8 that is RE-PARSED to Datetime64 (triple pass); (3) Column::from_values re-scans the output. FIX (shipping): a hand-rolled fast_iso_datetime_nanos (digits -> Hinnant civil-days -> epoch nanos, == chrono) + a typed contiguous-Utf8 path in to_datetime_with_options that reads byte spans directly and bails the whole column to the general path on any non-plain-ISO span (tz/slashes/text-month/invalid). Bit-identical.
notes: FOLLOW-UP refinement: the residual 1.49x output cost needs a NEW lazy ScalarValues variant (LazyAllValidDatetime64, mirroring LazyAllValidInt64) threaded through fp-columnar — Datetime64 columns currently box Eager Vec<Scalar::Datetime64> for their values, so from_values can't avoid the 1M Scalar boxing. That's a real fp-columnar addition (new enum arm + all ScalarValues match sites), not a one-liner; deferred as a modest ~1.5x->parity follow-up. The 11x typed-parse win is the bulk and is shipped.
close_reason: CLOSED — to_datetime now BEATS pandas. Two commits: 33268867 (typed-ISO parse fast path, 17.45x->1.49x) + 80f38c8e (typed Datetime64 output via new LazyAllValidDatetime64 ScalarValues variant + Column::from_datetime64_values, 1.49x->0.35x). Final: to_datetime is 0.35x@1M / 0.30x@100k = fp ~2.9-3.3x FASTER than pandas (cumulative ~45x fp-side speedup from the original 936ms->20.5ms). Bit-identical (fp-columnar suite + 126 fp-frame datetime + 26 conformance green). The LazyAllValidDatetime64 typed backing is reusable for any Datetime64-producing op.

description: Post-pass w8826 reprofile ranks series_add_align 100000x100 hottest at 1.005s +/- 0.010, narrowly ahead of csv_read 0.985s. title: DataFrame::loc_with_columns loses row index name
description: fp-frame/src/lib.rs:24400 builds Index::new(out_labels) where labels come from self.index, but discards self.index.name. Pandas df.loc[labels, columns] preserves row index name. Fix: rename_index(self.index.name()).
close_reason: Built subset Index with rename_index in loc_with_columns at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:24400.

title: [mock-code-finder] fp-types nanmedian/nanvar/nanstd/nansem/nanskew/nankurt silently drop Timedelta64 (collect_finite coerces via to_f64)
description:
fp-types/src/lib.rs nanmedian, nanvar, nanstd, nansem, nanskew, nankurt all build via `collect_finite(values)` which uses `.filter_map(|v| v.to_f64().ok())`. Timedelta64.to_f64() errors (NonNumericValue) → every Timedelta64 input is silently dropped.

Pandas:
- td_series.median()/std()/var()/sem() return Timedelta64
- skew/kurt return Float64 (dimensionless, this is OK)

Fix: median/std/var/sem add Timedelta64 fast path using i128 ns accumulation (mirroring nansum/nanmean fix in br-620mj). skew/kurt may also benefit but lower priority since pandas returns dimensionless f64 anyway.

Tests: nanmedian([Timedelta(1h), Timedelta(3h)]) == Timedelta(2h); nanstd on Timedelta64 returns Timedelta64; NAT skipped.

Continues br-naeja/dj6rv/q82t4/mcu90/e607u/yic5m/620mj Timedelta64 silent-stub remediation family.
close_reason: Implemented Timedelta64 fast path in nanmedian, nanvar, nanstd, nansem. nanskew/nankurt left alone (pandas returns dimensionless f64). Commit 42d1d211. 170 fp-types + 1901 fp-frame tests pass.

title: [parity] DataFrameGroupBy.prod() returns Float64 for Int64 input (same as rl25i, separate path + golden)
description: Sibling of br-frankenpandas-rl25i (which fixed the fp-groupby free function groupby_prod to preserve Int64). fp-frame's DataFrame.groupby().prod() uses its OWN aggregation path (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs ~21132 and ~25289: 'prod' => fp_types::nanprod(&group_vals)) which still returns Float64 for Int64 input. There is a golden groupby_prod_basic.txt (~line 115194/115221) that likely encodes the Float64 output and will need regeneration. Fix: mirror the Sum Int64-preserving path (i128 product, fallback to nanprod on overflow) for integer/bool input in BOTH fp-frame groupby prod sites; regenerate the golden; verify vs pandas oracle. Validation: cargo test -p fp-frame + golden regen.

title: [perf][ESCALATION] FMA build-decision to unblock corr/cov/spearman Gram (~900ms addressable, needs golden regen sign-off)
description:
PERF_PROFILE SWEEP (200k): the remaining BIG gaps are all bit-exact-bound, not slice-typing targets: df_kendall 655ms, df_spearman 251ms, df_corr/df_cov (blocked-Gram done 2.24x but ~2 GFLOP/s). The Float64 single-column materialization vein is EXHAUSTED — this session sem (1.66x), value_counts (1.16x low-card / 1.83x high-card, output/label-bound) all REJECTED <2.0; only 8-column DataFrame ops were materialization-bound enough (mode/nunique/quantile/describe/skew all shipped 2-8x prior sessions).

WHY BLOCKED: corr/cov/spearman dots are bit-exact vs golden. The bandwidth-cutting reorder (cache row-blocking, ~64x fewer reads) REASSOCIATES the f64 sum -> breaks parity. f64::mul_add (safe-Rust FMA, single-rounding) would ~halve the dot op-count AND enable vectorization, but changes result bits. Last session's bit-identical register-blocked spearman Gram was REJECTED (295>251ms: memory/latency-bound at 64col x 200k, strided i-reads lose to naive 2-col streaming).

ASK (orchestrator sign-off): approve regenerating the corr/cov/spearman/kendall numeric goldens under f64::mul_add (or workspace fma-contract) so the Gram can use fma + cache row-blocking. TARGET: corr/cov ~2-3x, spearman ~3-4x (fma + row-blocked Gram), ~900ms total addressable. This is the canonical NO-GAPS 'build our own BLAS kernel' unlock. Supersedes/extends bh7iy (which constrained to bit-identical and hit the memory wall). Kendall (655ms) is SEPARATE (integer Knight's-Fenwick, not fma).
close_reason: Rejected FMA/golden-regeneration approval under the active optimization contract. The bead states f64::mul_add changes result bits, while this campaign requires unchanged FP bits and current golden SHA verification for keeps. No runtime source edited. Proof: tests/artifacts/perf/proof_jawxr_fma_decision_reject.md. Route next: exact, profile-backed non-FMA work such as true cross-column Kendall dominance sharing, typed-columnar producer/consumer boundaries, exact join/filter materialization primitives, or safe SIMD/hash/table kernels whose current golden outputs remain byte-identical.

title: [parity] pivot_table aggfunc 'kurt'/'kurtosis' support
description: pivot_table_agg_value (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs ~960) supports sum/mean/count/size/min/max/first/last/prod/median/var/std/nunique/sem/skew but rejects 'kurt'/'kurtosis' with 'aggfunc not supported', while pandas pivot_table accepts kurt (groupby kurt). sem/skew already delegate to fp_types kernels; add a 'kurt'|'kurtosis' arm delegating to fp_types::nankurt (same pattern). Code-first; test added. fp-frame.

title: [test] differential: groupby cumsum within-group running sum
description: No-mock differential for DataFrameGroupBy.cumsum. Seeded LCG (no rand, no mocks): keys (small domain) + Int64 value; groupby(k).cumsum() yields per-group running sum aligned to original row order, vs HashMap<key,acc> oracle. Test-only; fp-frame.

title: [mock-code-finder] fuzz_scalar_cast corpus seed null_to_utf8.bin has expectation conflicting with pandas string spellings test
description:
fp-conformance/src/tests/fuzz_seed_fixtures.rs:1809 fails:
> scalar_cast corpus seed null_to_utf8.bin should pass: Err("missing cast drifted: value=Null(Null) target=Utf8 result=Utf8(\"None\") expected=Null(Null)")

But fp-types/src/lib.rs:2097 (test cast_scalar_to_utf8_uses_pandas_string_spellings) explicitly asserts that Scalar::Null(NullKind::Null) → Scalar::Utf8("None") (pandas-faithful — `pd.Series([None]).astype(str) == ['None']`).

The fuzz corpus seed expectation (preserve Null) contradicts the pandas spelling test (emit literal "None"/"nan"/"NaT").

Resolution: the fuzz corpus is stale. Either:
(a) Regenerate the corpus seed against the current pandas-faithful behavior.
(b) Delete the obsolete seed `null_to_utf8.bin`.

This is corpus drift after the br-x1k8c astype Utf8 parity work. Recommend (b) since the deterministic test at fp-types:2097 already locks in the correct behavior.

Path: locate `null_to_utf8.bin` under fp-conformance/fixtures/ (or wherever corpus seeds live) and remove or regenerate.
close_reason: Removed obsolete corpus seed and entry. Commit 55337af1. Fuzz test now passes.

title: [perf] Column-parallel row gather in DataFrame.take / take_rows_by_positions
description: df.take(random positions) 200k x8 = 20ms: take_rows (40150, df.take path) AND take_rows_by_positions_unchecked (32504, iloc/sort/drop_duplicates/groupby path) both looped column_order SERIALLY calling take_positions per column; a random-permutation gather is memory-latency-bound. Routed both through par_map_columns. Bit-identical (each column independent, reassembled in column_order). df_take 20 -> 6.2ms ~3.3x. golden a11ef155 (added shuf.take dump + df_take probe). 114 unit + 7 conformance green. Completes the gather-parallelization surface (reorder sibling already done rbllm).
notes: DONE (b853abe2): take_rows (40150) + take_rows_by_positions_unchecked (32504) per-col gather -> par_map_columns. df_take (random positions) 20->6.2ms ~3.3x. bit-identical golden a11ef155 (added shuf.take + df_take probe). 114 unit + 7 conformance green. LESSON: a SEQUENTIAL/strided take (step_by(2)) is bandwidth-bound + already fast (~0.8ms) — needs RANDOM positions to expose the latency-bound gather the parallelism wins.
close_reason: done

title: Add Series.argpartition method
[perf] Series::is_monotonic_increasing/decreasing Float64 typed fast path
description: Series::is_monotonic_increasing/decreasing got an Int64 fast path (z53ed, as_i64_slice) but Float64 still goes through self.column.values() — boxing every element into a Scalar + per-pair semantic_le/is_missing dispatch. Add a Float64 fast path via Column::as_f64_slice() (requires dtype==Float64 && validity.all(); explicit-null columns correctly fall to the Scalar path). Scan the &[f64]: a pair contributes false if either is NaN (matches is_missing(Float64(NaN))=v.is_nan() bail) else compares a<=b / a>=b (matches semantic_le/ge for non-NaN floats incl +/-inf, since partial_cmp is total off NaN). Bit-identical. ALREADY GUARDED by the existing differential harness series_predicate_fast_paths_match_scalar_reference_59dk2, which generates Float64 series (incl NaN) and asserts is_monotonic == the Scalar-path reference. Test-only verification: re-run 59dk2 + full fp-frame suite.
close_reason: Added Float64 typed fast path to Series::is_monotonic_increasing/decreasing via Column::as_f64_slice (was boxing Scalars). Scan &[f64]: NaN bails false (matches is_missing(Float64(NaN))=v.is_nan()), non-NaN incl inf compares <=/>= (matches semantic_le/ge since partial_cmp is total off NaN). as_f64_slice requires validity.all() so explicit-null cols fall to Scalar path. Bit-identical. ALREADY guarded by 59dk2 (generates Float64 incl NaN, asserts is_monotonic==Scalar reference). Verified: 59dk2 + series_is_monotonic 13 passed, full fp-frame 2898 passed/0 failed. Artifact: tests/artifacts/perf/jkgqe_series_is_monotonic_float64.txt

title: concat(axis=1) should preserve Int64 dtype across reindex (matches pandas 2.2.3 oracle)
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:22179 reindex_concat_axis1_column promotes Int64 to Float64 when reindexing introduces unaligned positions. The pandas 2.2.3 conformance oracle encodes the expected output as Int64-with-null (our types support nullable Int64 via Scalar::Null(NullKind::Null)). This drops 5 packet_filter_runs_dataframe_concat_axis1_packet cases (basic_strict, order_left_then_unseen_hardened, preserves_existing_nulls_hardened, sparse_alignment_strict, utf8_index_strict).

Fix: replace the function body with `Ok(column.reindex_by_positions(positions)?)`. reindex_by_positions fills missing positions with the dtype-appropriate missing sentinel.

Also update the concat_dataframes_axis1_aligns_and_fills_nulls unit test (line 41796) to assert Int64+Null(Null) instead of Float64+Null(NaN).

Verified by running `cargo test -p fp-conformance --tests packet_filter_runs_dataframe_concat_axis1_packet` after the fix — all 10 cases pass.

Filed by cc_1 because WhiteHeron has fp-frame/src/lib.rs reserved for ctmet fixture-regen work; this fix can land after that reservation releases.
close_reason: WhiteHeron landed the equivalent fix in fp-frame/src/lib.rs reindex_concat_axis1_column (now just delegates to reindex_by_positions) and updated the unit test to assert nullable Int64. Verified function body and test at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:22177 and :41770.

title: value_counts(dropna=False) collapses distinct NullKinds (None vs nan) into one bucket; blocked on IndexLabel having no typed-null variant
close_reason: COMPLETE: phase 1 (8735946b) added IndexLabel::Null(NullKind) — additive, 37 compiler-enumerated arms, conformance 1593/0, goldens unchanged. Phase 2 (04152db7) made value_counts(dropna=False) bucket by null kind and emit typed null labels; differential test pins live-pandas 2.2.3 behavior (object [None,nan,None,1] -> {None:2, nan:1, 1:1}); Display repr 'NaN' vs astype(str) 'nan' surfaces separated. fp-index+fp-frame 3197/0, conformance 1593/0, six goldens byte-identical. The silent NullKind collapse vs pandas is fixed.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Found in the NullKind-consistency sweep (sibling of 9x4qi/isin 2f6b5aaa). fp-frame value_counts_with_options(dropna=false) counts all missing into one null_count bucket emitting a single Null(NaN); pandas keeps None/nan distinct: object[None,nan,None,1].value_counts(dropna=False) -> {None:2, nan:1, 1:1}. BUT the fix is BLOCKED: scalar_to_value_counts_index_label (fp-frame:470) maps ALL Scalar::Null(_) -> IndexLabel::Utf8('<null>'), and IndexLabel has no typed-null variant (only Int64/Utf8/Timedelta64/Datetime64). So a kind-aware count would emit TWO '<null>' index labels (duplicate/confusing) rather than distinct None/nan. Proper fix needs IndexLabel to represent typed nulls (None vs NaN vs NaT) — a core-type change with wide blast radius. Also affects sort=False ordering: current code appends the null bucket at the END instead of first-seen position (minor, same site ~5429). Low priority until IndexLabel supports typed nulls.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: PARTIAL FIX shipped 065976dc: fixed the kind-AGNOSTIC half — the collapsed null bucket now sits at its first-seen position (pandas orders the null group by count with first-seen tiebreak / first-appearance for sort=False), was pinned to the end. REMAINING (this bead stays OPEN): kind-DISTINCTNESS (None vs nan as separate buckets) still blocked on IndexLabel having no typed-null variant. Verified ordering vs live pandas 2.2.3.

comments.author: cc-pandas
comments.text: Investigated 2026-06-04 (cc-pandas): blocker CONFIRMED real. value_counts_with_options (fp-frame:5667) hardcodes ScalarKey::Null(NullKind::NaN) for ALL missing, collapsing None/NaN/NaT. ScalarKey::Null(NullKind) ALREADY carries the kind so fixing COUNTS is trivial — but OUTPUT needs distinct index labels and IndexLabel (fp-index:99) has NO null variant; rendering nulls as text would COLLIDE with genuine 'None'/'NaN' strings. Verified pandas 2.2.3 object-dtype keeps None(2)/NaN(1) distinct. PROPER FIX needs IndexLabel::Null(NullKind) (invasive; fp-index reserved by BlackThrush). Half-fix yields duplicate '<null>' labels — rejected. Stays blocked.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Blast-radius measurement + implementation plan (cc-pandas 2026-06-07, post mock-code-finder sweep): IndexLabel:: refs = fp-frame 1640, fp-index 686, fp-io 199, fp-groupby 60, fp-conformance 54, fp-join 39 (2678 total; only EXHAUSTIVE matches break — compiler enumerates them; 94 match sites in fp-index alone). PLAN for IndexLabel::Null(NullKind): (1) semantics FIRST, decided against live pandas 2.2.3: Ord = nulls sort LAST within mixed labels (pandas NaN-last on sort_index), kind-insensitive for ORDERING but kind-SENSITIVE for Eq/Hash (None != nan as value_counts buckets; verify what pandas does for index.get_loc(nan) vs None — likely kind-insensitive LOOKUP, which means Eq for alignment must differ from bucket identity: use a separate wrapper for value_counts bucket keys if so, NOT IndexLabel Eq); (2) Display: None->'None', NaN->'nan', NaT->'NaT' matching pandas repr; (3) serde: new variant is additive for serialization, but old fixtures never contain it -> safe; (4) mechanical sweep: add variant, fix every compile error site choosing reject-vs-handle per site, golden-dump diff on full conformance corpus must be EMPTY except the value_counts target fixtures; (5) THEN fix value_counts_with_options:5667 to key ScalarKey::Null(kind) and emit IndexLabel::Null(kind), with live-pandas differential test (object[None,nan,None,1] -> {None:2, nan:1, 1:1}); (6) sibling audits: isin/unique/nunique/drop_duplicates null-bucket emitters may want the same labels — grep scalar_to_value_counts_index_label callers. Estimated 1-2 focused sessions. Recommend promoting to P2 epic with sub-beads (semantics-decision, mechanical-variant, value_counts-fix, sibling-sweep).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: PHASE 1 SHIPPED as 8735946b (content authored by cc-pandas, committed by a cod-pandas pane that swept the staged tree during a guard-blocked commit — second sweep incident after 36b0cf77; content verified verbatim, 7 files / 163+ / 29-). IndexLabel::Null(NullKind) added LAST (derived Ord = nulls-last), kind-sensitive Eq/Hash, Display None/nan/NaT, serde additive. 37 consumption arms swept compiler-guided across fp-index/fp-frame/fp-io/fp-join/fp-expr/fp-conformance: label<->Scalar bijection Null(kind)<->Scalar::Null(kind); to_csv EMPTY for missing index label (pandas verified); Excel blank cell; to_json null; typed gates treat Null as N/A; JoinKeyComponent Present(Null) sorts after concrete, before Missing; groupby-dropna proptest invariant now real. PROOF: purely additive (nothing constructs it), conformance 1593/0, all 6 perf goldens byte-identical, 3 workspace test failures PRE-EXISTING on clean main (filed k75hq, thxl7). PHASE 2 REMAINING (this bead stays open): value_counts_with_options ~5667 emit ScalarKey::Null(kind) buckets + IndexLabel::Null(kind) output labels; live-pandas differential test object[None,nan,None,1].value_counts(dropna=False) -> {None:2, nan:1, 1:1}; then sibling audit of scalar_to_value_counts_index_label callers (fp-frame:470 '<null>' mapping can now be replaced).

title: [perf] Lazy/blocked fanout-join output materialization (f64_inner_join 1239ms, gather-bound)
description: perf_profile f64_inner_join (100k, Int64 key card-512, 6 Float64 value cols/side) = 1239 ms/iter — the largest single perf gap in the bench suite. Shape: ~19.5M-row cartesian fanout output (n/512 left x n/512 right per key x 512 keys). Profile (n=60k): 52% memmove + 36% Column::take_positions. Value columns are already typed (ColumnData::Float64), gather is already typed 8B (verified: Scalar->typed take_positions branch is dead code here, no effect). The cost is RANDOM-ACCESS gather cache misses materializing the 19.5M x 13-col output eagerly via position vectors. LEVER (ambitious, structural, NO-GAPS): a lazy / zero-copy fanout-output column representation — keep (Arc source + position vector) and defer/stream materialization, or block the gather by key-group so each group's source rows are read contiguously (cache-resident) before scattering to output. Bit-identical: same values, same row order. Verify vs golden 52737f78 (n=5000). Target >=2x on the 1239ms. NOTE: the eager-materialization wall is shared by left_join/outer_join/right_join/str_*_join fanout shapes (21-50ms each at 100k). REJECTED levers documented in memory corr-join-perf-rejected-levers.
notes: ROOT CAUSE PINNED 2026-06-09: f64_inner_join (n=60k baseline min 310ms, golden 52737f78@5000) is OUTPUT-MATERIALIZATION bound, not gather-read bound. Run-aware typed gather REJECTED (left/key position runs are L1-cached; reads never the wall). REAL MECHANISM: merge_single_key_inner_unsorted tries build_single_key_dense_i64_inner_merge_output FIRST — it emits LAZY COMPRESSED cols (DenseI64LaneData::RepeatRunLengths=from_i64_repeat_values_run_lengths left; RepeatedSlices=from_i64_repeated_slices_shared right) and NEVER materializes the 19.5M output. It DECLINES (Ok(None)) the moment any col fails as_i64_slice (any Float64) -> falls to dense_int64_inner_positions + build_single_key_inner_merge_output flat take_positions (right bucket blocks cycle 512 keys, evict cache -> 19.5M cache-miss reads + 2GB write). LEVER = extend fused path to f64. (A) BEST: add LazyRepeatRunsFloat64 + LazyRepeatedSlicesFloat64 ScalarValues variants mirroring i64 (~fp-columnar lib.rs 1144/1164; ~20 match sites iter/len/clone/as_f64_slice/materialize) -> f64 cols also lazy, no materialization. (B) SIMPLER: materialize f64 Full but cache-efficient — per-bucket value tape once (tape[i]=right_f64[positions[i]]) then copy contiguous tape[offsets[b]..offsets[b+1]] blocks per matched left row; left broadcast; parallel across cols (fu8f5). Both bit-identical (matched walk order==flat position order; gate on golden 52737f78 + full join tests). Target >=2x (write-BW floor ~150ms vs 310ms). CRITICAL PATH: null/suffix/empty/require_all_left_keys_matched edges + 3ad4n/l4adm/6bsw3/muis1 — tightly-gated, careful tests. Shared by left/outer/right/str_*_join fanout.
close_reason: Landed ddb4c8e9 on main+master: lazy Float64 fanout lanes (LazyRepeatValuesFloat64 + LazyRepeatedSlicesFloat64) extend the fused dense-i64 inner merge to f64. 36.5x@60k / 91x@100k, bit-identical (golden 52737f78 unchanged; fp-columnar 386 + fp-join 113 + fp-conformance 419+ tests pass).

Add DataFrame::reindex_like(&DataFrame) that delegates to reindex(other.index.labels().to_vec()), preserving column order/dtypes and filling missing rows through existing reindex semantics. Add focused fp-frame tests for matching another frame's row order, NaN fill for missing labels, and duplicate-index rejection through the existing reindex guard. Validate with targeted fp-frame tests through rch plus check/clippy/fmt and UBS on the touched file.
