title: [test] metamorphic: fp-groupby order-invariant aggregations (reverse-rows)
description: Order-insensitive groupby aggregations (sum/count/min/max/prod/nunique) must be invariant to input ROW ORDER. Add a deterministic (seeded LCG, no rand-dep, no mocks) metamorphic test: build random Int64 key+value Series, compute each aggregation, then recompute on the row-REVERSED keys+values, and assert identical output index labels AND values (sorted keys are the same set; these aggregations are commutative). Complements the value-oracle tests (k3zcv/ybda2/xnbl7) by validating grouping correctness is independent of presentation order. first/last excluded (order-dependent by design). Test-only; fp-groupby.
close_reason: batch-verified-green: fp-groupby suite 77 passed/0 failed (ybda2 test now value-compares prod; Float64-vs-Int64 prod dtype divergence tracked in br-frankenpandas-rl25i)

title: fp-frame SeriesGroupBy::idxmin + idxmax silently drop Timedelta64 via to_f64()
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14267 SeriesGroupBy::idxmin (and :14314 idxmax) use val.to_f64() which fails on Timedelta64 — best_idx stays None and per-group result is NaN. pandas td_series.groupby(k).idxmin() returns the index label of the per-group min Timedelta.

Surgical: add a Timedelta64 detection branch mirroring the existing Utf8 branch. Sister to br-9kt88 (Series::idxmin), br-41rnd (DataFrameGroupBy::idxmin), br-tgm2i (groupby min).
close_reason: done

title: [test] differential harness: fp-index affine Int64 lookup fast paths == materialized
description: Recent fp-index commits fast-pathed lookup methods on affine Int64 ranges: searchsorted (ymhb6, arithmetic instead of binary search), asof (1851g/4jr8s), get_loc/position (affine.position O(1) arithmetic). These have subtle semantics (side=left/right insertion points, asof last<=key, absent vs present keys) and no cross-cutting differential test. Add a deterministic (seeded LCG, no rand, no mocks) harness comparing an ASCENDING affine Int64 index (Index::new_known_unique_int64_affine_range => arithmetic fast path) against the materialized equivalent (Index::new of IndexLabel::Int64 => binary-search/linear fallback) over many random query values spanning below-min / between-elements / exact-match / above-max: searchsorted(left & right), asof, get_loc, position, get_indexer. Asserts the fast path agrees with the trusted fallback for every query. Test-only.
close_reason: Added deterministic differential harness int64_affine_lookup_fast_paths_match_materialized_q70pn (seeded LCG, no rand, no mocks): 2000 ascending affine Int64 ranges, querying every integer across [min-2..max+2] (below-min/exact/between/above-max), asserting affine arithmetic fast path == materialized binary-search/linear fallback for get_loc/position/asof/searchsorted(left&right)/get_indexer. Validates searchsorted (ymhb6), asof (1851g/4jr8s), affine get_loc lookups. Result: PASS, no divergence. Verified: targeted 1 passed, full fp-index 421 passed/0 failed. Artifact: tests/artifacts/perf/q70pn_index_affine_lookup_differential.txt

title: [mock-code-finder] Series/DataFrame diff silently returns NaN for Timedelta64 input
description:
Skill: /mock-code-finder — silent stub via to_f64-else-NaN.

fp-frame Series::diff (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:5752) uses to_f64() on each element and silently produces NaN when to_f64 fails. Scalar::Timedelta64 fails to_f64, so diff on Timedelta64 Series silently produces all-NaN.

Pandas:
  >>> pd.Series([pd.Timedelta('5h'), pd.Timedelta('3h'), pd.Timedelta('8h')]).diff()
  0          NaT
  1    -1 days +22:00:00  # (3h - 5h)
  2    0 days 05:00:00     # (8h - 3h)

fp-frame: all-NaN.

Same pattern in:
- Series::diff (5752)
- DataFrame::diff (axis=0 — 31590)
- DataFrameGroupBy::diff (36550)
- SeriesGroupBy::diff (13559) — already uses different path, verify

Fix: add Timedelta64 fast path computing Timedelta64(a-b) with NaT propagation.

Tests:
- diff on Timedelta64 Series returns Timedelta64 differences (NaT at position 0).
- NaT propagation.
- existing Int64/Float64 tests remain green.
close_reason: Implemented Timedelta64 fast path in Series::diff, SeriesGroupBy::diff, and DataFrameGroupBy::diff. Commit 0e48b304. Tests: 3 new + 12 existing diff tests pass.

title: PostgreSQL/MySQL database backends
description:
## Background
README states "PostgreSQL/MySQL slices in progress" but only the generic SqlConnection trait with rusqlite backend exists.

## Justification
Production pandas users need PostgreSQL and MySQL support for real workflows.

## Scope
- Implement PostgreSQL backend (via sqlx or tokio-postgres)
- Implement MySQL backend (via sqlx or mysql-async)
- Add connection string parsing
- Integration tests with test containers

## Acceptance Criteria
- `read_sql()` works with PostgreSQL connection
- `read_sql()` works with MySQL connection
- `to_sql()` works with both backends
notes: Implementation CLOSED 2026-05-25. Gauntlet follow-up = PARITY-TEST PG/MySQL read_sql/to_sql/inspector (T5_SQL).
close_reason: done

Add parser/evaluator support for optional integer decimals with focused eval/query coverage.

title: astype(int64) of a finite non-integer float ERRORS instead of truncating toward zero like pandas (1.9->1); blocked by reserved fp-columnar test
close_reason: Fixed: fp-types cast_scalar_owned now truncates finite floats toward zero (1.9->1, -1.9->-1, 2.5->2) and only raises for non-finite/overflow, matching pandas 2.2.3 (NaN handled as missing). fp-types portion landed in 869ceaf7; fp-columnar codifying test (astype_lossy_float_to_int_errors -> astype_finite_float_to_int_truncates_toward_zero) updated in 254013dd. fp-columnar 357/0, fp-types 231/0.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: fp-types cast_scalar_owned (line ~1004) rejects FINITE non-integer float->int with TypeError::LossyFloatToInt (only non-finite + overflow SHOULD error, which pandas agrees with: NaN/inf->int raises IntCastingNaNError). pandas/numpy TRUNCATE toward zero: astype('int64') of [1.9,-1.9,2.5,-2.5]=[1,-1,2,-2]. FIX: drop the '*v != v.trunc()' check; keep '!v.is_finite()' + overflow guards; then '*v as i64' (truncates toward zero). BLOCKER: reserved test fp-columnar:13477 astype_lossy_float_to_int_errors casts Float64(1.5)->Int64 and asserts ColumnError::Type — it CODIFIES the bug (pandas truncates 1.5->1). Needs cod to update that test (Series.astype->Column::new->cast_scalar_owned). Sister to the bool fix shipped this turn (nonzero->True, which had NO reserved test codifying it).

title: perf: unsorted Int64 Index.value_counts typed dense-histogram — 9.9x low-card
description: value_counts_raw used cloned-key FxHashMap<IndexLabel,usize> with double-hash per row (contains_key + entry). Added value_counts_raw_i64 (dense direct-address histogram / inline FxHashMap<i64,usize>, first-seen + stable count sort). Low-card (100 distinct, 1M): 13.5->1.36ms (9.9x, now 2.5x FASTER than pandas 3.47ms). High-card (~900k distinct): 1.06x (sort-bound, marginal). Golden-identical (391 tests + golden_value_counts_idx_i64 asc+desc across skewed/heavy-dups/unique/all-same/neg/sparse/empty/extremes).
close_reason: Shipped e687b3b9: value_counts 9.9x low-card, now 2.5x faster than pandas

title: [test] differential: DataFrame sum/mean/min/max axis=0 per column
description: No-mock differential for DataFrame per-column reductions. Seeded LCG (no rand, no mocks): 2-column Int64 frame; assert sum/mean/min/max (axis=0) produce per-column reductions indexed by column name, vs independent oracles (sum exact Int64, mean f64, min/max Int64). Test-only; fp-frame.

title: [perf][no-gaps] parallelize MSD byte-radix top-level buckets — push str_groupby/str_sort past 2.0
description: After sort-based str-groupby grouping (b969de8a, prk0a), the MSD byte-radix (utf8_msd_argsort_bytes) is the dominant cost (~1.47x achieved; radix is the floor). The top-level (depth 0) byte-bucket partition produces up to 256 INDEPENDENT sub-ranges, each recursively sorted into a DISJOINT [b_lo,b_hi) slice of idx+aux — embarrassingly parallel with split_at_mut, no shared mutable state across buckets. LEVER: after the depth-0 counting scatter, spawn a thread pool (std::thread::scope, available_parallelism, morsel of buckets) over the non-trivial top-level buckets; each thread owns its idx/aux sub-slice (use split_at_mut to hand out disjoint &mut). Gate on n (e.g. >=1<<16) so small sorts stay serial. Bit-identical: same per-bucket recursion, just concurrent; buckets write disjoint ranges. Benefits str_groupby (sum/var/first/last) AND str_sort/str_series_sort (same radix). Target >=2.0 on str_groupby_sum. fp-columnar. Cross-check golden 2691faae unchanged.
close_reason: Landed c6a7bdef main+master: parallel MSD radix fan-out. str_sort 1.46x/str_series_sort 1.42x/str_groupby_sum 1.30x bit-identical (parallel-path goldens @50k unchanged), fp-columnar 386 + fp-frame 2830 green. Amdahl-limited 16-way at 500k; nested per-bucket parallelism = next step.

title: [perf] All-numeric to_csv fast path — skip per-column Scalar materialization (est ~1.5-2x)
description: write_csv_string_with_options (fp-io ~1726) is row-major: per cell it calls column.value(row_idx) which (for lazy typed columns) materializes the whole Vec<Scalar> via OnceLock on first touch (~n Scalar/col), then scalar_to_csv_cell allocates a String per cell. For a frame whose columns are all all-valid Int64/Float64/Bool, that Scalar materialization (~128MB for 1M x8 f64) is pure overhead. LEVER: an all-numeric fast path — pre-extract each column via as_f64_slice()/as_i64_slice()/as_bool (no Scalar), and either (a) keep the csv crate's write_record but source fields from the typed slices formatting with the SAME v.to_string() (avoids only the Scalar mat, ~1.3x, GUARANTEED byte-identical since csv crate unchanged), or (b) bypass the csv crate entirely and write directly into the output String (write!(buf,"{v}")/itoa/ryu + delimiter + terminator), which also drops the per-row Vec<String> + per-cell String + csv-crate quoting checks (~2x, but must reproduce the csv crate's EXACT bytes — terminator (\n vs \r\n), na_rep for NaN-Float64 -> empty, Bool -> True/False, header). Numeric fields never need CSV quoting so (b) is parity-safe for the cells; quote the header via the existing path or only take the fast path when names are quote-free. PROOF: golden = FNV/sha256 of the FULL output must be byte-identical to the current writer (bench_csv_write.rs already prints SMALL_FNV/BIG_FNV); if bytes differ, fix until identical or abandon. Same-command A/B on a 500k x6 numeric frame, Score>=2.0 for path (b). Formatter is v.to_string() (shortest round-trip = pandas str(float)), confirmed in scalar_to_csv. bench_csv_write.rs committed-able harness exists in fp-conformance/examples (uncommitted). NOTE: rch task .output capture has been flaky/truncating this session — run the bench so its digest line survives (single line).
close_reason: Shipped 8b3ca76f (main+master). All-numeric to_csv fast path: read as_f64_slice/as_i64_slice + write_pandas_float into the output buffer, skipping per-column Vec<Scalar> materialization + per-cell String + Vec<String> rows + csv-crate machinery. Same-binary A/B 500k x6 numeric, min-of-5: 282.9 -> 151.2 ms/iter = 1.87x (meets the ~1.5-2x bead target). BYTE-IDENTICAL: write_csv_string output FNV unchanged (big 54eeed96e3829bd3 / small 690ef22569464a84); fp-io csv tests 123 pass. write_pandas_float writes {:?} directly (Python str(float) form) so the faster formatter stays byte-for-byte identical. Ceiling for byte-identity is ~1.87x (remaining half is the unavoidable float->string format; ryu would diverge).

title: fp-frame DataFrame::corr + cov silently exclude Timedelta64 columns
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:28335 corr_candidate_columns filters to Bool/Int64/Float64 only; Timedelta64 columns are silently excluded from the result matrix. Plus :28359 col_data uses to_f64().unwrap_or(NaN) which produces NaN for every Timedelta cell.

pandas df.corr() / df.cov() on Timedelta columns returns f64 correlations using ns ordering. Surgical: include Timedelta64 in corr_candidate_columns, and in col_data map Scalar::Timedelta64(ns) → *ns as f64 before falling through. Sister to br-xekug/of7v2 (Series corr/cov Timedelta).
close_reason: done

title: Timedelta::format negative values: FP emits '-0 days 00:00:01' (abs components); pandas uses Python borrow form '-1 days +23:59:59'

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: FIXED in 0e9a94ab. Timedelta::format now computes components via div_euclid/rem_euclid (floor division) so negatives use the Python borrow form: -1s -> '-1 days +23:59:59' (was '-0 days 00:00:01'), with '+' separator when days<0. Positive output unchanged. Test timedelta_format_negative_uses_python_borrow_form covers -1s/-1day/-25h/-1.5s/-500ns/-1ns vs pandas 2.2.3. fp-types 228/0. NOTE: the component ACCESSORS (Timedelta::days/seconds/microseconds) still use abs-based math (days(-1s)=0 vs pandas -1) — separate latent divergence if those are exposed; format() now independent of them.

title: [mock-code-finder] fp-types nanargmax/nanargmin silently skip Timedelta64 (returns wrong argmax position)
description:
fp-types/src/lib.rs:~1781-1820 — nanargmax/nanargmin call v.to_f64() in the comparison loop. Timedelta64.to_f64() errors, so Timedelta64 values are silently skipped. argmax/argmin on a Timedelta64 Series returns None (or the wrong position).

Pandas: td_series.argmax()/argmin() return the position of the largest/smallest Timedelta64 by ns comparison.

Surgical fix: add a Timedelta64 fast path before the f64 comparison loop, comparing the i64 ns representation directly (mirroring br-yic5m nanmin/nanmax pattern). NaT is already filtered by v.is_missing().

Tests: nanargmax([Timedelta(1h), Timedelta(3h), Timedelta(2h)]) == Some(1); nanargmin same shape.

Continues br-naeja/dj6rv/q82t4/mcu90/e607u/yic5m/620mj/x0x91/j8ntk/5djk7 Timedelta64 family.
close_reason: Added Timedelta64 i64-ns compare path to nanargmax/nanargmin. Combo commit 7140bc35. 175 fp-types tests pass.

title: Add DatetimeIndex tz_localize/tz_convert (UTC pass-through with reject-closed elsewhere)

FrankenPandas's underlying Datetime64 storage is naive UTC; full timezone metadata is deferred. Add as: tz_localize('UTC') returns clone (no-op pass-through since storage is already UTC), other timezones reject with a typed compatibility error referencing the deferred-tz roadmap. tz_convert always rejects since the source must be tz-aware. Single PR with regression tests covering: tz_localize('UTC') returns Self (clone), tz_localize('US/Eastern') rejects, tz_convert('UTC') rejects.
close_reason: Shipped in 70c19f1: DatetimeIndex now exposes pandas-parity tz_localize (UTC pass-through, others reject) and tz_convert (always rejects on tz-naive source). 1 regression test passes; 306 fp-index tests total.

title: [mock-code-finder] fp-groupby groupby_sum silently drops Timedelta64 values (returns Float64 0 instead of summing deltas)
description:
fp-groupby/src/lib.rs:257 (groupby_sum_with_global_allocator) and :314 (groupby_sum_with_arena) accumulate group sums via f64:
```rust
if let Ok(v) = value.to_f64() {
    entry.1 += v;
}
```
Timedelta64.to_f64() returns NonNumericValue, so this `if let Ok` swallows every Timedelta64 input. groupby_sum on a Timedelta64 column produces all-zero Float64 output for every group.

Pandas: df.groupby("g")["dur"].sum() on a Timedelta64 series returns a Timedelta64 Series of per-group sums.

Fix is more invasive than diff/pct_change: need a typed accumulator path that detects Timedelta64 input, sums in i64 ns, and emits Scalar::Timedelta64. Likely needs a `groupby_sum_timedelta64_path()` function and dispatch from groupby_sum_with_trace based on values dtype.

Priority lowered to P3 (low) because this requires real refactoring, not the surgical Timedelta64 fast-path pattern used in diff/pct_change. Other similar gaps likely exist in groupby_mean / groupby_min / groupby_max for Timedelta64.

Tests: groupby_sum_timedelta64_returns_timedelta_sums, NaT propagation behavior (probably matches pandas: skip NaT by default).
close_reason: done

title: [perf] Typed all-valid f64 fast path for Series.rolling apply_rolling (sum/mean/var/std) — no per-window Vec alloc
description: apply_rolling rebuilt a fresh Vec<f64> per window via window_values (filter_map over Scalar::to_f64). title: [perf] Register-blocked corr/cov small-panel kernel
description:
Profile-backed target: no ready perf beads are available; active perf ownership is typed-columnar/storage by RubyGoose and fp-index/groupby equality by BlackThrush. The non-overlapping frame residual remains df_corr/df_cov in fp-frame: RCH reprofile after br-frankenpandas-dce50 shows df_corr 5000x10 mean 115.98 ms and df_cov 119.44 ms; repeated pre-row-fused baselines show roughly 111-118 ms. Prior dot-marginal, branchless all-present, and full row-fused accumulator-array levers were rejected or insufficient, so this pass must use a different primitive.

Alien primitive: safe-Rust small-panel/register-blocked dense moment kernel for corr/cov. One lever only: add a complete-data fast path that works in small column tiles and keeps pair moments in local scalar/register variables for each tile, then writes final cells once. Do not use full n*n accumulator arrays. Preserve original row order within each pair, transpose swapped-finalize behavior, min_periods, row/column order, floating-point bits, and NaN fallback. Fallback remains the current pairwise loop for any NaN/missing value.

Proof obligations: RCH baseline first using current HEAD; golden sha256 for df_corr and df_cov before/after; paired RCH-built hyperfine A/B for df_corr 5000x10 and df_cov 5000x10; focused fp-frame corr/cov tests/check/clippy/fmt/UBS as feasible. Keep only Score>=2.0; otherwise remove source hunk, close as rejected, and record evidence.
close_reason: Rejected register-blocked corr/cov small-panel kernel: RCH-built paired hyperfine showed a hard regression. df_corr 5000x10 before 110.9 ms +/- 6.4, after 245.8 ms +/- 14.9; df_cov before 114.7 ms +/- 5.9, after 243.0 ms +/- 7.0. Golden outputs were byte-identical (corr sha256 6f577ab82a9d5e845a81757e6bf2488425011bfa02252553900406fc27f0c80c; cov sha256 beb4e3f5011ca6bf5ca53046415118ca71e958037c31fdab574fd3e0b2ef8d99), but Score <2.0. Source hunk removed; fp-frame diff empty and cargo fmt -p fp-frame -- --check passed. This rejects further local pair-loop tiling; the next corr/cov primitive should replace the storage/extraction shape with a typed contiguous SoA moment/dot kernel or packed f64 view cache, coordinated with the typed-columnar owner.

title: Add DatetimeIndex is_month/quarter/year start/end boundary accessors
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): DatetimeIndex is missing six boundary-position accessors. Mirrors the day-of-X pattern shipped in br-k860x.

Add six pandas-named methods:
- is_month_start -> True when day == 1.
- is_month_end -> True when day == days_in_calendar_month.
- is_quarter_start -> True when month in {1,4,7,10} and day == 1.
- is_quarter_end -> True when month in {3,6,9,12} and day == days_in_calendar_month.
- is_year_start -> True when month == 1 and day == 1.
- is_year_end -> True when month == 12 and day == 31.

Each returns Vec<Option<bool>> aligned to DatetimeIndex labels via map_datetime_labels. Single PR. Add regression tests at every relevant boundary (Jan 1, Jan 31, Mar 31, Apr 1, Jun 30, Jul 1, Sep 30, Oct 1, Dec 31, Dec 32 → next year), Feb leap-year edge (Feb 29 in 2024 vs Feb 28 in 2023), and NAT propagation.
close_reason: Shipped in 6990193: DatetimeIndex now exposes pandas-parity is_month_start/is_month_end/is_quarter_start/is_quarter_end/is_year_start/is_year_end. 2 regression tests pass; 261 fp-index tests total.

title: [mock-code-finder] concat_dataframes loses index name even when all frames share it (pandas preserves shared name)
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:20839 and :20996 — DataFrame concat helpers build the result index via `Index::new(labels)` without preserving the source index name.

Pandas: when all concatenated frames share the same index.name, that name is preserved on the result. If names differ, pandas drops the name (None).

Surgical fix: at both call sites, compute the shared name and pass to rename_index:
```rust
let shared_name: Option<&str> = frames
    .first()
    .and_then(|f| f.index().name())
    .filter(|n| frames.iter().all(|f| f.index().name() == Some(*n)));
let index = Index::new(labels).rename_index(shared_name);
```

When ignore_index=true (the integer-range path), name is None — leave that case alone.

Tests: concat two frames sharing index.name "x" → result.index.name() == Some("x"); concat with different names → None.
close_reason: Applied shared-name detection + rename_index in both concat helper functions. Commit 03556392. 1932 fp-frame tests pass.

AXIS-ORDERING PARITY SWEEP COMPLETE: pivot_table (8f2c2737) + pivot + crosstab all sort like pandas.

description: Re-measured 2026-06-06 on db492a03: inner_join 100000 rows cardinality 512 (19.5M output rows) = FP 957ms vs pandas 1234ms — ahead but thin vs the 5-7x leads elsewhere. PROFILE FIRST (perf record inner_join 100000 10). Expected hotspot: single-threaded dense output build of 19.5M rows x4 columns. Candidate primitives: parallel per-column output fill (proven pattern from CSV merge e595e700 — column buffers are independent), or partitioned build+probe with per-partition output ranges computed by prefix-sum so each partition writes its slice concurrently (communication-avoiding, order preserved by construction). Proof gate: golden join_1to1+inner_join shas, fp-join suite, interleaved paired internal-timer bench (load-aware, minimums), Score>=2.0 or record rejection.

Labels identical on materialization; unique+AscendingInt64 cache pre-seeds provably correct for 0..N. Profile showed 19.5M-label Vec build (2.9% CPU) + drop (3.9%) + ~312MB/iter alloc traffic. Goldens inner_join be7d1711 / join_1to1 18988b22 byte-identical; fp-join 100/0, fp-frame merge 5/0, conformance green. 6/6 stable interleaved pairs: 703->413ms min = 1.70x; FP now 3.0x faster than pandas 2.2.3 on the bead shape (was 1.29x). Score 4x5/1=20 KEEP. Residual: build_single_key_dense_i64_inner_merge_output 24% CPU — parallel per-column fill is the routed follow-up.

title: [bug] cov(s,s) != var(s) at ddof=1 for large near-equal values (one-pass cancellation)
description: proptest prop_cov_self_equals_var_at_ddof_one intermittently fails: cov_with_options uses one-pass (Sxy - Sx*Sy/n)/(n-1) which catastrophically cancels for large-magnitude near-equal data, while var() is two-pass (mean-centered, stable). Repro: s=[Float64(-809259.015408116), Float64(-808751.0970670774)] index [0,1] -> cov(s,s)-var(s) ~1e-9 relative > tol. PRE-EXISTING (not from n7ufr typed cov — typed path == its own Scalar fallback; the one-pass FORMULA is the issue). FIX: switch cov to two-pass mean-centered accumulation (matches var, stable) — verify vs cov goldens. Latent/flaky: proptest finds it with some probability per 100-case run.
notes: 2026-06-10 (2375d2e2): FIXED. Series.cov switched from one-pass (sum_xy - n*mean_x*mean_y)/(n-ddof) to two-pass mean-centered cross-sum Σ(x-mean_x)(y-mean_y)/(n-ddof) in new cov_centered_cross_sum (preserves exact pair selection + iteration order of old path; typed Float64 + Scalar fallback). cov(s,s) now == var(s) (both two-pass). MORE pandas-faithful: np.cov (pandas nancov delegate) is itself two-pass. corr() untouched (uses cov_components). Removed dead cov_pair_sums. Verified: prop_cov_self_equals_var_at_ddof_one @20k cases, 32 cov + 49 corr unit tests, 8 cov conformance packets, cov goldens unchanged; new deterministic regression test series_cov_self_equals_var_large_near_equal_r3sfc pins the repro. NOTE follow-up: cov_components (corr's internal path) still one-pass — same latent cancellation, but corr goldens currently green; separate bead if corr stability becomes an issue.
close_reason: done
