title: [test] differential: Series.value_counts counts + descending order
description: No-mock differential for Series.value_counts. Seeded LCG (no rand, no mocks): random all-valid Int64 series (small range => ties); assert value_counts output has exactly the distinct values as labels with counts equal to an independent HashMap tally, counts are non-increasing (sorted desc), and total counts == n. Guards value_counts correctness (the op is hashmap/perf-sensitive). Test-only; fp-frame.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index asi8/strftime/microseconds/nanoseconds
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): IndexVariants is missing the underlying integer accessors (asi8) and the sub-day breakdown (microseconds/nanoseconds), plus DatetimeIndex.strftime.

Add five methods:
- DatetimeIndex.asi8 -> Vec<i64> — underlying nanoseconds-since-epoch with NAT preserved as i64::MIN.
- TimedeltaIndex.asi8 -> Vec<i64> — underlying nanosecond duration with NAT preserved as Timedelta::NAT.
- TimedeltaIndex.microseconds -> Vec<Option<i64>> — (nanos rem_euclid NANOS_PER_SEC) / 1_000 (0..=999_999).
- TimedeltaIndex.nanoseconds -> Vec<Option<i64>> — nanos rem_euclid 1_000 (0..=999).
- DatetimeIndex.strftime(format) -> Vec<Option<String>> — chrono format() applied per non-NAT label, NAT collapses to None.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: asi8 round-trips, microseconds/nanoseconds for a (1 day + 2:34:56.789012345) input, NAT propagation, and strftime with %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%.3f format.
close_reason: Shipped in 8c7c856: DatetimeIndex now exposes pandas-parity asi8/strftime; TimedeltaIndex now exposes pandas-parity asi8/microseconds/nanoseconds. 3 regression tests pass; 267 fp-index tests total.

title: [test-harness] live-oracle str_prefix Unicode transport: removeprefix/removesuffix exactness tests diverge under forced system-pandas (FP correct)
close_reason: FIXED in 7e71536a. Root cause: OracleRequest (fp-conformance/src/lib.rs) omitted str_prefix/str_suffix, so the live oracle never received the prefix/suffix and returned the series unchanged (wrong expected). Added both fields to the struct + payload build (mirroring regex_pattern); with the oracle handlers from 486fd76a, the oracle now computes the real pandas result. Verified: full live-oracle gauntlet vs system pandas 2.2.3 = 834/834 (was 832/834). FrankenPandas str.removeprefix/removesuffix confirmed byte-exact correct; it was a harness transport gap, not an FP bug.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Found running the gauntlet (FP_ALLOW_SYSTEM_PANDAS_FALLBACK=1 cargo test -p fp-conformance live_oracle) vs system pandas 2.2.3: 832/834 live-oracle tests PASS. The 2 reds are live_oracle_series_str_removeprefix_unicode_exactness and ...removesuffix... FP is CORRECT: source uses Rust escapes, value0=U+00E9clair (precomposed), str_prefix=U+00E9 (precomposed); strip_prefix removes -> clair, which is exactly what pandas does for matching code points (verified directly). But the oracle returns expected=eclair (no removal), meaning str_prefix arrives at the Python oracle with different normalization than value0 (transport corrupts/normalizes str_prefix only). Root cause is in the fp-conformance live-oracle request serialization, NOT fp-frame removeprefix (fp-frame:21390 uses str::strip_prefix, byte-exact = pandas). These tests set allow_system_pandas_fallback=false so they SKIP in normal CI; only the forced gauntlet surfaces it. Fix: ensure str_prefix/str_suffix reach pandas_oracle.py with identical bytes to the series values (no NFC/NFD). All non-reserved fp-conformance. See memory live-oracle-gauntlet.

title: [test] metamorphic: diff is inverse of cumsum
description: No-mock metamorphic: diff(cumsum(s))[i]==s[i] for i>=1 (and [0] missing), since cumsum then diff recovers the original increments. Seeded LCG, Int64 series incl negatives. Test-only; fp-frame.

title: [mock-code-finder] SeriesGroupBy::min/max silently NaN Timedelta64 (same agg_numeric path as c1bxu)
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs SeriesGroupBy::min and SeriesGroupBy::max both delegate to agg_numeric for non-Utf8 columns. agg_numeric drops Timedelta64 values via to_f64, so group min/max returns Null(NaN) instead of Timedelta64 extrema.

Pandas: td_series.groupby(...).min()/.max() return Timedelta64.

Surgical fix: add a Timedelta64 fast path branch before delegating to agg_numeric. Use existing column_is_timedelta detector (just added in c1bxu). Track per-group min/max of i64 ns. Emit Scalar::Timedelta64. NaT skipped.

Tests: SeriesGroupBy::min/max on Timedelta64 returns Timedelta64 extrema per group.
close_reason: Added agg_timedelta_extrema helper for SeriesGroupBy::min/max. Commit 2505393b. 1915 fp-frame tests pass.

Likely recent fp-types Display/PeriodFreq changes (pandas 2.x renamed annual alias A->Y; FP may have updated Display to pandas semantics while the README tests pin the OLD behavior, or vice versa). Verify each side vs pandas 2.2.3 + README text, fix whichever is wrong.

title: [perf] Build Float64 isnan/isfinite/isinf masks from raw buffers
description: Column::isnan/isfinite/isinf currently materialize Scalar values and build Vec<Scalar::Bool>. Add typed Float64 fast paths over raw buffers plus validity masks, preserving NaN/null semantics while avoiding source scalar materialization.

title: [perf] Arc-share LazyAllValid Int64/Float64/Bool buffers — O(1) typed-numeric column clone
description: Extends the utf8 oifvy Arc-clone to the numeric typed buffers. LazyAllValid{Int64,Float64,Bool} held owned Vec, so Column::clone deep-copied O(n); the buffers are immutable after construction so cloning Arc-shares them O(1) (observationally a deep copy). Column::clone of typed numeric columns is hot in groupby/concat/align/copy (cf the 8.7x groupby win 85431093 from preserving typed backing across clone). Bench 2M-elem column, 500 clones: i64 0.7913->0.000026 ms/clone, f64 0.7604->0.000026 ms/clone (~30000x). Golden FNV over materialized values identical (clone==orig AND baseline==after). fp-columnar 380 + fp-frame 2829 tests pass. Only ~4 internal as_slice()->as_ref() sites; public as_f64_slice/as_i64_slice sigs unchanged. Also unblocks numeric contiguous-range join views (analogue of jbyuc.1.1.1).
close_reason: Shipped ab90a562 (main+master). LazyAllValid{Int64,Float64,Bool}.data -> Arc<[T]>; Clone Arc-shares O(1). Same-command A/B Column::clone of 2M-elem: i64 0.7913->0.000026 ms, f64 0.7604->0.000026 ms (~30000x). Golden FNV identical (clone==orig, baseline==after). fp-columnar 380 + fp-frame 2829 tests pass. 4 internal as_slice->as_ref; public as_f64/i64_slice sigs unchanged.

title: DataFrame(dtype='boolean') nullable-Boolean constructor accepts non-0/1; pandas is STRICT (error), and dtype='bool' (numpy) stale fixture expects error but pandas succeeds
description: FP-P2D-023 remaining 2 bool cases after 8nupg lossy fix: (1) dataframe_constructor_list_like_dtype_bool_invalid_int_error_strict uses constructor_dtype='boolean' (pandas NULLABLE Boolean) of int 2 -> pandas ERRORS 'Need to pass bool-like values' (strict 0/1 only). FP gives True. Fixture CORRECT, FP wrong. ROOT: parse_constructor_dtype_spec (fp-conformance 14531) maps BOTH 'bool' AND 'boolean' -> DType::Bool (numpy lenient); 'boolean' should map to DType::BoolNullable. AND cast_scalar_owned (fp-types ~996) Bool|BoolNullable arm is lenient (nonzero) for both — split it: BoolNullable strict (Int64 0/1 else InvalidBoolInt, Float64 0.0/1.0 else InvalidBoolFloat; the error variants already exist + match 'expected 0/1 for bool cast from int64 but found {value}'), Bool stays nonzero. This is correct for astype('boolean') too (pandas astype('boolean') of 2 errors). (2) dataframe_from_dict_dtype_bool_invalid_error_hardened uses dtype='bool' (numpy) of [2,0] -> pandas SUCCEEDS [True,False] (nonzero, e426cd40) -> fixture expects error = STALE, regen to expected_frame [True,False]. Part of exyng. VERIFY blast radius: any test asserting astype('boolean')/BoolNullable of non-0/1 -> True would break (intended).
close_reason: Fixed in 2fa20bcf: cast_scalar_owned splits Bool (numpy nonzero) from BoolNullable (pandas nullable Boolean, strict 0/1 -> InvalidBoolInt/InvalidBoolFloat); conformance 'boolean' spec now maps to DType::BoolNullable; numpy-bool from_dict stale fixture regenerated to [True,False]. FP-P2D-023 packet green. fp-types 233/0, fp-columnar 361/0, fp-frame 2800/0, clippy clean. Sister cleanup this turn: FP-P2D-020 dict-of-series fixtures resynced to from_series promotion+dedup (f0873cc1) + e2e groupby_sum Int64 (3b575ea3) -> full fp-conformance suite GREEN (lib 1586/0, ag_e2e 8/0). title: [idea-wizard] build parity acceleration control plane
description:
Idea-wizard output from 2026-05-08 after reviewing the open parity epics, recent closed conformance/profiling/fuzzing/oracle beads, and bv graph state.

Goal: accelerate the existing FrankenPandas parity epics without duplicating their implementation work. The current open graph is mostly independent parity umbrellas (MultiIndex, SeriesGroupBy, Index variants, DataFrame wrappers, IO formats); this epic creates shared infrastructure and evidence workflows that make those epics easier to drain correctly.

Top winnowed ideas:
1. Live pandas API coverage gate plus bead drift reconciler.
2. Differential fixture generator with RaptorQ sidecars for new method slices.
3. AACE semantic witness ledger for index-alignment/materialization boundaries.
4. High-RAM benchmark baseline and execution telemetry for pandas-vs-Franken comparisons.
5. Bead hygiene/progress reconciler for stale umbrella descriptions and child closeout state.
6. Null/NaN metamorphic conformance suite across alignment/groupby/join/reshape.
7. Error compatibility catalog and harness for pandas-observable exceptions.

Acceptance: all children remain non-overlapping with open implementation epics, each child names concrete output artifacts, and bv reports no dependency cycles after creation.

title: Conformance integrity: ~15 of 39 fixture-replay failures are FIXTURE DEFECTS, not subject bugs (gauntlet CONF-META-01)
description: The Python oracle (pandas_oracle.py) implements only a SUBSET of the ~268 FixtureOperation variants live; the rest are fixture-replay-only and were pinned WITHOUT live-pandas validation, so several encode expectations that contradict real pandas 2.2.3. Verified fixture defects (FrankenPandas is CORRECT): isdigit/isdecimal *_unicode_ascii_only_* (FP-P2D-203/224 — pandas returns True for fullwidth/Arabic/superscript digits); str.normalize NFD (FP-P2D-303 — NFD decomposes, fixture stored composed); dt floor/ceil/round/date 'T'-vs-space serialization (FP-P2D-234/240/241/242/311/312/313/322/334 — FP matches str(Timestamp)); total_seconds datetime fixture FP-P2D-245 expects Int64 seconds-since-midnight (pandas datetimes have no total_seconds). Consequence: the green/red gate can fire for the WRONG reason. Highest-leverage fix: bring fixture-replay-only ops under live-oracle dispatch so every expected_* is live-validated, and flag replay-only packets in gate output. Until then, the honest SUBJECT-side conformance is materially better than the 96.88% fixture pass rate implies. See workspace reports/phase11_conformance_classification.md.
close_reason: Fixed all ~15 known fixture defects: isdigit/isdecimal Unicode (FP-P2D-203/224), rolling min_periods (FP-P2D-127), str.normalize NFD (FP-P2D-303), datetime floor/ceil/round/date/to_timestamp serialization (FP-P2D-234/240/241/242/311/312/313/322/334), and dt.total_seconds on datetime (FP-P2D-245). All conformance tests pass (1586 passed).

Verified vs pandas 2.2.3 ([b,a,c]+[c,a] -> [a,b,c]; identical [b,a,c] kept).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Found via align-union audit. pandas pd.DataFrame({'a':s1,'b':s2}) with misaligned series indexes SORTS the union index ([y,x]+[y,x,w] -> [w,x,y]), but pd.concat([s1,s2],axis=1) does NOT (keeps first-appearance [y,x,w]). FP's DataFrame::from_series uses multi_way_align which is FIRST-APPEARANCE order (deliberate, comment says 'matches align_union(sort=False)') — correct for concat axis=1 but WRONG for the DataFrame-dict-of-series constructor. ENTANGLED: from_series is shared by many callers (concat, dt-component frames with identical indexes, conformance builders). Fix needs a SORTED variant for the DataFrame-dict path specifically (or sort at that caller), not changing from_series globally (would break concat). Most callers use identical indexes (0..n) so unaffected; only misaligned dict-of-series diverges. Verified vs live pandas 2.2.3. Low priority / needs caller disambiguation.

title: [perf][EPIC] Kill Vec<Scalar> AoS -> typed contiguous columnar storage (filter/gather/sort numpy-class)
description:
FLAGSHIP vs-upstream gap (orchestrator campaign: no perf gap is acceptable; build our own safe-Rust typed columnar, NOT link C BLAS/numpy). Supersedes/expands piw16.

## Baseline (RubyGoose, release-perf, 2026-06-02, 100k rows x10 Float64)
- filter_bool:     FP 4.868 ms  vs pandas 0.423 ms  = 11.5x   <-- biggest gap
- sort_single:     FP 12.190 ms vs pandas 5.563 ms  = 2.2x
- drop_duplicates: FP 19.506 ms vs pandas 14.941 ms = 1.3x

## Root cause (single, shared)
Column stores `values: Vec<Scalar>` (AoS); Scalar is a 32-byte enum (Utf8(String)/Interval are the wide variants). filter/sort materialize the result by gathering+cloning 32-byte Scalars and rebuilding a Vec<Scalar> output. filter_bool gather = 71% self in Column::take_positions; sort_single routes through the SAME take_rows_by_positions gather (1M x 32B = 32MB/iter). numpy gathers contiguous 8-byte f64.

Confirmed NO contained lever: any typed gather that still emits Vec<Scalar> is net-NEGATIVE (adds an O(n) extraction pass); the output rebuild is the wall. The orchestration layer (take_rows_by_positions / take_positions) is already fully fast-pathed (sfysu / 2a6ln / 8cc83365) -- nothing left at that layer.

## Alien primitives (alien_cs_graveyard.md)
- Struct-of-Arrays contiguous columnar layout.
- SIMD/branchless gather via selection vectors (positions -> std::simd gather on typed buffers, no per-element enum match).
- Section 7.2 cache-oblivious / cache-blocked access for frames larger than LLC.
- forbid(unsafe_code) stays: std::simd / autovectorizable loops only.

## Target architecture
`Column { dtype, data: ColumnData (typed Vec<f64>/Vec<i64>/...), validity: ValidityMask, scalars: OnceCell<Vec<Scalar>> }`. ColumnData already EXISTS (fp-columnar:328) but is only materialized on demand -- make it the source of truth. Hot kernels (take_positions, filter, sort gather, compare) operate on typed data and emit typed-backed Columns with EMPTY scalar cache -> zero Scalar materialization on hot paths. `values() -> &[Scalar]` lazily materializes via OnceCell for the ~5400 legacy callers (compat preserved).

## Blast radius / feasibility
Column.values field is PRIVATE; only 9 direct `Column {` struct literals repo-wide (5 fp-columnar, 3 fp-frame, 1 fp-conformance) + constructors new/from_values. But 334 internal self.values[..] reads in fp-columnar must move to a private typed accessor or typed kernels. All reads (no values_mut / no mutation aliasing) -> lazy materialization is sound.

## Isomorphism hazards (must prove; ABSOLUTE parity)
1. NULL-KIND PRESERVATION -- values() currently returns the exact Scalar::Null(NaN|NaT|Null) per invalid position; typed ColumnData+validity loses null-kind. Reconstruct via dtype canonicalization: take_positions already calls normalize_missing_for_dtype, so nulls are dtype-canonical (Float64->NaN, Datetime64->NaT, Int64->Null, ...). MUST verify this invariant holds at EVERY constructor before relying on it; otherwise store a per-column null-kind (or per-position kind).
2. Float bit pattern: gather copies bits, no arithmetic -> identical.
3. Ordering/tie-break: gather preserves positional order -> identical.
4. Utf8/Interval columns: keep Vec<String>/Vec<Interval> typed; gather clones same as before.

## Phasing (one lever per commit; re-bench each; Score>=2.0 to keep)
- P0 (groundwork): baseline + plan (DONE -- this bead).
- P1: introduce typed-backed Column + OnceCell values() with FULL parity (all crates green + conformance + golden sha256 of filter/sort/gather outputs vs current). No behavior change; sets storage.
- P2: typed std::simd gather in take_positions for Float64/Int64/Bool -> realizes filter_bool + sort_single win. Re-bench; require >=2x.
- P3: extend typed kernels to compare / sort-key / groupby-hash; re-profile.

## Proof protocol per phase
rch crate-scoped build/test; full workspace cargo test; fp-conformance differential vs oracle; golden sha256 of operation outputs captured pre-change must match post-change; hyperfine / perf_profile before+after numbers in commit. Prove isomorphism or revert. Push main + main:master.

Accomplished by accumulated landed work; follow-up depth routed to new beads.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: HAZARD #1 CONFIRMED + LOCKED. normalize_missing_for_dtype (fp-columnar) PRESERVES Null(NaN) and Null(NaT) regardless of dtype; only generic Null(Null) is canonicalized to missing_for_dtype. Column::new skips coercion for DType::Null values. => a Float64/Int64 column can legally store Null(NaT)/Null(NaN), and values()/take_positions reproduce the exact NullKind. CONSEQUENCE for P2: a typed ColumnData + ValidityMask (valid/invalid bit only) is INSUFFICIENT — it must carry a per-position null-kind code (2 bits: NaN/NaT/Null) or values() materialization will diverge. Locked via new characterization test take_positions_preserves_exact_null_kind_contract (fp-columnar) which must keep passing through the migration. Down-payment shipped this turn; P1/P2 are the next levers.

title: [perf] Column::sort_values/argsort typed LSD radix fast path — 2.74x on 5M i64
description: sort_values/argsort sorted Vec<(usize,&Scalar)> with a per-comparison 32B enum-match comparator (compare_scalars_na_last) — the documented AoS floor under the sort_single hotspot. Added a typed fast path: for all-valid Int64/Float64 columns (as_i64_slice/as_f64_slice), build order-preserving u64 radix keys (i64 sign-bit flip; f64 IEEE sortable-bits with -0.0 normalize) and run a stable 8-pass LSD radix argsort (comparison-free, O(n)), then re-ingest typed via from_i64_values/from_f64_values. Descending uses the bitwise-NOT key (preserves tie stability). Falls back to the Scalar comparator for non-numeric/missing columns (na-last semantics unchanged). Isomorphism: 200-trial LCG fuzz (negatives/zero/dups/wide) proves bit-identical to the scalar path for asc+desc sort_values AND argsort. Measured 2.74x (radix 39.2s vs scalar 107.4s, 10x sort of 5M i64) on rch.
close_reason: Shipped 2d42cf62. Typed LSD radix sort_values/argsort. 2.74x (radix 39.2s vs scalar 107.4s, 10x sort 5M i64, rch). Isomorphism: 200-trial fuzz bit-identical asc+desc + argsort. fp-columnar 362/0, fp-frame sort 79/0, clippy clean.

title: [perf] Column::duplicated/drop_duplicates hash-free direct-address dedup — 4.8-12.3x
description: duplicated_keep built a per-value Key enum over &[Scalar] (32B) and hashed with std SipHash; at high cardinality the SwissTable (2M+ entries, 16-32MB) is DRAM-probe-bound (drop_duplicates ~3.8x gap). Replaced with a hash-free DIRECT-ADDRESS table for bounded-range all-valid Int64: one min/max pass, then a dense seen-bit / saturating-count Vec indexed by (v-min), O(n) no probing, L3-resident (cap 16M entries, <=16x rows). Float64 + sparse-range Int64 + non-numeric fall back to a typed FxHashSet / Scalar path (also added, ~1.5-1.8x). Isomorphism: 150-trial brute-force O(n^2) reference fuzz (first/last/none, i64+f64, dup-heavy) bit-identical. Measured (rch, 5M i64 x10, vs old Key-enum+SipHash): card=1000 4.80x, card=2M 12.25x. Child of fi6zx.
close_reason: Shipped cd4efdac. Hash-free direct-address dedup for bounded-range Int64 + FxHash typed fallback. Measured (rch 5M i64 x10 vs old Key-enum+SipHash): card=1000 4.80x, card=2M 12.25x. Isomorphism: 150-trial brute-force fuzz bit-identical (first/last/none, i64+f64). fp-columnar 363/0, fp-frame drop_duplicates 18/0, clippy clean.

Exposed pub Column::argsort_with(ascending) (typed LSD radix for all-valid Int64/Float64, Scalar na-last comparator fallback) and routed Series::sort_values_na through it when non-categorical AND all-valid (as_i64/f64_slice Some) — na_position is moot with no missing, and the non-missing comparison (cmp/partial_cmp) is identical so output is bit-identical. Measured user-facing Series.sort_values 2.40x (radix 57.6s vs scalar-cmp 138.3s, 10x sort 5M i64, incl shared reorder_by_positions). fp-frame sort 79/0. NOTE: 5 PRE-EXISTING stale-golden failures (series_keys/to_series_dict Debug-format drift) confirmed red on clean HEAD, unrelated. Child of fi6zx.

title: [perf] Series.isin hash-free bitset probe + typed bool output — 4.7-5.1x
description: Series::isin built an IsinIndex (std SipHash HashSet) and emitted Vec<Scalar::Bool> (32B/elem). For all-valid Int64 self + all-Int64 needles in a bounded span, replaced with (a) a dense membership bitset (hash-free, 1 load/elem) and (b) typed Bool output via Column::from_bool_values (1B/elem). isin was OUTPUT-bound at large n: the bitset probe ALONE was only 1.5x; adding the typed-bool output cut (32B→1B) lifted it to 4.7-5.1x. Bit-identical: with only Int64 needles IsinIndex::contains(Int64)==ints.contains, and from_bool_values yields the same Bool column as from_values over Scalar::Bool. Isomorphism: 150-trial Vec-scan reference fuzz (incl out-of-span self + dup needles). Measured (rch 5M i64 x10, full path): needles=64 4.74x, needles=100k 5.07x. Mixed/float/non-Int64 needles fall back to IsinIndex. Child of fi6zx.

title: [perf] Series.isna/notna validity-mask read + typed bool output — 7.1x
description: Series::isna/notna materialized the Scalar vector and tested is_missing per element, emitting Vec<Scalar::Bool> (32B/elem). Replaced with a direct read of the Column ValidityMask (bit-packed, dtype-agnostic) + typed Bool output via from_bool_values (1B/elem), with an all-valid fast path (validity.all() -> all-false/all-true). title: [perf] DataFrameGroupBy dense var/std (two-pass, ddof=1)
description: Extended the dense DataFrameGroupBy aggregation to var/std. Both passes over the typed slice (no Scalar gather/hashing). Float64 and Int64 value columns (nanvar coerces Int64→f64 via collect_finite, so v as f64 matches). Single bounded int key. Bit-identical (verified to_bits in fuzz: var bit-exact, std = sqrt(var) bit-exact, singleton groups → Null). title: [perf] column merge: dense direct-address join index for single bounded-int key
description: merge_dataframes_on_with_options builds FxHashMap<&CompositeJoinKey, bucket> on the build side and probes per row — hashing on build (n_right) + probe (n_left). comments.author: cc-pandas
comments.text: Investigated + REJECTED the isolated dense inner-probe lever (cc-pandas). Implemented dense_int64_inner_positions (CSR direct-address, fills the dup-key gap where ordered_unique returns None) — isomorphic (existing jdupk fuzz + 92 fp-join tests pass). BUT positions-only A/B (2Mx2M, rch): card=200k dense 12.8s vs FxHashMap-count 10.5s = 0.82x (dense SLOWER at high cardinality — CSR's 2 right-passes + flat alloc lose vs single hashmap build when the table is large/DRAM-bound, mirroring the dedup high-card lesson). KEY FINDING: the inner-join PROBE is NOT the merge bottleneck — the full merge is dominated by OUTPUT MATERIALIZATION (reindex/gather of N output rows x all columns). So a dense probe gives ~neutral full-merge change at high card (possible slight regression) and only wins at low cardinality. Reverted to avoid an ambiguous/regressing perf claim. REAL lever for merge perf = the materialization/reindex path (gather output columns by left/right position vectors — typed gather like Column::take_positions), NOT the probe. Existing dense_int64_left/right/outer helpers use the same range<=4*rows gate; they're only net-beneficial because materialization dominates and low-card wins. NEXT: profile merge materialization (build_single_key_inner_merge_output / reindex) for the typed-gather lever.

title: [mock-code-finder] Series::clip_with_series silently no-ops on Datetime64 input
description:
Skill: /mock-code-finder — silent stub via to_f64-else-passthrough.

After br-frankenpandas-h7bkw added a Timedelta64 fast path, the same gap remains for Datetime64. Series::clip_with_series (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:5068) still falls through to 'out.push(val.clone()); continue;' when val.to_f64() fails. Scalar::to_f64 for Datetime64 returns TypeError::NonNumericValue (well, actually let me check), so Datetime64 input is silently passed through.

Pandas:
  >>> s = pd.Series(pd.to_datetime(['2024-01-15', '2024-06-01']))
  >>> lower = pd.Series(pd.to_datetime(['2024-03-01', '2024-03-01']))
  >>> s.clip(lower=lower)
  0   2024-03-01
  1   2024-06-01

fp-frame would return the input unchanged on Datetime64.

Sister to br-h7bkw (Timedelta64) and br-98b6e (Utf8). Same shape: add a Datetime64 fast path before the to_f64 fallback. NAT bounds (i64::MIN) should be treated as missing.

Tests:
- clip_with_series on Datetime64 with Datetime64 lower bound clips to bound when below.
- clip_with_series on Datetime64 with Datetime64 upper bound clips to bound when above.
- clip_with_series on Datetime64 with NAT bound passes through unclipped (same row).
- existing numeric/Utf8/Timedelta64 tests remain green.
close_reason: Invalid bead: Scalar enum has no Datetime64 variant. Datetime values are stored as Utf8 (ISO strings), already covered by the Utf8 fast path (br-98b6e).

title: [mock-code-finder] Series::drop loses index name (same pattern as lxot8 dropna fix)
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:4204 — Series::drop calls Self::from_values, which builds a fresh unnamed Index. Same pattern as lxot8 dropna, which I already fixed for that method.

Pandas: Series.drop preserves index name (and all other index metadata):
> s.set_axis(['a','b','c']); s.index.name='myidx'; s.drop(['a']).index.name == 'myidx'

Surgical fix: replace line 4204 with:
```rust
let index = Index::new(new_labels).rename_index(self.index.name());
let column = Column::from_values(new_values)?;
Self::new(self.name.clone(), index, column)
```

Tests: series_drop_preserves_index_name_<bead>. Existing drop tests stay green.
close_reason: Mirrored lxot8 fix in Series::drop. Index built via rename_index, Self::new direct construction. Commit 6709e6a7. 1909 fp-frame tests pass.

title: [parity] interpolate(method='index'/'values'/'time') unsupported (only linear/nearest/pad/zero)
description: Series.interpolate_method errored on method='index'/'values'/'time'. pandas does LINEAR interpolation weighted by the actual index coordinates (not integer position), so irregularly-spaced gaps fill by real distance — e.g. [1,NaN,NaN,4] idx [0,1,3,7] -> [1,1.4286,2.2857,4] not [1,2,3,4]. Add the arm: x-coords from Int64/Datetime64/Timedelta64 index labels; edge handling matches linear (leading stays NaN, trailing forward-carries). Verified vs pandas 2.2.3.
close_reason: Added interpolate method='index'/'values'/'time': linear interpolation weighted by index coordinates (Int64/Datetime64/Timedelta64 labels -> x-axis), edge handling matches linear (leading NaN, trailing forward-carry). Non-numeric index errors clearly. Verified vs 6-case pandas 2.2.3 oracle (irregular spacing, leading/trailing, regular index==linear). 18 interpolate tests green, clippy clean.

title: csv parse_dates: mixed naive+tz-aware column should stay object, not coerce to Datetime64
description: read_csv(parse_dates=['ts']) on a column mixing naive and tz-aware timestamps (e.g. '2024-01-15 10:30:00' + '2024-01-15T10:30:00Z') — pandas 2.2.3 canNOT unify these into datetime64[ns] so it keeps the column as OBJECT dtype (a Series of mixed Timestamp objects: naive '2024-01-15 10:30:00' and tz-aware '2024-01-15 10:30:00+00:00'). FrankenPandas instead coerces the whole column to typed Datetime64(ns), losing the naive-vs-tz distinction => the sole remaining fp-conformance failure (packet_filter_runs_csv_read_frame_parse_dates_mixed_timezone, FP-P2D-432-ish). The combined-columns parse_dates case (uniform naive) was FIXED (1164bc42, now typed Datetime64 matching pandas). Fix: fp-io csv parse_dates must detect heterogeneous tz-awareness within a column and fall back to object/string repr (NOT Datetime64) to match pandas. fp-frame's Datetime64 tz is opaque-metadata anyway (Phase-3 per 0ezw7), so it can't represent mixed-tz. Niche but real. After this, fp-conformance = 0 fp-frame-caused failures (1595/1595).
close_reason: FIXED 0bf95014. Added ToDatetimeOptions.mixed_tz_as_object (default false). When csv/sql parse_dates detects a mixed naive+tz-aware string column (values_have_mixed_timezone), normalize each value to its pandas str(Timestamp) form (normalize_mixed_timezone_values: naive 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS', aware '...+HH:MM' via existing format_naive_datetime/format_aware_datetime) and keep the column as object, instead of the prior self-inconsistent coercion (naive->Datetime64, aware->Utf8). CONFORMANCE NOW 1595/1595 (100% green) — this was the last failing packet. Bare to_datetime unchanged; uniform columns still coerce.

title: Resample::aggregate_scalar loses source axis name (sum/mean/min/max/...)

title: [parity-bug] ewm().mean() uses adjust=False but pandas defaults to adjust=True
description: FP Ewm::mean() uses the recursive y_t=alpha*x+(1-alpha)*y_{t-1} (adjust=False), but pandas ewm default is adjust=True: y_t = sum_i (1-a)^i x_{t-i} / sum_i (1-a)^i. Verified vs pandas 2.2.3: [1,2,3,4].ewm(alpha=0.5).mean() = [1,1.6667,2.4286,3.2667] (pandas default) NOT [1,1.5,2.25,3.125] (FP/adjust=False). Every ewm.mean() diverges. The existing tests ewm_mean_basic/ewm_mean_with_alpha ENCODE the adjust=False bug. Fix: implement pandas' exact ewma algorithm (old_wt tracking, ignore_na=False decay-on-NaN, constant-series != guard). FOLLOW-UP (separate): ewm var/std/sum/cov/corr likely share the adjust=False error; and FP ewm() lacks com/halflife/min_periods/adjust=/ignore_na= params.
close_reason: ewm().mean() now uses pandas DEFAULT adjust=True (normalized weighted average via old_wt recurrence, ignore_na=False decay-on-NaN, constant-series guard). Fixed eager Ewm::mean + OnlineEwm streaming (kept matching); DataFrame/groupby ewm route through it. Verified vs 7-case live pandas 2.2.3 oracle battery (alphas, leading/interior NaN, constant). Updated tests + regenerated 2 goldens to pandas-correct values. 39 ewm tests green, clippy clean. FOLLOW-UP: ewm var/std/sum/cov/corr likely share adjust=False error; ewm() lacks com/halflife/min_periods/adjust=/ignore_na= params

title: SeriesGroupBy::apply_scalar uses source-axis name instead of by-name on group-keyed result
description: fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14607 routes through series_from_groupby_apply_parts which applies self.series.index().name() (source axis name). But apply_scalar's result is group-keyed (one row per group), so the index name should be self.by.name() like agg_scalar (p6y8q). Fix: bypass the apply_parts helper and build the Series directly with by-name.
close_reason: Built result Index directly with by-name in apply_scalar at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14607.

title: Series::value_counts result loses source name
description: fp-frame/src/lib.rs:4408 builds result Series with name='count' via Self::from_values. Pandas Series.value_counts returns a Series whose name=='count' (good) but whose index.name == the source Series' name. We currently lose self.name -> result.index.name propagation. Fix: build with Index::new(labels).rename_index(Some(self.name())) — when self.name is non-empty.
close_reason: Built result index with rename_index(Some(self.name())) when non-empty at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:4423; result.index.name now matches pandas (source Series name).

title: [perf] fp-frame::DataFrame::sort_values_na:28484 — typed-column sort fast-path; sort_single 12.43ms vs pandas 3.19ms (3.9x)
description:
MEASUREMENT BY RubyGoose profiling pass 2026-06-01. See tests/artifacts/perf/HOTSPOT_TABLE.md.

SCENARIO: DataFrame::sort_values("c0", true), 100k rows x 4 float cols.

BASELINE (100k, 20 hyperfine runs, release-perf + frame pointers):
- FP sort_single: 12.43 ms/iter (sigma 0.56); alloc-heavy (User 432ms / Sys 435ms)
- pandas 2.2.3: p50 3.19ms, p95 5.03ms => 3.9x slower.

PROFILER EVIDENCE (samply, symbolicated; samply_sort_single.json):
- ~50% self  fp_frame::DataFrame::sort_values_na  (0x3f08d/0x3f070/0x3f081)
- 8.4% self  libc memcpy / element move (unsymbolized 0x12ac5b)
- 3.4% self  fp_index::Index::take
- 2.4% self  fp_columnar::Column::new (materialization, see br-frankenpandas-2a6ln)

ROOT CAUSE: sort_values_na does order.sort_by(|l,r| compare_scalars_with_na_position(&col.values()[l], &col.values()[r], ..)) — a comparison sort over boxed Scalar enums with per-comparison enum dispatch + bounds-checked Vec<Scalar> indexing (~1.7M comparisons for 100k). pandas sorts a contiguous typed f64 buffer (cache-friendly, branch-light) in 3.19ms.

HYPOTHESIZED FIX (H3): add a typed-column fast path — when the sort column is a dense Int64/Float64 (no nulls or nulls bucketed), extract the native slice and sort indices with a native comparator (or pdqsort/voracious radix on the typed buffer), falling back to the generic Scalar comparator only for mixed/object/null-heavy columns. Materialization reuse from br-frankenpandas-2a6ln compounds the win.

DO NOT optimize yet without re-baselining against HOTSPOT_TABLE.md.
close_reason: Completed typed dense sort fast path. Re-baseline sort_single 100000x4 via rch hyperfine: 802.4 ms mean / 800.2 ms median per 70 iters (11.46 ms/iter). After: 763.9 ms mean / 757.6 ms median per 70 iters (10.91 ms/iter). Score Impact 1 x Confidence 5 / Effort 2 = 2.5. Golden output unchanged sha256 0d34cbf3261960c869ee85a7a91641c038e9e2f7c253ca2dec701075da028493. Validation: rch cargo test -p fp-frame --lib dataframe_sort_values_dense_float_fast_path_keeps_stable_ties; rch cargo test -p fp-frame --lib dataframe_sort_values_na_position_first; rch cargo test -p fp-frame --lib dataframe_sort_values_numeric_keeps_na_last_and_stable_ties; rch cargo fmt -p fp-frame -- --check; rch cargo clippy -p fp-frame --all-targets -- -D warnings; rch cargo build -p fp-conformance --profile release-perf --example perf_profile; cmp golden before/after.

title: [perf][no-gaps] Close ALL vs-upstream perf gaps in pure safe Rust — no C BLAS/LAPACK/XLA linkage
description:
TARGET: replace Vec<Scalar> AoS with contiguous typed columnar arrays so filter_bool/gather/sort_single/groupby/join reach numpy-class speed (currently ~0.3x weighted vs pandas).

NO vs-upstream perf gap is acceptable or 'architectural'. We build our OWN 100% memory-safe-Rust BLAS/LAPACK-class kernels (cache-blocked GEMM + packed panels + register micro-kernels, blocked LU/QR/Cholesky/SVD, portable SIMD elementwise/reductions, SIMD string/memory scans). NEVER link C BLAS/LAPACK/MKL/XLA. #![forbid(unsafe_code)] stays (safe SIMD + autovectorization only). Behavior parity absolute: identical output/ordering/FP-bits/errors or revert. Method: profile -> /alien-graveyard (canonical docs /data/projects/alien_cs_graveyard/*.md) + /alien-artifact-coding to harvest the technique -> /extreme-software-optimization to ship (one lever/commit, isomorphism proof + golden sha256, Score>=2.0, re-bench via rch crate-scoped). Push main + main:master.

See /data/projects/.scratch/no_gaps_directive.txt for the full orchestrator directive (broadcast to all 27 agents 2026-06-02).

title: [perf] Float64 value_counts bit-tally fast path (4x vs-pandas gap)
description: fp-bench vs-pandas (now functional) measured Series.value_counts on a 100k all-unique float64 column at 21.69ms p50 vs pandas 5.37ms = 4.04x SLOWER (the biggest dataframe_ops gap). Root cause: value_counts had int64-direct and utf8-contiguous fast paths but NO float64 path, so float columns hit the generic HashMap<ScalarKey> tally — materializing a Scalar::Float64 per row, wrapping ScalarKey, hashing through std SipHash. Lever (bit-identical): all-valid Float64 bit-tally via FxHashMap<u64> keyed on canonicalized bits (v==0.0 -> 0 else v.to_bits(), matching scalar_key_allow_missing's normalized.to_bits), presized to len. Result: 21.69 -> 12.5ms = 1.74x (min ~12.3ms). 32 value_counts tests pass; bit-identical by construction (same key, first-seen order, value, stable sort). Residual vs pandas (12.5 vs 5.4ms) is the 100k Utf8 index-label construction (format!("{v:?}")) which is golden-locked; closing it needs a Float64 IndexLabel output (parity-gated, pandas returns a float index).
close_reason: KEPT 1.74x. Added Float64 bit-tally fast path to Series::value_counts (the only scalar type lacking one). fp-bench vs-pandas gap: value_counts 100k float64 21.69->12.5ms (was 4.04x slower than pandas 5.37, now 2.3x). FxHashMap<u64> keyed on canonicalized bits, presized; bit-identical (32 value_counts tests pass). Residual is Utf8 label construction (golden-locked); next = Float64 IndexLabel output (parity-gated). title: [perf] ordered identity join uniqueness proof without hash table
description: Profile-backed post-9pyou target. All positions valid (loc fails-closed). 91 loc tests pass; bit-identical. fp-bench loc_labels 100k 38.3->~8ms=~3.9x; was 19.3x slower than pandas (1.99ms), now ~4x. Helps reindex + any RangeIndex op.

title: [perf] Direct df_dot output assembly without row-band intermediate
description: Profile-backed residual after kept df_dot passes uza04.98 and uza04.100. Evidence: tests/artifacts/perf/proof_uza0498_comm_avoiding_dot_gemm.md reduced df_dot 100000 from 572.056ms to 224.882ms by removing A transpose; tests/artifacts/perf/proof_uza04100_dot_parallel_assembly.md then showed phase timers extract ~0.4ms, compute ~55ms, assemble ~95ms, result_cols ~110ms and kept 231.576ms -> 134.998ms. Residual: df_dot allocates/frees a ~205MB row-band intermediate plus ~205MB output each iteration. Alien primitive: cache/locality-preserving direct materialization of GEMM output tiles, preserving each C[i][j] l=0..k ascending f64 fold and output column order while eliminating the intermediate buffer. Acceptance: RCH baseline hyperfine and golden sha256 first, one lever only, crate-scoped validation, keep only Score>=2.0.

Candidate RCH check passed on vmi1153651; remote smoke on vmi1227854 was 516.198ms/iter and not comparable to local fallback baseline. Source reverted; evidence retained in tests/artifacts/perf/proof_uza04116_direct_dot_output_reject.md. Next primitive: worker-private Float64 output chunk adoption or reusable output arena, not shared-column row-slice writes.

title: [perf] Float64 value_counts typed tally path after numeric-index parity

Preserve pandas ordering/tie-breaking, null dropping, f64 bit identity (including -0.0/NaN payload behavior), golden sha256, and Score>=2.0 before keeping.

Direct fp-bench value_counts 100k float64 improved mean 10.802ms -> 9.672ms (1.12x) and p50 10.363ms -> 9.433ms (1.10x), but hyperfine full-process regressed/noised 300.1ms +/- 9.0 -> 305.1ms +/- 12.7 (0.98x). perf and samply were blocked by perf_event_paranoid=4. Next route: deeper typed-output Float64 value_counts materialization primitive, not this sort guard.

Implement one lever only: carry u64 canonical bits + first-seen f64 + count through the Float64 default materializer and construct IndexLabel::Float64 / Scalar::Int64 directly, preserving canonical -0/+0 handling, first-seen tie order, stable descending count order, null/NaN drop behavior, f64 payload bits, result/index names, and all non-Float64 fallbacks. Evidence required: rch crate-scoped baseline, golden sha256 before/after, isomorphism proof, rch rebench, Score>=2.0 or reject and remove source hunk.
notes: Post-close validation refresh: after removing unrelated cached-witness draft hunks, rch exec -- cargo test -p fp-frame series_value_counts -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo check -p fp-frame --all-targets, and rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fp-frame --all-targets -- -D warnings passed on vmi1227854; proof artifact updated. cargo fmt -p fp-frame -- --check remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing formatting drift.
close_reason: Kept typed Float64 value_counts output materializer: direct fp-bench mean 12.906ms -> 10.140ms (1.27x), hyperfine 297.7ms -> 254.4ms (1.17x), golden SHA unchanged 5c55febe9b1f6dd9a7f828800fae9e3389b056f30ae3bd116468806f32faf512, focused tests and cargo check passed. Clippy/fmt blockers are separate pre-existing hygiene drift.

title: [perf] Reuse contiguous UTF-8 witness for repeated duplicated masks
description: Post-x8mdu shifted profile artifact tests/artifacts/perf/x8mdu_after_cardinality_x50.json shows str_duplicated 500000x50 is now the hottest string-cardinality residual: factorize 87.4ms +/-3.8, unique 296.9ms +/-6.8, value_counts 313.7ms +/-16.1, duplicated 376.6ms +/-7.4. Profile-backed next primitive: extend or reuse the cached all-valid contiguous UTF-8 witness to generate duplicated masks without rehashing spans per repeated call. One lever only, preserve pandas duplicated keep semantics (first/last/false), missing fallback, ordering/tie behavior, f64/RNG N/A. Baseline with RCH first, golden sha256/cmp, paired/reversed hyperfine, Score>=2.0, crate-scoped gates.
close_reason: NOT a vs-pandas gap (measured). FP string ops are at/beyond pandas 2.2.3 (500k x1000-card, amortized): factorize 1.6 vs 28.6ms, unique 6.0 vs 24.7, duplicated 3.1 vs 18.7 (6x faster), value_counts 6.3 vs 36.6 — via the OnceLock-cached factorize witness. The only FP-slower case is a SINGLE COLD call (~1.7x, witness build vs pandas khash), which the cache amortizes away in any repeated use. The bead's lever (reuse witness in duplicated) was profiled + REJECTED: 2.4x on the looped profile but 9x cold-single-call REGRESSION (building the witness for a one-shot dup mask costs more than the existing 7.78ms span-hash). duplicated single-call (7.78ms) is already 2.4x faster than pandas. No work warranted.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: REJECTED build-and-cache approach (BlackThrush): duplicated() Utf8 via factorize witness + dense code dedup = str_duplicated 500000 LOOPED x50 7.78->3.25ms/iter (2.4x) BUT cold single call (iters=1) 7.78->70ms = 9x REGRESSION. Witness build (build_utf8_factorize_default_witness) ~67ms/500k rows; break-even ~12 repeated calls. Real duplicated is single-call => profiler-loop-gaming that regresses the common path; reject. duplicated single-call 7.78ms is already cheaper than building the witness. RE-SCOPE: real residual = the ~67ms cold witness BUILD (factorize/unique/value_counts all pay it; ~9x a bare span-hash) — optimize build_utf8_factorize_default_witness in fp-columnar. fp-frame/lib.rs under active peer value_counts edit (clobbered my WIP mid-bench).

title: [perf] Spearman typed Float64 rank extraction avoids Scalar materialization
description: Profile-backed target after br-frankenpandas-0ghpb compact reprofile: tests/artifacts/perf/fp_reprofile_after_0ghpb_compact_cod_pandas.json measured df_spearman 5000 5 at 136.0 ms +/- 3.7. CSV remains hotter, but CSV parser micro-levers have a rejection streak, so pivot to the prior Spearman rejection's structural next primitive: zero-Scalar typed Float64 rank extraction/materialization avoidance. Current Series::spearman_ranks_if_complete forces Column::values() scalar materialization before ranking. One lever: borrow complete all-valid Float64 rank input via Column::as_f64_slice(), preserving row/column order, EPS tie grouping, NaN fallback, floating-point arithmetic, and RNG/hash behavior. Acceptance: RCH-built baseline and after binaries, golden sha256 unchanged, hyperfine A/B win with Score >= 2.0, crate-scoped fp-frame validation.
close_reason: Completed: Spearman rank precompute now borrows complete all-valid Float64 columns via Column::as_f64_slice instead of forcing Scalar materialization. RCH-built baseline /data/tmp/cargo-target-cod-pandas-uza04-12-before/release-perf/examples/perf_profile df_spearman 5000 5: 140.5 ms +/- 6.0. Paired A/B: before 136.9 ms +/- 4.8 vs after 115.3 ms +/- 5.1, 1.19x +/- 0.07 faster; reversed confirm after 108.1 ms +/- 4.8 vs before 136.2 ms +/- 4.5, 1.26x +/- 0.07 faster. Golden before/after SHA dc37f75e1ee2e23e28ed89e0f178a0268cb65cc420bc82f97cb16d219eb7dd1e byte-identical. Isomorphism: column/row order unchanged, average-rank tie grouping unchanged, NaN/missing/non-Float64 fallback unchanged, floating-point arithmetic in ranks/correlation unchanged after input extraction, RNG/hash behavior N/A. Score Impact 2.5 x Confidence 0.9 / Effort 1.0 = 2.25 retained. Validation: cargo fmt -p fp-frame --check; RCH cargo test -p fp-frame spearman_ranks_complete_float64_borrows_typed_column --lib; RCH cargo test -p fp-frame spearman --lib; RCH cargo check -p fp-frame --lib; RCH cargo clippy -p fp-frame --lib -- -D warnings; git diff --check. UBS on fp-frame timed out after 180s with no findings output.

description: Profile-backed residual after kept df_dot passes br-frankenpandas-uza04.98 and br-frankenpandas-uza04.101 plus rejected br-frankenpandas-uza04.116. Evidence: proof_uza04100_dot_parallel_assembly.md kept df_dot 100000 at 231.576ms -> 134.998ms but identified output allocation churn: ~205MB row-band intermediate plus ~205MB output each call. proof_uza04116_direct_dot_output_reject.md showed direct shared final-column row-slice writes preserved goldens but regressed, implying worker-private locality must be retained. Alien primitive: worker-private Float64 output chunks/adoption or equivalent chunked column assembly that avoids redundant copy/zero-fill while preserving each C[i][j] l=0..k ascending f64 fold, output row order, and other.column_order. Acceptance: RCH crate-scoped baseline and profile/golden before edit, one lever only, golden sha256 byte-identical for df_dot, paired/reversed hyperfine via RCH, Score>=2.0 or remove source hunk, fp-frame focused tests/check/clippy/fmt/UBS as feasible.

title: [perf] df_dot output NaN witness skips redundant Float64 scan
description: Profile-backed continuation after br-frankenpandas-uza04.120 rejected lazy shared output storage. Target: DataFrame::dot still builds contiguous Float64 output columns from row-banded GEMM buffers, then Column::from_f64_values scans every output column for NaN. One lever: carry an exact per-output-column NaN witness from the GEMM store loop and construct NaN-free columns with a hidden all-valid Float64 constructor, preserving contiguous as_f64_slice outputs. Semantics: every C[i][j] remains the same l=0..k ascending f64 fold; if any stored result is NaN, use existing from_f64_values path for that column; otherwise all-valid validity is exactly equivalent. Required evidence: baseline before edit, df_dot golden sha256/cmp, paired/reversed hyperfine, Score>=2.0 or remove source, crate-scoped check/clippy/test/fmt/UBS as feasible.
close_reason: Rejected output NaN-witness lever: goldens matched, but longer paired df_dot 100000x20 showed only ~1.02x with high variance, below Score>=2.0; no source retained. Next route needs larger column-panel/typed chunk primitive.

title: [perf] Canonicalize Int64 unit-range Index::new inputs
description: Profile-backed target: series_add_align stack samples show alignment semantic witness creation spending time serializing/hashing Int64 indexes and detecting duplicates. Existing lazy Index::new_known_unique_int64_unit_range already has fast semantic fingerprints, but benchmark/user-created shifted range indexes enter through Index::new(Vec<IndexLabel>) and stay materialized. One lever: detect obvious Int64(start..start+len) inputs in Index::new/IndexLabels::new and canonicalize to the existing lazy unit-range backing, seeding duplicate/sort caches. Preserve materialized labels, order, name behavior, equality, hash/fingerprint semantics, alignment output, and all non-unit/mixed/duplicate fallbacks. Baseline series_add_align first, golden sha256/cmp, paired/reversed hyperfine, Score>=2.0 or remove source.
close_reason: Rejected: exact goldens matched but Index::new unit-range canonicalization regressed series_add_align 100000x100 from ~69 ms to 1.13-1.23 s; source removed, evidence in proof_lavender_index_new_unit_range.md

title: [perf] Cache sorted-unique union semantic output identity
description: Profile-backed continuation from series_add_align stack samples and br-frankenpandas-uza04.122 rejection. record_alignment_semantic_witness already proves left/right are duplicate-free and sorted before using semantic_sorted_unique_union_output_fingerprint, but the cached fingerprint path still rebuilds output SemanticIndexIdentity via semantic_index_identity_from_fingerprint, which calls output_index.has_duplicates() on the freshly-built union index every iteration. One lever: under the sorted-unique union preconditions, return/cache the full output SemanticIndexIdentity with has_duplicates=false, preserving output len/fingerprint/role exactly and leaving alignment/column arithmetic paths unchanged. Baseline series_add_align first, golden sha256/cmp, paired/reversed hyperfine, Score>=2.0 or remove source.
close_reason: Rejected: exact goldens matched but sorted-unique union output identity cache was neutral/slower (baseline ~69.6-72.3 ms vs candidate ~71.3-73.8 ms); source removed, evidence in proof_lavender_sorted_union_identity.md

title: [perf] df_dot worker-private Float64 chunked output after shared-tape reject

One lever: add private all-valid Float64 chunk backing and let DataFrame::dot output columns adopt Arc-backed worker-private row-band chunks when exact per-band NaN witnesses prove the column all-valid; NaN-bearing columns fall back to the old concat plus Column::from_f64_values path. Preserve l=0..k accumulation order, output row/column order, Float64 bits, NaN/null fallback semantics, and RNG-free behavior.
close_reason: Kept worker-private Float64 chunked df_dot output: paired forward 959.5ms -> 749.7ms (1.28x), paired reversed 964.8ms -> 740.8ms (1.30x), golden SHAs unchanged for df_dot 2000 and 5000; Score 5.33.

title: [perf] df_dot BI=8 row micro-tile rejection

The lever preserves each C[i][j] l=0..k ascending f64 fold, output order, Float64 bits, NaN/null fallback, and RNG-free behavior. close_reason: Rejected BI=8 row micro-tile: goldens matched but paired df_dot 100000x6 hyperfine favored baseline BI=4 by 1.07x-1.10x; source hunk removed; see proof_lavender_df_dot_bi8.md.

title: [perf] df_dot full-tile 4x4 microkernel unroll
description: Profile-backed continuation on current main after br-frankenpandas-uza04.124 and .125: fresh routing matrix shows df_dot 100000x6 at 123.108 ms/iter, still far above the next checked lane str_sort_chain at 14.157 ms/iter. One lever: keep BI=4,BJ=4 but manually unroll the dominant full-tile 4x4 update in DataFrame::dot, replacing iterator/enumerate nested loops with explicit accumulator updates. For every C[i][j], l still runs 0..k in ascending order with the same f64 multiply/add; edge tiles and output assembly remain unchanged. Acceptance: baseline/golden before edit, paired/reversed hyperfine, Score>=2.0 or remove source.
close_reason: Rejected 4x4 full-tile unroll: goldens matched but paired timing was not stable/large enough (forward 786.0ms -> 768.0ms, reversed baseline 779.3ms vs unroll 827.2ms); source hunk removed.

title: [perf] df_dot row-band worker cap 32
description: Profile-backed continuation after current-main df_dot remains the dominant checked lane and tile-width / full-tile unroll variants failed. Local parallelism is 64, and DataFrame::dot currently allows up to 64 row-band compute workers plus assembly workers. One lever: cap dot worker fanout at 32 to reduce row-band/thread scheduling overhead while preserving the same row-band ownership, output order, and per-cell l=0..k f64 accumulation order. Acceptance: current-main baseline/goldens before edit, paired/reversed hyperfine, Score>=2.0 or remove source.
close_reason: Rejected row-band worker cap 32: goldens matched but wall time regressed; baseline 64-worker path stayed 1.08x-1.10x faster in paired hyperfine. Source hunk removed.

title: [perf] df_dot finite-bound NaN witness elision
description: Profile-backed continuation: current df_dot 100000x6 remains dominant after tile-width, unroll, and worker-cap rejects. One semantic-witness lever: before GEMM, compute a safe finite/bounded input proof (all operands finite and k*max_abs_a*max_abs_b cannot overflow f64). If the proof holds, every output cell is finite or at least non-NaN, so DataFrame::dot can skip per-output value.is_nan witness updates and build all-valid output chunks directly. If the proof does not hold, fallback is the existing exact output NaN witness path. Preserve per-cell l=0..k f64 fold, output order, NaN/null semantics, and RNG-free behavior. Acceptance: baseline/golden before edit, paired/reversed hyperfine, Score>=2.0 or remove source.
close_reason: Rejected finite/bounded NaN witness elision: goldens matched but input-bound scan regressed wall time; baseline stayed 1.13x-1.14x faster. Source hunk removed.

One lever only: try a structurally different safe-Rust GEMM primitive centered on right-side k-panel packing/reuse or equivalent panel locality that preserves every output cell's l=0..k f64 accumulation order, output row/column ordering, NaN/null semantics, and RNG-free behavior. Baseline via rch + hyperfine first, golden sha256 before edit, paired forward/reversed rebench, Score>=2.0 or remove source hunk and close as rejected. Do not repeat BI/BJ widening, 4x4 unroll, worker-cap, or finite-input scan micro-levers.
close_reason: Rejected row-panel packed-B reuse: goldens matched but clean serial hyperfine showed baseline 1.87x-1.92x faster; source hunk removed. Next route: reprofile and avoid row-panel/BI-BJ/unroll/worker-cap/finite-scan families.

title: [perf] regular-stride typed take_positions gather

Next attack should move beyond regular-gather microstructure toward a different primitive, e.g. frame-level row block materialization or streaming Spearman rank-correlation accumulation.

Baseline/profile artifacts for br-frankenpandas-uza04.38 show dense inner_join output lifecycle/memmove/Column materialization remains the residual: inner_join 100000x3 baseline 126.060 ms mean, golden sha256 be7d17114e5a2a88607bdc65228751789e208ebbd9ac2f5d0c616cdf64e641f1; join_1to1 baseline 34.635 ms mean, golden sha256 18988b22befdb71941112638f6f1c3415cbf0f79fc1f00ecd013318d924682c7. Coalesced contiguous right-tape copies preserved goldens but regressed to 156.889 ms and 40.681 ms, Score 0.0, so do not retry copy-loop microvariants.

Deep primitive: add a private lazy right bucket-tape Int64 Column representation in fp-columnar and wire dense i64 inner-join right lanes to emit repeated bucket tape segments instead of eagerly materializing O(output_len) right-side Vec<i64> when fanout is high. Conceptually this is a segment/range tape: owned right-value tape plus ordered (start,len) segments in left probe order, with as_i64_slice/values/take_positions materializing on demand exactly like the current eager expansion. It is the right-lane counterpart to br-frankenpandas-3ad4n lazy repeat-run left lanes, but preserves right bucket insertion order and duplicate tie-breaking.

One lever only. Required proof: RCH crate-scoped baseline first; golden sha256 before/after byte-identical for inner_join and join_1to1; downstream forced-read gate with inner_join_read to prove no regression when consumers materialize; preserve left probe order, right bucket insertion order, duplicate tie-breaking, output column order/names/dtypes, null/mixed/sorted/indicator/validate fallbacks, FP bits, RNG/tie/hash behavior; paired RCH hyperfine same host; keep only Score >= 2.0. If rejected, route to a fundamentally different output-lifecycle primitive, not another bucket copy loop.
close_reason: Completed: current main includes the lazy repeated-slice Int64 right-lane join lever from 78c93250. Same-worker proof kept goldens byte-identical and improved inner_join 100000x3 from 129.2 ms to 45.5 ms in the committed ledger; independent OrangePeak artifacts confirmed 136.204 ms to 47.654 ms, join_1to1 control 42.704 ms to 36.185 ms, and inner_join_read forced-materialization gate 416.320 ms to 381.545 ms. Score 10.0. Post-change profile routes next to residual build_dense_i64_inner_output_data / Column::new lifecycle.

This must not repeat rejected frame-side unchecked routing from br-frankenpandas-uza04.4 or fused-mask/eager-stride families; it is a shared descriptor primitive, not a bounds-check or copy-loop micro-tweak.

Required loop: RCH crate-scoped baseline and profile before edits; golden sha256 for filter_bool before/after; preserve row order, duplicate position behavior, DataFrame index/column order/names, dtype/validity/null/NaN behavior, f64 payload bits including -0.0/inf/NaN, tie-breaking N/A, RNG unchanged; focused unit proof for descriptor materialization; crate-scoped fmt/check/clippy/tests/UBS on touched files; paired RCH rebench; keep only Score>=2.0, then reprofile and route the shifted bottleneck.

title: [perf] CSV numeric chunks: persistent scoped worker-pool morsels
description:
Profile-backed target: current RCH-built perf_profile CSV residual is about 494 ms for csv_read 100000x20 and 512 ms for csv_read_no_na_filter 100000x20. GDB/perf attach is blocked on this host, but strace -f -c on the RCH-built before binary shows the simple numeric parallel path creates 80 worker threads for 20 reads (4 clone3 per read), with futex+clone3+mmap/mprotect/sigaltstack/rseq/thread setup a large syscall-profile share; taskset single-worker check is slower (csv_read 638 ms), so keep parallel morsels but remove per-read thread creation.

Alien primitive: morsel-driven parallel execution / persistent safe-Rust worker pool from alien_cs_graveyard §8.2 and thread-per-core batching ideas, implemented in safe Rust. One lever only: replace std::thread::scope chunk spawning in parse_simple_numeric_csv_parallel_chunks with a persistent scoped worker pool (Rayon scoped tasks if the measured dependency cost stays favorable), preserving borrowed input lifetimes and chunk-order merge. Fallback remains unchanged.

Proof obligations: fresh RCH-built before/after hyperfine for csv_read, csv_read_options, csv_read_no_na_filter; strace before/after should drop clone3 count on hot loop; golden sha256 unchanged; row ordering, column ordering, dtype promotion, null/NaN/default NA handling, float bits, errors, RNG/tie/hash behavior unchanged; validate focused fp-io CSV tests/check/clippy/fmt/UBS as feasible; keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected persistent scoped worker-pool/Rayon morsel lever: fresh RCH before baseline csv_read 498.8 ms +/- 7.7, csv_read_options 496.3 ms +/- 7.2, csv_read_no_na_filter 483.7 ms +/- 12.0; golden SHA before/after byte-identical 434a0ecec4b33d7d0ff1b232053fca83d4ca07b685f0351d13a083fdbfbf9333. Strace before showed 80 clone3 for 20 reads; Rayon after reduced per-read spawning but initialized a 64-thread pool and showed heavy futex/sched_yield traffic under strace. Paired hyperfine was not a real win: csv_read apparent noisy 688.4 -> 506.4 ms but focused confirmation only 498.1 -> 486.0 ms (~1.02x); csv_read_options paired 496.5 -> 503.0 ms, focused noisy 534.7 -> 489.3 ms; no_na_filter paired 510.3 -> 510.5 ms, focused 489.8 -> 489.0 ms flat. Score < 2.0. Source/dependency hunk removed; Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock/fp-io diff empty. Next CSV attack must be a different primitive, e.g. exact decimal parser or column finalization/allocation model, not thread-pool swap.

Static one-dimensional rank/select cannot answer exact Kendall arbitrary prefix row-set queries without repeating dynamic Fenwick/bitset state or building pair-specific 2D dominance indexes. Do not retry static rank/select over only row order or only rank order, row-major rank-signature batching, dynamic multi-Fenwick/bitset batching, morsel scheduling, merge-sort, sqrt/block counters, per-pair validation removal, or cache-layout micro-tweaks. Next viable primitive: offline divide-and-conquer dominance summaries that share recursion/rank summaries across target columns in one left-column pass, preserving exact discordance counts and falling back to current per-pair counter if exactness cannot be proved. Required loop: fresh RCH baseline/profile, df_kendall 2000/5000/20000 golden SHA, one lever, paired/reversed hyperfine 50000x1 and 200000x1, exact isomorphism for order/diagonal/symmetry/tie+NaN+fallback/f64 bits/RNG, Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected chunked single-pair dominance counter. Goldens unchanged for df_kendall 2000/5000/20000 (acf366c266b66f8497fb55734ed1b4ec40952a7c014f43db262c7c1e625e15e1, 031978ba431260b942dd36d9be055064a7453118a7c00888c35747644d33d99e, f164fa86300fbea93e46aa99a5e0f7413fa8c0baf2ee49c6a689ed92652a4c3b), but paired hyperfine rejected it: baseline was 1.17x faster on 50000x1 in both orders, 1.14x faster on 200000x1 forward, and 1.06x faster on 200000x1 reversed. Runtime source hunk removed; proof: tests/artifacts/perf/proof_uza0492_chunked_dominance_reject.md. Next route: true cross-column offline dominance sharing for a fixed left order, not chunked single-pair counters or previously rejected rank-select/row-signature/multi-Fenwick families.

title: [perf] Fixed-left CDQ target-panel Kendall dominance row builder
description:
Follow-up after br-frankenpandas-uza04.92 rejected the chunked single-pair Kendall dominance counter. Current upstream .92 proof: df_kendall 2000/5000/20000 goldens unchanged (acf366c266b66f8497fb55734ed1b4ec40952a7c014f43db262c7c1e625e15e1, 031978ba431260b942dd36d9be055064a7453118a7c00888c35747644d33d99e, f164fa86300fbea93e46aa99a5e0f7413fa8c0baf2ee49c6a689ed92652a4c3b), but paired hyperfine rejected the candidate: baseline was faster on every paired/reversed gate. Runtime source hunk was removed. Do not retry per-pair counter rewrites, chunked dominance counters, row-major rank-signature batching, dynamic multi-Fenwick/bitset batching, morsel scheduling, merge-sort, sqrt/block counters, validation removal, cache-layout tweaks, or static 1D rank/select.

Next primitive: fixed-left CDQ target-panel Kendall dominance row builder. Build an exact safe-Rust helper around complete_kendall_no_tie_parallel_matrix that computes one whole Kendall matrix row for a fixed left column at a time. For left column i, invert order_refs[i] once into left_pos_by_row, then process all right columns j > i as target-rank streams. Use a shared CDQ recursion over left-position intervals; at each split, scan each target stream in target-rank order and add right_seen_before_left into discordant[j]. Partition streams by left half/right half and recurse. Every unordered row pair should be counted exactly once at its split ancestor.

Likely edit surface: crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs near complete_kendall_no_tie_parallel_matrix, plus private helpers beside it and tests near the Kendall tests. Leave Series::count_ordered_rank_inversions as the fallback/proof oracle.

Proof obligations: exactness proof that each unordered row pair belongs to exactly one CDQ split and is counted iff target rank reverses left rank; bit parity against Series::kendall_no_tie_fast_with_ordered_ranks for deterministic random matrices and adversarial permutations; existing df_kendall 2000/5000/20000 golden SHAs unchanged; preserve complete-finite/no-tie gating, diagonal 1.0, symmetry copy, NaN/tie fallback, final f64 formula, and RNG-free behavior.

Performance gates: fresh RCH crate-scoped baseline/profile first, one lever, paired/reversed hyperfine on df_kendall 50000x1 and 200000x1, keep only Score >= 2.0. This is matrix-row work sharing, not another drop-in inversion counter.
close_reason: Rejected fixed-left CDQ target-panel Kendall row builder. Goldens unchanged for df_kendall 2000/5000/20000 (acf366c266b66f8497fb55734ed1b4ec40952a7c014f43db262c7c1e625e15e1, 031978ba431260b942dd36d9be055064a7453118a7c00888c35747644d33d99e, f164fa86300fbea93e46aa99a5e0f7413fa8c0baf2ee49c6a689ed92652a4c3b), and focused CDQ/complete-matrix parity tests passed on RCH, but paired hyperfine rejected it: baseline was 2.63x faster on 50000x1 forward, 2.59x faster on 50000x1 reversed, 2.87x faster on 200000x1 forward, and 2.83x faster on 200000x1 reversed. Runtime source hunk removed; proof: tests/artifacts/perf/proof_uza0493_cdq_target_panel_reject.md. Next route: cache-oblivious/succinct bitplane exact Kendall witness, not CDQ target-panel repartition copies or prior rejected families.

title: [perf] Cache-oblivious bitplane Kendall dominance witness after CDQ reject
description:
Follow-up after br-frankenpandas-uza04.93 rejected the fixed-left CDQ target-panel row builder. Current proof: df_kendall 2000/5000/20000 goldens unchanged (acf366c266b66f8497fb55734ed1b4ec40952a7c014f43db262c7c1e625e15e1, 031978ba431260b942dd36d9be055064a7453118a7c00888c35747644d33d99e, f164fa86300fbea93e46aa99a5e0f7413fa8c0baf2ee49c6a689ed92652a4c3b), focused CDQ and complete-matrix parity tests passed on RCH, but paired hyperfine rejected the candidate: baseline was 2.63x faster on 50000x1 forward, 2.59x faster on 50000x1 reversed, 2.87x faster on 200000x1 forward, and 2.83x faster on 200000x1 reversed. Runtime hunk was removed. Do not retry per-pair counter rewrites, chunked dominance counters, fixed-left CDQ target-panel repartitioning, static one-dimensional rank/select, row-major rank-signature batching, dynamic multi-Fenwick/bitset batching, morsel scheduling, merge-sort, sqrt/block counters, validation removal, or cache-layout micro-tweaks.

Next primitive: cache-oblivious / succinct bitplane exact Kendall witness. Mine alien graveyard sections 7.1 succinct rank/select and 7.2 cache-oblivious layouts, then build a row-order rank-bitplane witness that avoids copying every target stream at every recursion level. The candidate should compute exact discordance counts from compact bitplanes or wavelet-matrix-style partitions with contiguous scans and bounded scratch, while preserving the current complete-finite/no-tie gates and the existing per-pair ordered-rank function as oracle/fallback.

Proof obligations: exactness proof that each unordered row pair contributes once and only once to each affected column-pair discordance count; bit parity against Series::kendall_no_tie_fast_with_ordered_ranks on deterministic random/adversarial permutations; existing df_kendall 2000/5000/20000 golden SHAs unchanged; preserve diagonal 1.0, symmetry copy, NaN/tie fallback, final f64 formula, ordering, and RNG-free behavior.

Performance gates: fresh RCH crate-scoped baseline/profile first, one lever, paired/reversed hyperfine on df_kendall 50000x1 and 200000x1, keep only Score >= 2.0. This is an exact compact-witness replacement, not a CDQ copy/repartition variant.
close_reason: Rejected: exact bitplane prefix Kendall witness preserved goldens but regressed df_kendall. Baseline faster by 2.18x/2.10x at 50000x1 and 3.73x/3.72x at 200000x1; source hunk removed; proof tests/artifacts/perf/proof_uza0494_bitplane_prefix_reject.md.

Need an advanced-math design proof before implementation: avoid explicit O(n^2) row-pair enumeration, update many column-pair discordance counters per packed row/partition operation, and preserve exact integer discordance counts. Mine alien-graveyard cache-oblivious divide-and-conquer, bitset/SWAR, wavelet/tree accumulators, and communication-avoiding all-pairs kernels.

Non-repeat boundaries: do not retry bitplane-prefix scans, CDQ repartition copies, per-pair counter rewrites, chunked dominance counters, fixed-left target-panel row builders, static one-dimensional rank/select, row-major rank-signature batching, dynamic multi-Fenwick/bitset batching, morsel scheduling, merge-sort, sqrt/block counters, validation removal, or cache-layout micro-tuning.

Proof obligations: exactness proof that every unordered row pair contributes once and only once to each affected column-pair discordance count; bit parity against Series::kendall_no_tie_fast_with_ordered_ranks on deterministic random/adversarial permutations; df_kendall 2000/5000/20000 golden SHAs unchanged; preserve diagonal 1.0, symmetry copy, NaN/tie fallback, final f64 formula, ordering, and RNG-free behavior.

Performance gates: fresh crate-scoped RCH baseline/profile first, one lever, paired/reversed hyperfine on df_kendall 50000x1 and 200000x1, keep only Score >= 2.0.
close_reason: Rejected before source edit: exact row-pair sign-tensor Kendall aggregation either enumerates O(n^2) row-pair signatures or collapses back into rejected rank-select/bitplane/CDQ/per-target dominance families. Fresh RCH baseline: 117.8 ms +/- 7.1 for 50000x1, 477.2 ms +/- 14.9 for 200000x1; goldens unchanged; proof tests/artifacts/perf/proof_uza0495_sign_tensor_design_reject.md. Route next to br-frankenpandas-fbav3 safe-Rust Gram kernel lane.

title: [perf] Fuse MSD-argsort with output-key gather in str groupby dense path (+ fix degenerate-key benchmark)
description:
Profile-backed (env-gated phase timers; dynamic profilers blocked by perf_event_paranoid=4, no sudo, no valgrind in this env). Evidence: tests/artifacts/perf/blackthrush_str_groupby_dense_phase_profile.md.

FINDING 1 (benchmark artifact): build_str_key_frame (perf_profile.rs:329) writes each key as key_{mixed:016x}_{key_id:04x}; the embedded full 64-bit mixed makes every key UNIQUE, so cardinality=4096 is defeated and ng==n==200000. All str_groupby_* benches measure the DEGENERATE all-distinct path (near_all_distinct => full MSD-argsort branch), not realistic K<<n grouping. Fix the builder to honor key_cardinality (drop/bound the mixed prefix) so the bench hits the O(n) hash-group branch (ng=K) realistic case, then re-profile for the true no-gaps target. Golden builder build_str_key_frame_repeated(n,64) already groups for real, so golden vs bench shapes diverge for the same scenario name.

FINDING 2 (degenerate-path cost, n=200000): group ~11ms (fp_columnar::utf8_msd_argsort_bytes over 200k ~26B spans) + out_idx ~11ms + agg ~3ms (count). The out_idx phase (lib.rs ~52378) gathers each group key span in SORTED order: out_key_bytes.extend_from_slice(&bytes[offsets[r]..offsets[r+1]]) with r=first_row[g] (original unsorted row) => 200k random reads scattered across the 5MB input buffer (cache-miss bound), into a ZERO-capacity out_key_bytes (realloc churn), then two Arc::from(Vec) copies.

LEVER (bit-identical): fuse the output-byte gather into the MSD argsort. utf8_msd_argsort_bytes already visits spans in sorted order in its final stable distribution pass; a variant that ALSO emits the concatenated sorted byte buffer + offsets makes out_idx ~free, collapsing ~25ms -> ~14ms. Bit-identical: same sorted key bytes/offsets/Index. Applies to sort=True near_all_distinct branch only. CAVEAT: ~1.8x ceiling is BELOW Score>=2.0 on its own AND optimizes a benchmark-artifact shape; pre-sizing out_key_bytes to total span bytes + building the Arc backing without the intermediate Vec are smaller independent bit-identical cleanups. Required loop: fresh RCH baseline, golden sha256 for str_groupby_count/sum/std at 1000+200000, paired hyperfine, Score>=2.0. fp-frame + fp-columnar; coordinate fp-columnar reservation with OrangePeak.
close_reason: Rejected all-singleton dense string groupby direct aggregation. Goldens unchanged for str_groupby_count/sum/std at 1000 and 200000 rows (cmp_status=0), but paired hyperfine was neutral/sub-bar: count 219.1ms -> 226.6ms, sum 226.2ms -> 214.6ms (~1.05x), std 265.2ms -> 267.4ms. Runtime hunk removed. Follow-up br-frankenpandas-uza04.100 filed for benchmark-shape correction and dictionary-coded string groupby primitive.

title: [perf] Contiguous-source direct Utf8 gather in Column::reindex_by_positions
description: perf-record (perf_event_paranoid unblocked) on str_outer_join 200000: Column::reindex_by_positions is 21% self (top fn), feeding all 12 null-introducing Utf8 column gathers of the outer join. The cmxjz branch calls as_all_valid_str_vec() which, for a CONTIGUOUS source, materializes a Vec<&str> over the WHOLE source (offsets.windows + per-row from_utf8 revalidation) just to scatter-index it, then grows new_bytes from zero capacity. Lever: a contiguous-source branch that copies each present span straight from bytes[offsets[idx]..offsets[idx+1]] (validity by construction, no from_utf8), pre-sizing new_bytes via a one-pass present-span-length sum. Byte-identical to cmxjz (same spans/offsets/validity/backing). Helps str_outer_join, str_left_join, reindex, take broadly. Baseline str_outer_join 36.8ms, str_left_join 13.4ms @ 200000.
close_reason: Rejected: contiguous-source Utf8 gather in reindex_by_positions is neutral. Goldens BYTE-IDENTICAL (str_outer_join/str_left_join/reindex_str/str_series_take @ 2000+200000, 8/8 sha256). Two-pass pre-size REGRESSED (+16%/+19%, extra scattered pass); one-pass avg-reserve NEUTRAL (36.0 vs 36.8ms outer, 13.1 vs 13.4 left). The Utf8 reindex gather is memcpy/scatter-bound — from_utf8+Vec<&str> are free vs the span memmoves. Score<<2.0; source reverted. Proof: tests/artifacts/perf/proof_uza0497_contiguous_reindex_gather_reject.md. Next swing: cache-oblivious/radix-partitioned Kendall per-pair inversion counter (Fenwick is cache-miss-bound), needs profiler stably unblocked.

title: [perf] Communication-avoiding row-banded df.dot GEMM (cut 64x A re-streaming)
description: df_dot 100000 (m=100k,k=256,n=256 GEMM) = 572ms = ~11.6 GMAC/s aggregate on 64 cores — 22x below the kernel's FMA-free ceiling. Root cause (structural, not kernel): the seti2 kernel partitions OUTPUT COLUMNS across workers, so every one of 64 workers re-streams the entire A (m*k=205MB) from DRAM => ~13GB A traffic, memory-bandwidth bound. Lever (communication-avoiding GEMM, bit-identical): partition OUTPUT ROWS into per-worker bands; each worker computes all n columns for its band, reading its A-band from DRAM exactly once (whole A once total, ~64x less), 4x4 A-tile L1-resident, B packed once (n*k~512KB) streamed from L2. Same 4x4 l=0..k ascending fold per cell (separate */+=, no FMA) => byte-identical. Output column-major-within-band, assembled via contiguous copies (no strided transpose).
close_reason: KEPT (Score 2.54). Communication-avoiding row-banded df.dot GEMM + eliminated the column->row A transpose. Phase timers showed extract=~300ms (scattered stride-k transpose of A) dominated over compute=~150ms; prior seti2 optimized the wrong phase. Lever: keep A column-major (borrow column &[f64], kernel reads a_cols[l][row], contiguous over the 4-row tile) + row-band workers (each reads its A-band once, B packed in L2). Bit-identical: same 4x4 l=0..k ascending fold, no FMA. Golden sha256 byte-identical df_dot 2000/5000; fp-frame dot tests 12/0; df_dot 100000 572.056 -> 224.882 ms = 2.54x (min-of-6). Proof: tests/artifacts/perf/proof_uza0498_comm_avoiding_dot_gemm.md.

title: [perf] Parallelize + lazy-index the serial rank-corr prep (df.corr spearman/kendall)
description: Phase timers on pairwise_rank_corr (df_kendall/df_spearman 100000): the parallelized pair-matrix was NOT the bottleneck. Kendall n=32: series-build 31ms + SERIAL prep 117ms (per-column O(n log n) argsort+rank) + matrix 30ms. Spearman n=64: SERIES-BUILD 65ms (rebuilds an identical 100k-label index per column) + prep 10ms + matrix 5ms. The 9 rejected kendall beads (uza04.87-95) all attacked the 30ms matrix; the 117ms serial prep + 65ms redundant index build were never touched. Two bit-identical levers: (1) build series_list row index via Index::new_known_unique_int64_unit_range (lazy unit-range) instead of materializing n x len IndexLabel Vecs; (2) fan the per-column kendall prep (values/order/rank, independent per column) across workers. Both preserve per-column results exactly. Targets ~4x on both.
close_reason: KEPT (kendall 5.28x, spearman 5.03x). Phase timers showed the parallelized pair-matrix was NOT the bottleneck: kendall prep=117ms SERIAL per-column argsort+rank (uza04.87-95 all attacked the 30ms matrix instead); spearman series-build=65ms rebuilding an identical 100k-label index per column. Two bit-identical levers: (1) Index::new_known_unique_int64_unit_range for series_list (lazy unit-range, no n*len label Vecs); (2) parallelize the independent per-column kendall (values/order/rank) prep across workers. Goldens BYTE-IDENTICAL df_spearman/kendall/corr/cov @ 2000/5000 (8/8); fp-frame corr tests 52/0. df_kendall 210.353->39.809ms (5.28x), df_spearman 110.701->22.026ms (5.03x), min-of-6. Proof: tests/artifacts/perf/proof_uza0499_rankcorr_parallel_lazy_prep.md.

Add SeriesIoExt CSV file/string/options wrappers via Series::to_frame(None) with focused roundtrip/options coverage.

title: Series::where_cond_series loses index name

title: fp-frame Series::cumprod errors on Timedelta64 (should return all-NaT to match nanprod)
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:6308-6318 Series::cumprod f64 fallback calls val.to_f64() which errors on Timedelta64. pandas td_series.cumprod() raises TypeError because Timedelta² has no dimension. Mirror fp-types nanprod (br-szq6a) and Series::prod (br-mpw1f): return Scalar::Timedelta64(NAT) at every position when input is uniformly Timedelta64.

Surgical (one-file edit; matches the existing cumsum/cummin/cummax Timedelta branches added in br-gqrmf/v7spg).
close_reason: done

title: fp-frame DataFrameGroupBy::quantile silently drops Timedelta64 via to_f64().ok()
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:36815-36841 DataFrameGroupBy::quantile filters values via to_f64().ok() — Timedelta64 values get silently dropped, returning NaN per group. pandas df.groupby(k).quantile() preserves Timedelta64 dtype (per fp-types nanquantile br-5djk7).

Surgical: replace the inline f64 implementation with a call to fp_types::nanquantile, which already handles Timedelta dtype preservation. Sister to br-x3lk4 (Resample::var) which made the same fix.
close_reason: done

title: fp-frame SeriesGroupBy::cumsum/cumprod/cummin/cummax silently coerce Timedelta64 to NaN
description:
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14434+ SeriesGroupBy::cumsum/cumprod/cummin/cummax use to_f64() which fails on Timedelta64, falling into the else branch that emits Scalar::Null(NaN) per value. pandas td_series.groupby(...).cumsum()/cummin()/cummax() returns Timedelta64; cumprod raises TypeError (mirror as NaT per fp-types nanprod br-szq6a).

Sister to:
- Series::cumsum br-gqrmf  
- Series::cummin/cummax br-v7spg
- Series::cumprod br-v36qy
- SeriesGroupBy::sum/mean br-c1bxu (column_is_timedelta detector)

Surgical (one-file edit): in each of the four funcs, add a column_is_timedelta() branch that uses transform_groups with a typed i64-ns accumulator and emits Scalar::Timedelta64. cumprod emits NaT.
close_reason: done
